Title: Workforce Insights Strategist, Career Choice
Description:
Description

Amazon Career Choice empowers employees to build skills for new careers at Amazon or elsewhere.

This work begins by understanding which career paths are most in demand and viable to support through education and training experiences that can be completed while employees continue to work in their current roles. We currently offer nearly 50 different career options and 500+ schools to over one million eligible employees in 14 countries. For us, success is an employee completing an educational program and then being placed in a new job using the skills they learned through Career Choice.

We are looking to expand our policy & strategy team to welcome someone who can dive deep into data about the future of work and bring insights forward to the big picture, answering the fundamental question of which future career paths we can build successfully for our employees. This role will have primary responsibility for collecting and curating data on labor market trends, industry hiring, and workforce evolution, making informed predictions about where the most essential skills and careers will create the most hiring opportunity.

We expect that you come to the team with a background in quantitative analytics, research, and statistics, with prior applied experience in labor, workforce, or employment strategy or a parallel field (e.g., education, talent management, public policy). Comfort with AI tools and the ability to validate output is key. You will be providing guidance and support to program managers with portfolios covering diverse geographies and industries, helping them to optimize for the best employment outcomes we can achieve together.

Key job responsibilities


Identify and manage public and vendor-contracted data sources providing insights on hiring trends, labor demand, and in-demand skills
Produce regular and ad-hoc analyses of that data for different industries, geographies, and employers
Keep peers and senior leadership informed of trends showing both emerging needs and declining demand for high-velocity decision support around program offerings
Analyze and share insights from program surveys to surface voice of customer issues for further investigation or intervention by program teams
Analyze effectiveness of different educational programs against hiring requirements to indicate where different educational offerings may be required
Prepare inputs for technical documents, white papers, and AI knowledge base/model training
Coordinate with Central Science and other research teams for access to internal data and cross-team evaluation projects


A day in the life

Every day is different. You might be working on integrating an external data source with our tech and product teams, drafting a document showing recent and projected trends in a particular industry, or helping the program teams isolate actionable insights and themes from individual opinions expressed in survey responses.

About The Team

Our mission is simple: help people build careers they're excited about. We manage a network of 600+ educational partners and work directly with employers to create hiring pathways for graduates. We measure success not by enrollment, but by whether someone completes their program and lands a new job. We're a small, collaborative team that operates with a startup mentality inside a large company — moving fast, using data to make decisions, and constantly iterating on what works. You'll join a group of people who care deeply about workforce development and believe that access to education can change lives.

Basic Qualifications


Experience analyzing large datasets and communicating findings to stakeholders and senior leaders
Experience in data analysis and extracting insights to make data-backed business decisions
Can develop ad-hoc reporting to meet specific business needs
Can communicate effectively with all levels of the organization
6+ years of program or project management experience in quantitative research or analytical sciences


Preferred Qualifications


Master's degree or above in BI, finance, engineering, statistics, computer science, mathematics or equivalent quantitative field
Knowledge of analytics & statistical tools such as SAS, PowerBI, SQL & ETL DW concepts


Amazon is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of protected veteran status, disability, or other legally protected status.

Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how-we-hire/accommodations for more information. If the country/region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner.

Title: Data Labeling Analyst / Content Moderator
Description:
Role: Data Labeling Analyst / Content Moderator

Location: WA-Seattle (onsite)




Job Responsibilities:

We are seeking a highly skilled and detail-oriented Content Moderation and Quality Assurance Specialist to join our team as a Contingent Worker (Contract). The successful candidate will be responsible for training cutting edge AI models for content moderation, for ensuring the quality and accuracy of content moderation decisions, creating golden sets, calibrating vendor sites, and managing vendor relationships.

This is an excellent opportunity for someone who is passionate about content moderation and quality assurance to make a meaningful impact on our organization.

This role may involve exposure to potentially graphic and/or objectionable content including but not limited to graphic images, videos and writings, offensive or derogatory language, and other potentially objectionable material. Ii.e. child exploitation, graphic violence, animal abuse, self-injury and other content which may be considered offensive or disturbing




Must-Have Skills




Strong understanding of content moderation policies, procedures, and guidelines.
Excellent analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills.
Ability to work independently and collaboratively as part of a team.
Strong attention to detail and ability to maintain accuracy in a fast-paced environment.
Experience working with vendors or external partners preferred.
Familiarity with data analysis and reporting tools preferred.







Nice-to-have Skills

Experience with content moderation platforms and tools.
Certification in content moderation or a related field
Knowledge of industry best practices and standards for content moderation and quality assurance.

Title: Document Reviewer - Remote
Description:
Job Description

Job Title: Document Reviewer

Job Type: Contract

Location: Remote

Job Summary: In this role, you'll apply your expertise to help train next-generation AI systems. Your work will shape how models learn, reason, and perform through high-quality, real-world input.

Key Responsibilities


Carefully review large volumes of structured and unstructured data, ensuring accuracy and completeness.
Identify and flag issues related to data quality, consistency, and potential risks involving Personal Identifiable Information (PII).
Annotate, classify, and, where necessary, redact sensitive information to maintain privacy and compliance standards.
Follow strict guidelines, taxonomies, and workflow processes for data categorization and validation.
Perform quality assurance (QA) checks to verify data integrity and validate outputs.
Maintain detailed records of reviewed documents and actions taken to ensure traceability and accountability.
Collaborate with the customer's team, providing clear and concise written and verbal communication about findings and recommendations.


Required Skills And Qualifications


Proven experience in document review, auditing, annotation, redaction, or data classification at scale.
Exceptional attention to detail with a high level of accuracy in repetitive tasks.
Familiarity with handling and protecting Personal Identifiable Information (PII).
Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to document findings and communicate effectively with team members.
Demonstrated ability to follow complex guidelines and adhere to established taxonomies.
Experience performing QA or validation work on large data sets.
Ability to balance efficiency with precision in a remote work environment.


Preferred Qualifications


Background in legal, compliance, privacy, auditing, compliance or information security domains.
Prior experience supporting data-driven AI or machine learning projects.
Comfort with collaboration tools and digital workflow management platforms.

Title: Program Analyst
Description:
About TransWest: At TransWest, we design, implement, and manage world-class transportation systems for some of the most recognizable organizations globally. With a relentless focus on reliability, safety, and exceptional client service, we are redefining the way people commute while supporting our clients' sustainability and operational goals.

As a Transportation Business Analyst at TransWest, your mission is to transform data into decisions. You'll be the storyteller behind the strategy—providing the insights that power everything from daily operations to quarterly planning. If you're driven to deliver clarity, inspire confidence, and solve complex problems through data, this is your chance to shape the future of how we move.

Compensation:


$90,000 - $110,000 / year
Annual Discretionary Bonus Program


Benefits:


Medical, Dental, Vision & Life Insurance
401k with matching
Annual pay raises
Holiday pay
Paid time off up to 80 hours per year with increasing amounts based on your years of service


Responsibilities:

Analytical Leadership


Gather, validate, and analyze data sets coming from a range of source across commute, campus, and on-demand programs
Identify trends, develop forecasts, and offer strategic recommendations to improve service and efficiency.
Monitor performance metrics and SLA's including ridership, OTP, GPS, fleet maintenance intervals, parking occupancy, and incident reports.
Collaborate with clients and client stakeholders to fulfill reporting requests and provide actionable insights.


Program Reporting & Storytelling


Develop dashboards and scorecards in Power BI for internal teams and external client use.
Prepare daily, weekly, and monthly reports to inform MBRs, QBRs, and client presentations.
Create compelling data visualizations and presentations to communicate key findings and outcomes.


Operational Collaboration


Partner with Dispatch and Operations to monitor and validate incidents and service reliability.
Deliver actionable insights to Program Managers to support client communications and decisions.
Field incoming questions and issues across chat, email, phone, and tech platforms, supporting real-time decision-making.


Optimization & Insight Delivery


Proactively identify opportunities to enhance service performance and client value.
Recommend operational changes to Program Team based on historical data, current metrics, and predictive modeling.
Support new business planning efforts by contributing data-informed logistics strategies.


Culture & Core Values


Model TransWest's culture of safety, inclusion, and data-informed decision making.
Be a strong communicator and collaborator across all levels of the organization.
Embrace our entrepreneurial, fast-paced environment while balancing rigor and creativity.
Other duties as needed by our team.


Requirements:


Associate's degree or equivalent work experience in Business, Data Analytics, Operations, Transportation, or a related field.
Minimum of 1 year of experience analyzing data, interpreting trends, and developing actionable recommendations.
Proficiency in Microsoft 365, including advanced skills in Excel and working knowledge of Word, PowerPoint, Teams, and Outlook.
Experience supporting programs, projects, or operational initiatives in a professional environment.
Experience in transportation operations, transportation demand management, transit, mobility services, logistics, or a related field.
Strong organizational skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities independently while collaborating effectively within cross-functional teams.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills with the ability to present complex information in a clear, concise manner.


Preferences:


1+ years of experience building and using PowerBI dashboarding and data visualization.
Proven ability to identify trends, develop strategies and implement solutions to drive results.
Knowledge of Transportation technology like Tripshot, and systems or ability to learn quickly.


TransWest Culture: Come join our close-knit team. We enjoy having summer BBQ's, holiday parties, games, contests and so much more at our bus yards and in the office.

Our Commitment to Diversity, Equity, Inclusion, and Belonging: At TransWest, we pride ourselves on building a diverse workforce and collaborative spaces where people of different lived experiences, backgrounds, abilities, and identities can thrive. Everyone on the team at TransWest emphasizes and practices our core values every day that are based on continued growth, reliability, safety, caring for our work, clients, and each other. Our commitment to diversity, equity, and inclusion is an evolving area where we will continue to listen and learn from our team members, business partners, community partners, and thought leaders to optimize our impact.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
About Us:

American Technology Consulting (ATC) is a service-first tech company dedicated to helping businesses unlock their full potential through innovative technology solutions. We empower organizations to leverage data for smarter decisions and meaningful impact. At ATC, we foster a culture of growth and invite passionate individuals to join our team, contribute to impactful projects, and excel as data-driven problem solvers. We embrace diversity and welcome candidates from all educational and professional backgrounds, including recent Master’s graduates without prior technical experience.




Job Overview:

As an Entry-Level Data Analyst at ATC, you will be at the forefront of turning raw data into actionable insights. This role offers a fantastic opportunity to work on real-world challenges, collaborate with cross-functional teams, and apply your problem-solving skills in a supportive and innovative environment.




Key Responsibilities:

Collect, clean, and organize data to prepare it for analysis.
Identify trends, patterns, and correlations using statistical and analytical tools.
Develop insightful dashboards, visualizations, and reports using tools like Tableau, Power BI, or Excel.
Assist teams in making data-driven decisions by presenting clear and concise insights.
Collaborate with stakeholders to understand data requirements and deliver solutions.
Ensure data accuracy and consistency through validation and quality checks.
Stay updated on industry trends, best practices, and new data tools or technologies.

Title: Data Entry Operator
Description:
1. About Our Client:

The organization operates in the data management sector, focusing on maintaining accurate and secure databases. It addresses challenges related to data accuracy and confidentiality, ensuring that client information is properly verified and securely handled.




2. About the Opportunity:

The Data Entry Operator role involves maintaining and updating the organization''s database, ensuring data accuracy and confidentiality. This position supports operational efficiency by coordinating data entry activities and verifying client documents, contributing to the integrity and reliability of information systems.




3. Responsibilities:

• Maintain and update the organization’s database accurately

• Coordinate and oversee activities of the Data Entry Operator

• Update internal systems efficiently

• Ensure confidentiality by keeping data private and secure




4. Requirements:

• Detail-oriented with excellent typing skills

• Tech savvy with proficiency in Microsoft Office applications such as Word

• Good command of English, both oral and written

• Strong customer service skills

• High school degree or equivalent

• Strong communication and teamwork abilities

• Excellent attention to detail and time management skills

• The pay range and compensation package for this role will be determined based on the candidate’s experience, skills, and other relevant factors.

• Health insurance

• Signing bonus

• Dental insurance

Title: Data Engineer(BI Reporting)
Description:
Job Description (Posting)

Job Title

Data Engineer(BI Reporting)

Job Summary

The Project Manager (SAFe Product Owner/Product Manager) is responsible for overseeing projects from initiation through delivery, ensuring that the projects are completed on time, within scope, and within budget. As a SAFe Product Owner/Product Manager, the individual will be responsible for managing and prioritizing the product backlog, representing the customer, and working closely with the development team to ensure successful product delivery.

Key Responsibilities


Lead and manage cross-functional project teams to deliver high-quality projects within the defined constraints.
Develop and maintain project plans, budget tracking, and resource allocation.
Communicate with stakeholders to ensure alignment on project scope, timelines, and deliverables.
Facilitate team meetings, including sprint planning, reviews, retrospectives, and daily standups.
Collaborate with Product Owners and Scrum Masters to prioritize features and resolve dependencies.
Act as a liaison between the business stakeholders and the development team to ensure clear communication and understanding.
Identify and mitigate project risks, proactively resolving issues to keep projects on track.
Monitor project progress and performance metrics, providing regular updates to leadership.


Skill Requirements


Strong understanding of Agile methodology and the SAFe framework.
Excellent communication and interpersonal skills to effectively collaborate with cross-functional teams and stakeholders.
Ability to prioritize and manage conflicting priorities in a fast-paced environment.
Proficiency in project management tools such as Jira, Microsoft Project, or similar tools.
Knowledge of product management principles and practices.
Strong leadership skills to motivate and guide teams towards successful project outcomes.


Certifications: SAFe Product Owner/Product Manager (POPM) certification is preferred.

Experience

7 12 Years

Skill (Primary)

Scaling Agile (APPS) Product Management SAFe Agile

Other Requirements


3 days onsite mandatory.
Need someone with strong exposure working for service-based organizations, managing projects with onsite and offshore coordination, and extensive client-facing experience.
Excellent communication skills are mandatory.
Strong Capital Markets experience is mandatory.


Job Description

We are seeking an experienced professional with strong expertise in Snowflake, Python, Oracle PL/SQL, and reporting/BI tools to deliver scalable data solutions and lead project execution, with proven delivery experience across Capital Markets corporate functions, securities, and front-to-back-office.

Key Responsibilities


Design, develop, and optimize data solutions using Snowflake, Python, and Oracle PL/SQL.
Develop and maintain reports and dashboards using standard BI/reporting tools to support business decision-making.
Lead end-to-end project deliverables, ensuring quality, timeliness, and alignment with customer requirements.
Drive and manage weekly and monthly governance calls, including status reporting, risk management, and stakeholder communication.
Ensure all project tasks and milestones are completed on time with high quality standards.
Provide technical leadership and mentorship to the team, reviewing designs and code to ensure best practices.
Engage directly with customers to understand business needs, translate them into technical solutions, and manage ongoing interactions.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to ensure smooth delivery and adoption of solutions.
Apply Generative AI concepts and tools where relevant to enhance data engineering, analytics, automation, and productivity use cases.
Continuously identify opportunities for process improvement, automation, and innovation.


Required Skills and Experience

Technical Skills


Snowflake
Oracle PL/SQL
DBT
Python
Any BI reporting tool
Strong hands-on experience with Snowflake, including data modeling, performance optimization, and cost management.
Advanced proficiency in Python for data processing, automation, and integration.
Solid experience in Oracle PL/SQL, including complex queries, procedures, and performance tuning.
Experience with at least one enterprise reporting or BI tool.
Proven leadership skills with experience leading teams and owning project delivery.
Strong customer interaction and stakeholder management skills.
Experience driving governance forums and executive-level status reviews.
Exposure to Generative AI, including use cases, tools, or solution implementation, is highly desirable.


This role requires a strong balance of technical depth, leadership capability, and customer-facing skills to successfully deliver high-impact data and analytics solutions.

Title: BI Engineer I
Description:
Opportunity

Job Description:

We are seeking an entry level Business Intelligence Engineer to join our dynamic team. As a key member of our Customer Care data analytics department, you will play a pivotal role in supporting our data-driven decision-making. This role is perfect for someone who loves working with SQL, Python, and modern visualization tools, and who wants to grow their skills in streaming data, automation, and cloud-based analytics. You'll work in a fastmoving, data-driven environment where you’ll help build scalable insights solutions, optimize reporting, and contribute to our mission of making data-driven decisions easier and more accessible for everyone.

Responsibilities

Develop and optimize queries to extract and analyze data from large-scale databases.

Work with real-time and batch data processing to support operational reporting and alerting.

Build dynamic dashboards and self-service analytics solutions using modern visualization tools (Tableau, Streamlit, Plotly, etc.).

Automate data collection and reporting workflows, reducing manual effort for the business. Assist in designing data pipelines for customer care analytics, ensuring data is structured for ease of use and scalability. Collaborate with cross-functional teams (engineering, operations, product, and customer experience) to identify and solve business problems using data. Implement data validation, monitoring, and quality checks to ensure clean, reliable data for stakeholders.

Stay up to date on emerging trends in BI, data engineering, and cloud computing to improve our analytics stack.

Interest in empowering teams with self-service dashboards and analytics tools.

Ability to automate manual reporting tasks to drive efficiency. Strong communication and collaboration skills—able to work effectively with business and technical teams.

Support senior analysts in conducting data analysis projects.

Learn and apply data analysis techniques and best practices.

Participate in team meetings and contribute to knowledge sharing.

Qualifications

Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, or a related field.

Understanding of SQL and relational databases.

Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.

Excellent communication and collaboration skills.

Eagerness to learn and develop data analysis skills

Nice To Have

Experience with cloud computing platforms (e.g., AWS, Azure, GCP).

Experience with Python

Familiarity with data visualization tools (e.g., Tableau, Power BI, Streamlit, Plotly).

Knowledge of retail and e-commerce. Experience with A/B testing, statistical analysis, or predictive modeling is a bonus.

Title: Business Intelligence & Analytics Specialist
Description:
Company Description

Ramboll is a global architecture, engineering, and consultancy company. As a foundation-owned people company, founded in Denmark, we believe that the purpose of sustainable change is to create a thriving world for both nature and people. So, that’s where we start – and how we work. Our history is rooted in a clear vision of how a responsible company should act and being open and curious is a cornerstone of our culture. Ramboll in the Americas has thousands of experts working across more than 70 offices. Ramboll experts deliver innovative solutions across Environment & Health, Water, Energy, and Planning & Urban Design.

Job Description

This is a hybrid-based position in our Syracuse, NY office.

Applicants must be currently authorized to work in the United States on a full-time basis. No sponsorship is available for this position.

Are you eager to develop your excellence in an inclusive, collaborative, and empowering community? Are you excited about joining a company that strives to make a difference – for its employees, clients, and society?  

We are looking for a hands-on Analyst with strong SQL, VBA, and .NET skills who can turn business performance data into reliable, actionable reporting. The role requires intermediate SQL query expertise (temporary tables, indexes, joins, expressions) and practical experience with SQL Server, SSMS, Stored Procedures and Table-Valued Functions.

Your new role 

Ramboll is seeking a Business Intelligence & Analytics Specialist for an open position in our Syracuse office. The ideal candidate will be working in a challenging and dynamic environment that will include a detail-oriented team and independent work.

Your key responsibilities will be:



SQL Data Sourcing
Extract data from corporate databases.
Analytics & Business Intelligence
Translate complex business requirements from Client, Sales, and Operations teams into actionable KPIs and metrics.
Design, build, and deploy interactive dashboards that empower leadership to make data-driven decisions.
Optimize and support Legacy Applications
Maintain and make functional enhancements to existing Excel-based project management tools utilizing VBA.
Proactively identify opportunities to migrate legacy, manual spreadsheets into automated, centralized BI solutions.
Document tool architecture and logic to ensure business continuity and straightforward system updates.
AI-Driven Process Improvement
Collaborate cross-functionally to identify operational bottlenecks and manual workflows.
Research, test, and implement emerging AI tools to streamline day-to-day operations.

Qualifications

About you



Bachelor's degree in a technical field and 5 years of relevant experience
Intermediate SQL Server skills (temporary tables, indexes, joins, expressions)
Proficiency with SSMS and familiarity with Stored Procedures and Table-Valued Functions
Intermediate experience with Visual Studio 2022 or newer. Working knowledge of VB.Net and ability to edit, compile and distribute existing .NET applications
Experience with VBA (Excel and MS Access) for reporting/automation
Understanding of business practices for project costs and revenue, plus basic knowledge of financial and project management systems
Demonstrated experience or eagerness to become fluent in BI reporting
Understanding of AI prompt engineering and development of AI-enabled applications
Familiarity with Remote Desktop and basic server app management
Comfort navigating large networks and related infrastructure constraints



Additional Information

What we can offer you



Investment in your development
Being valued for the unique person you are
Never being short of inspiration from colleagues, clients, and projects
The long-term thinking of a foundation-owned company



Salary Transparency Statement

At Ramboll, we are committed to fair and transparent compensation practices that support our people and our purpose of creating sustainable societies. The anticipated base salary range for this position is $82,029-$121,505 per year. This range reflects the compensation we reasonably expect to offer at the time of posting. Actual compensation will vary based on factors such as location, skills, experience, education, and internal equity. Because we operate across multiple U.S. markets, pay may differ depending on the cost of labor in specific locations. In addition to base salary, this position may be eligible for bonus, overtime or other forms of compensation and we offer a comprehensive benefits package that supports well-being, professional development, and work-life balance. Our recruiters are happy to share more about the specific salary range and total rewards package applicable to your location during the hiring process.

Ready to join us?

Please submit your application with your up-to-date CV. We invite diversity in all its forms and encourage applicants from all groups to apply.

Thank you for taking the time to apply! We look forward to receiving your application.

Work at the heart of sustainable change with Ramboll in the Americas

Ramboll is a global architecture, engineering, and consultancy company. As a foundation-owned people company, founded in Denmark, we believe that the purpose of sustainable change is to create a thriving world for both nature and people. So, that’s where we start – and how we work. Our history is rooted in a clear vision of how a responsible company should act and being open and curious is a cornerstone of our culture. Ramboll in the Americas has thousands of experts working across more than 70 offices. Ramboll experts deliver innovative solutions across Environment & Health, Water, Energy, and Planning & Urban Design.

Where People Flourish

Our mission is to create sustainable societies where people and nature flourish. This means that a culture of inclusion is embedded in everything we do. Our people bring diverse backgrounds and experiences to the company, enabling us to deliver innovative and forward-thinking solutions to our clients. We also know how important it is to achieve the right balance of where, when, and how much you work. At Ramboll, we offer flexibility as part of our positive and inclusive approach to work. We are committed to equal employment opportunity, regardless of age, disability or medical condition, gender identity, marriage and domestic partnership, pregnancy and maternity, race, ancestry, or national origin, religion or belief, sex and sexual orientation, military service and veteran status, or any other protected characteristic. Ramboll wants to ensure opportunities are accessible to candidates with disabilities. So, please let us know if there are any changes we could make to the application process to make it more comfortable for you. You can contact us at job.advert.accessibility@ramboll.com with such requests.

Title: Senior Healthcare Data Analyst
Description:
1. About Our Client:

The organization operates within the behavioral health industry, focusing on enhancing the overall well-being of individuals through integrated treatment of mind and body. It serves over 15 million people across the United States, addressing the critical challenge of improving behavioral, physical, and social health through its services.




2. About the Opportunity:

The Senior Healthcare Data Analyst plays a key role in supporting clinical and operational performance by providing data-driven insights. This position is responsible for translating evaluation questions into analytic strategies, generating reports, and conducting statistical analyses that guide decision-making and improve healthcare outcomes.




3. Responsibilities:

• Lead reporting and analytics initiatives to support product innovation and population health strategies.

• Collaborate with clinical and strategy teams to identify target populations and develop performance measures.

• Perform claims and utilization analyses to identify cost drivers and savings opportunities.

• Model cost-saving scenarios and recommend improvements to financial outcomes.

• Evaluate financial impacts of contracts and strategic initiatives.

• Deliver timely analyses to support management and operational goals.




4. Requirements:

• Bachelor’s Degree.

• Minimum of 5 years’ experience in a similar role within healthcare payer or provider settings.

• Knowledge of statistical methods such as regression, propensity matching, and analysis of variance.

• Proficiency in SQL, SAS, Python, R, Tableau, PowerBI, or similar tools for data analysis and visualization.

• Experience with enterprise data warehouses and commercial/Medicare/Medicaid claims data.

• Ability to interpret technical materials and financial data.

• Effective presentation skills for both internal and external stakeholders.

• Strong problem-solving, analytic, and communication skills.

• Ability to work independently and collaboratively.

• Must pass comprehensive background checks and drug screening.




5. Pay Range and Compensation Package:

• Annual compensation between $103,000 and $115,000, plus an annual performance-based, discretionary incentive.

• Compensation depends on factors such as skills, education, certifications, experience, and market data.




6. Benefits & Perks:

• Medical, dental, and vision insurance coverage.

• 401(k) plan with competitive employer matching.

• Company-paid life and disability insurance.

• Paid parental leave and wellbeing incentives.

• Generous paid time off including volunteer time.

• Flexible spending accounts for healthcare and dependent care.

• Professional development and tuition reimbursement.

• Remote work flexibility depending on the role.




Equal Opportunity Statement: Our client is an equal opportunity employer. They celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, or national origin.




Note:

RemoteHunter is not the Employer of Record (EOR) for this role. Our purpose in this opportunity is to connect exceptional candidates with leading employers. We help job seekers worldwide discover roles that match their goals and guide them to complete their full application directly through the hiring company’s career page or ATS.

Title: Data Analyst - Finance (Remote)
Description:
Role: Data Analyst - Finance (Remote)
Location: Remote (Work from Anywhere)
Job Type: Contract
Payout: $40 - $65/hour




Role Overview:

We are hiring for one of our clients, seeking a Financial Data Analyst to work on a contract basis. This role involves analyzing financial data to support decision-making and reporting. The position requires expertise in financial modeling, data visualization, and regulatory compliance.




Key Responsibilities:

• Collect, process, and analyze financial data from multiple sources to identify trends and anomalies.

• Develop and maintain financial models to forecast revenue, expenses, and cash flow.

• Prepare detailed reports and dashboards for stakeholders to support strategic planning.

• Ensure data accuracy and compliance with accounting standards and regulatory requirements.

• Collaborate with cross-functional teams to integrate financial insights into business operations.




Required Skills & Qualifications:

• Proficiency in financial data analysis tools such as Excel, SQL, or Python.

• Experience with financial modeling and forecasting techniques.

• Strong understanding of accounting principles and financial reporting standards.

• Ability to create visualizations using tools like Tableau or Power BI.

• Familiarity with data cleaning and validation processes.




More About the Opportunity:

This role offers a unique opportunity to work with a global leader in the financial services industry, contributing to accurate financial reporting and strategic decision-making.




Equal Opportunity Employer:

We hire based on skills and expertise. All qualified candidates are welcome regardless of background, experience, or prior employment history. Applications are reviewed solely on demonstrated technical ability and qualifications.

Title: Virtual Assistant - Remote
Description:
About The Company

Decentralized Masters is a pioneering organization in the field of DeFi education with a global footprint. Established just two years ago, the company has rapidly expanded from a small startup to a thriving enterprise with over 150 dedicated professionals. Recognized as one of the fastest-growing entities in the decentralized finance sector, Decentralized Masters is on a trajectory to become a unicorn by 2030, driven by innovative strategies and a bootstrap funding model. The company is projected to generate approximately $50 million in revenue this year, reflecting its strong market presence and growth potential.

At Decentralized Masters, the focus extends beyond mere educational offerings; the organization has successfully empowered over 4,500 investors to enter and thrive in the DeFi ecosystem. The company prides itself on fostering a vibrant community, leveraging cutting-edge technology, and assembling a team of leading experts in DeFi and blockchain technology. Its core mission is to deliver high-quality, long-term resources that enable members to safeguard and grow their financial futures in the rapidly evolving world of Web3 and digital finance.

Driven by an ambitious vision, Decentralized Masters aims to build the largest and most influential DeFi ecosystem globally. Its commitment to setting the gold standard in DeFi education underscores its transformative approach to digital finance, positioning it as a key player shaping the future of blockchain-based financial systems.

About The Role

The Virtual Assistant role at Decentralized Masters offers an exciting opportunity to be at the heart of a dynamic and innovative organization. This position involves managing communications, supporting customer onboarding, and assisting with administrative tasks to ensure smooth operations across the company. As a vital link between clients and the organization, the Virtual Assistant will be responsible for maintaining clear and consistent communication, escalating customer queries when necessary, and preparing reports and presentations to support strategic decision-making.

This role requires a proactive individual with excellent organizational skills, a customer-centric mindset, and the ability to work independently in a fully remote environment. The ideal candidate will be a native English speaker or possess a high level of professional proficiency, based in South America, and experienced in customer service and administrative support. Familiarity with DeFi or cryptocurrencies is a plus, but not mandatory. The position offers the chance to contribute to a fast-growing company that is revolutionizing digital finance and Web3 education.

Qualifications


High school diploma or equivalent; additional qualifications as an Administrator or Executive Assistant are a plus
Proven experience as a Virtual Assistant or in a similar administrative/customer service role
Customer service experience is essential
Native English speaker or professional-level proficiency in English
Must be located in South America
Familiarity with DeFi or cryptocurrency concepts is advantageous
Excellent proficiency with word-processing and spreadsheet software (Google Suite, MS Excel)
Experience with online calendars and scheduling tools (e.g., Google Calendar)
Strong communication skills via phone, email, and instant messaging
Exceptional time management and organizational skills
Ability to work independently and remotely with minimal supervision


Responsibilities


Manage and respond to emails and other communication channels promptly and professionally
Handle escalation of customer queries to ensure timely resolution
Assist with onboarding new customers, providing necessary information and support
Draft and prepare reports, presentations, and other documentation as needed
Maintain clear and continuous communication with clients and internal teams
Perform general administrative duties to support daily operations
Coordinate scheduling and calendar management for team members and clients
Support team with various tasks to enhance overall productivity and efficiency


Benefits


Competitive salary package
Full-time employment with a fully remote work environment
Unlimited paid time off (PTO) to promote work-life balance
Participation in team off-site events and company retreats
Dynamic and youthful company culture with team members across more than 30 countries
Flexible working location—work from anywhere in the world
Opportunity to be part of a fast-growing company at the forefront of DeFi and Web3 innovation


Equal Opportunity

Decentralized Masters is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. We do not discriminate based on race, ethnicity, gender, age, sexual orientation, disability, or any other protected characteristic. We encourage applications from candidates of all backgrounds who meet the qualifications and are passionate about contributing to the future of DeFi and blockchain technology.

  • Maintain clear and continuous communication with clients and internal teams
  • Perform general administrative duties to support daily operations
  • Coordinate scheduling and calendar management for team members and clients
  • Support team with various tasks to enhance overall productivity and efficiency

Benefits

  • Competitive salary package
  • Full-time employment with a fully remote work environment
  • Unlimited paid time off (PTO) to promote work-life balance
  • Participation in team off-site events and company retreats
  • Dynamic and youthful company culture with team members across more than 30 countries
  • Flexible working location—work from anywhere in the world
  • Opportunity to be part of a fast-growing company at the forefront of DeFi and Web3 innovation

Equal Opportunity

Decentralized Masters is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. We do not discriminate based on race, ethnicity, gender, age, sexual orientation, disability, or any other protected characteristic. We encourage applications from candidates of all backgrounds who meet the qualifications and are passionate about contributing to the future of DeFi and blockchain technology.

Title: Technology Associate - Relocation to Dubai
Description:
Job Purpose

Are you a fresh graduate ready to shape the future of technology and travel with one of the world’s leading aviation brands?

As a Technology Associate, you will join a fast-paced one-year development experience within Emirates Group IT, gaining hands-on exposure, mentorship, and opportunities across diverse technology specialisations including AI, cloud, cybersecurity, and software engineering.

The Technology Associate is part of the Emirates Group IT team and supports the design, development, implementation and maintenance of technology solutions and products that enable and enhance Emirates Group business operations.




In This Role, You Will




Perform relevant technical tasks with a goal of developing scalable and sustainable solutions using established technology and engineering principles.
Participate in analysing business and system requirements to support the technical design process and contribute to the development of technical solutions aligned with agreed standards.
Develop, configure, test and implement technology solutions within a collaborative team environment to meet both functional and technical requirements.
Research, diagnose, troubleshoot and identify solutions to resolve system or application issues. Follow standard procedures for proper escalation of unresolved issues to the appropriate internal teams.
Collaborate with members of cross-functional teams to identify areas of inefficiency and propose improvements, while adhering to Emirates Group IT standards, guidelines and best practices.
Contribute to unit testing, validation and quality assurance activities with guidance from senior team members.
Support the maintenance of Technology Playbooks, standards documentation and other governance artefacts.




Qualifications

To be considered for this role, you must meet the below requirements:




Bachelor’s degree in information technology, computer science, computer engineering, software engineering or technology related field.
CGPA: 3.0 or higher (or equivalent).
1+ years of experience through exposure to current technologies in coursework or through internships in technology related fields.




This role is designed as an entry point for a university graduate in a technology related field of study as mentioned above.




Skills/Knowledge




Strong problem-solving skills with the ability to analyse technical challenges and contribute to well-designed, efficient and quality technology solutions.
Demonstrated ability to work collaboratively within cross-functional teams, with open communication and a positive team mindset.
Foundational understanding of technology delivery methodologies (e.g., Agile, DevOps practices) and quality assurance concepts including unit testing, functional testing and test automation.
Demonstrable passion for exploring and learning new technologies aligned to business needs.
Exposure to one or more contemporary technology domains (e.g., application development, cloud, enterprise platforms, integration, data, digital or security technologies), with foundational understanding relevant to business requirements.




Salary & Benefits




Join us in Dubai and enjoy an attractive tax-free salary and travel benefits that are exclusive to our industry, including discounts on flights and hotel stays around the world.

This is a full-time, office-based opportunity located in our Dubai headquarters. Successful candidates will be supported with a comprehensive relocation package, with Emirates arranging and assisting throughout the relocation process to ensure a smooth transition to Dubai.

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
Job Title: Data Engineer

Company: Aaratech Inc.

Experience: 2+ years

Compensation: $70,000 – $80,000 per year

Work Authorization

At this time, we are only able to consider candidates who are authorized to work in the United States without sponsorship.

Job Summary

Aaratech Inc. is seeking a Data Engineer. In this role, you will design, develop, and maintain scalable data pipelines while supporting enterprise data integration, analytics, and business intelligence initiatives.

Key Responsibilities

Design, develop, and maintain ETL/ELT data pipelines.
Build and optimize data integration workflows from multiple enterprise data sources.
Develop SQL queries and data transformation processes.
Monitor data quality, integrity, and pipeline performance.
Troubleshoot and resolve data processing issues.
Collaborate with Data Analysts, Business Analysts, and Software Engineers to support reporting and analytics requirements.

Qualifications

Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Data Engineering, or a related field.
2+ years of experience in Data Engineering, ETL Development, Data Integration, or Database Development.
Experience with SQL, relational databases, and ETL tools such as Azure Data Factory, SSIS, Informatica, or Talend.
Basic knowledge of data warehousing concepts and cloud data platforms.
Strong analytical, communication, and problem-solving skills.

Preferred Skills

Experience with Azure, AWS, or Google Cloud data services.
Knowledge of Snowflake, Databricks, or Amazon Redshift.
Basic Python programming or scripting skills.
Experience with Power BI or Tableau.
Any certifications such as Microsoft Azure Data Engineer Associate, AWS Certified Data Engineer, or Snowflake certifications are a plus.

About Aaratech Inc.

Aaratech Inc. is a technology-driven organization focused on delivering innovative solutions across cybersecurity, healthcare, and enterprise domains. We are committed to helping organizations build scalable data platforms, optimize data workflows, and enable data-driven decision-making through modern technologies and innovative engineering solutions.

Title: Supplier Quality Coordinator
Description:
About the Company:

Built on a dream and distilled in Austin, Texas, Tito's Handmade Vodka has been bringing people (and dogs) together over the last two decades. Tito's Handmade Vodka has gained a reputation for its high-quality vodka, charitable contributions, and its goal to make people happy while making the world a better place.

About the Position:

Tito's Handmade Vodka is seeking a Supplier Quality Coordinator to be responsible for leading, coordinating, and supporting packaging-based projects in the bottling facility. This position requires attention to detail, organization skills and collaboration with internal teams and external suppliers to support consistent product quality. Tito's Handmade Vodka is seeking a Supplier Quality Coordinator to be responsible for coordinating supplier quality activities with Procurement and the Quality department.

This role is ideal for someone who thrives on organization, takes pride in closing the loop, and knows how to navigate a fast-paced environment where no two days look the same. Full-time, on-site position, Overtime offered.

Core Responsibilities:

· Facilitate and track packaging trials on the bottling lines.

· Understand packaging and technical specifications for each raw material.

· Understand the supplier's name, location, and products manufactured for each raw material.

· Understand, at an entry level, the performance of bottle equipment for packaging materials.

· Coordinate delivery of materials and run schedules to meet project timelines.

· Collaborate with Quality, Maintenance, Engineering, and Production teams on projects.

· Serve as the first point of contact for the Purchasing department for any quality issues affecting bottling line performance.

· Participate in project meetings and provide updates on trial timeline and material status.

· Understand the resolution of the packaging supplier's Root Cause and Corrective Actions.

· Create and maintain detailed records, logs, and documentation.

· Prepare various reports and correspondence regularly.

· Facilitate onsite supplier visits for Quality and Procurement.

· Maintain a positive supplier partnership.

· Establish and maintain strong working relationships with suppliers, subcontractors, and internal stakeholders.

· Assists as a liaison between suppliers and finance in the disposition of non-conforming material.

· Identify process improvements in material qualification processes to increase efficiency and accuracy.

· Support initiatives aimed at improving supplier quality and reducing defects.

· Other duties may be assigned.



Requirements



· Bachelor's Degree in Supply Chain, Business Administration, Logistics, Procurement, Engineering, Packaging Science or related field or equivalent experience

· Experienced in Microsoft Office Applications (Excel, Word, PowerPoint, etc.)

· Minimum of 1-2 years' experience in manufacturing, project management, material handling and/or quality assurance.

· Highly recommended, but not required, Purchasing and supplier relations experience.

· Self-Starter and Critical thinker with the ability to communicate clearly and work effectively across departments.

· Ability to solve problems and exercise judgement based on the analysis of multiple sources of information

· Can explain difficult or sensitive information and transfer knowledge to others



Benefits




· Competitive Salary + Bonus Plan

· Medical, Dental & Vision Insurance

· Company Paid Life & Disability Insurance

· Voluntary Insurance Plans

· 401k plan with company match, profit sharing

· Vacation, Sick, Flex Time

· Paid Parental Leave

· Adoption Assistance Program

· Dependent Care Flexible Spending Account

· Health Savings Account

· Lifestyle Spending Account

· Financial Wellness Benefit

· Employee Assistance Program


Equal Opportunity Employer

Fifth Generation Distilled Spirits, Inc.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Job Title: Business Analyst II

Our Vision: RigUp is where the best workers power the world’s most successful projects. We are united by a common mission: to deliver services and technology that fundamentally change how skilled workers and the businesses they serve get hard work done.

Job Description

This role leads the execution of digital and process improvement opportunities from intake through solution delivery. The position collaborates with business unit colleagues and leadership to document value cases, refine requirements, gather user feedback, and coordinate with digital resources to build, deliver, and train users on digital solutions. The role also supports data foundation efforts to enable self-serve analytics, develops and maintains analytics assets, and drives digital fluency across the organization.

Responsibilities


Lead execution of digital and process improvement opportunities through the pipeline to solution delivery
Collaborate with business unit colleagues and leadership to document value cases, refine requirements, and collect user feedback
Coordinate with other digital resources to build, deliver, and train users on digital solutions
Execute data foundation efforts with IT to enable self-serve analytics for GLS
Develop visualizations and reports using Power BI and maintain GLS analytics workspaces
Drive digital fluency by delivering training and support for common productivity tools
Develop and lead a digital citizen community
Partner with enterprise IT to leverage digital expert resources
Make recommendations to simplify work and improve efficiency across digital platforms
Step in to lead initiatives and support long-term strategic planning


Skills And Qualifications


Technical expertise in digital platforms used for productivity and data automation: Power Apps, Power BI, Power Automate, AI Builder, VS Code/GitHub
Technical expertise in data management platforms: SharePoint, Azure file share
Technical expertise in opportunity/portfolio management tools: Azure DevOps (ADO)
Familiarity with Sample Manager is a plus
Self-starter with the ability to learn new tools and identify fit-for-purpose applications
Strong rapport-building skills and clear communication
Technical aptitude to understand how technology integrates with people and organizations
Knowledge in coding preferred
Bachelor’s degree in computer science, data science, data analytics, business information systems, or business management preferred
2–4 years of experience required


Location: Richmond, CA

Pay: $47.61/hr (W2)

Schedule: 9/80 -In office M-TH, Fridays remote

Title: Data Systems Analyst (7199U) #87317
Description:
About Berkeley

At the University of California, Berkeley, we are dedicated to fostering a community where everyone feels welcome and can thrive. Our culture of openness, freedom, and belonging make it a special place for students, faculty, and staff. As a world-leading institution, Berkeley is known for its academic and research excellence, public mission, diverse student body, and commitment to equity and social justice. Since our founding in 1868, we have driven innovation, creating global intellectual, economic, and social value. We are looking for applicants who reflect California's diversity and want to be part of an inclusive, equity-focused community that views education as a matter of social justice. Please consider whether your values align with our Guiding Values and Principles, Principles of Community, and Strategic Plan.

At UC Berkeley, we believe that learning is a fundamental part of working, and provide space for supportive colleague communities via numerous employee resource groups (staff organizations). Our goal is for everyone on the Berkeley campus to feel supported and equipped to realize their full potential. We actively support this by providing all of our full-time staff employees with at least 80 hours (10 days) of paid time per year to engage in professional development activities. Find out more about how you can grow your career at UC Berkeley.

Departmental Overview

University Development and Alumni Relations (UDAR) strengthens Berkeleys excellence through engagement with alumni, parents, undergraduate and graduate students, and friends of the University, raising public awareness about Berkeley and increasing private philanthropy. UDAR provides overall direction for the campus philanthropic advancement strategies and works to strengthen unit development in partnership with campus fundraisers. The Division handles a range of central activities and services encompassing communications, stewardship, prospect development, data management, gift management, reporting and analytics, and more.

Advancement Operations develops and delivers the centralized business systems and services that support the work of UC Berkeleys 30 decentralized fundraising unitshelping them collaborate and communicate in order to maximize their collective results. Advancement Operations also provides governance through its role as the official source of alumni and fundraising data.

The Advancement Information Services (AIS) unit develops and delivers the primary business data and systems that support the work of UC Berkeleys centralized and decentralized fundraising activities. AIS oversees several interrelated information technology systems and tools, including the Cal Advancement Data System (CADS), the database in which advancement professionals research and record fundraising activity. AIS encompasses three units: Data and Business Solutions, Technology Services, and Strategic Planning and Management.

Data Management (DM) is the office of record for data related to alumni, donors, and friends of UC Berkeley. DM helps the advancement community meet programmatic and campaign goals by providing accurate, complete data that documents the campuss relationship with its constituents. DM protects, archives, enhances, and manages the data and delivers it through efficient processes and effective systems in a secure environment. In addition, DM supports the campus advancement community with customer service on all CADS issues.

Position Summary

The Data Systems Analyst plays a key role in leveraging data to drive alumni engagement, donor relations, and fundraising strategy. This position ensures the integrity, accessibility, and strategic use of advancement data across platforms and tools University Development and Alumni Relations (UDAR) uses daily to engage constituents. Serving as a bridge between technical teams and advancement professionals, the Data Systems Analyst manages and/or develops data practices, guidelines, dictionaries, and services that support data standardization in alignment with UDAR's data and governance strategies. The Data Systems Analyst ensures compliance with data privacy standards (such as FERPA and donor confidentiality) and partners closely with business units to enable data-driven strategies that enhance institutional fundraising and engagement efforts.

Application Review Date

The First Review Date for this job is: July 20, 2026. For full consideration, please apply on or before the first review date.

Responsibilities


Applies professional and broadly defined business analysis and data / information administration and data use concepts to manage and perform complex assignments.
Identifies data sources, target populations, and/or new constituencies to be enhanced or established.
Develops methodology for data enhancement or retrieval in assigned specialty area(s).
Protects integrity, authenticity, and security of data in all assignment activities.
Independently designs and implements regular data audits to identify and resolve issues related to data quality, completeness, consistency, and accuracy.
Develops audit methodologies, recommends corrective actions, and collaborates with business partners to ensure continuous data integrity improvements.
Independently manages documentation, including data dictionary, business rules, practices, and processes.
Translates legacy processes and practices to future state.
Provides ongoing CRM training and support for business units as it pertains to data uploads, standards, and maintenance.
Independently designs information transformation and upload processes.
Utilizes programming skills to clean, prepare, and optimize data sets, creating efficiencies and improving data quality.
Conducts complex data management research and plans metadata acquisition for sets of unstructured data objects such as collections of images, documents or recordings.
Coordinates student worker schedules and assignments.
Systematically reviews their work for accuracy and completeness.
Proactively meets with student workers to identify, resolve, and prevent errors.
Continuously works on skill building and professional development to advance their role.


Required Qualifications


Minimum two years experience working with data quality and governance issues and requirements.
Minimum two years experience applying knowledge of data management systems, practices and standards.
Demonstrated proficiency in writing SQL, programming/scripting languages (e.g., Python, R), or utilizing advanced spreadsheet automation tools (e.g., Excel Power Query, Google Apps Script) for data analysis, transformation, and process automation.
Demonstrated ability to conduct work using relevant state, federal, and global rules and regulations as it pertains to data management and privacy.
Demonstrated ability to work with others from diverse backgrounds.
Demonstrated effective communication and interpersonal skills.
Demonstrated service orientation skills.

About Berkeley

At the University of California, Berkeley, we are dedicated to fostering a community where everyone feels welcome and can thrive. Our culture of openness, freedom, and belonging make it a special place for students, faculty, and staff. As a world-leading institution, Berkeley is known for its academic and research excellence, public mission, diverse student body, and commitment to equity and social justice. Since our founding in 1868, we have driven innovation, creating global intellectual, economic, and social value. We are looking for applicants who reflect California's diversity and want to be part of an inclusive, equity-focused community that views education as a matter of social justice. Please consider whether your values align with our Guiding Values and Principles, Principles of Community, and Strategic Plan.

At UC Berkeley, we believe that learning is a fundamental part of working, and provide space for supportive colleague communities via numerous employee resource groups (staff organizations). Our goal is for everyone on the Berkeley campus to feel supported and equipped to realize their full potential. We actively support this by providing all of our full-time staff employees with at least 80 hours (10 days) of paid time per year to engage in professional development activities. Find out more about how you can grow your career at UC Berkeley.

Departmental Overview

University Development and Alumni Relations (UDAR) strengthens Berkeleys excellence through engagement with alumni, parents, undergraduate and graduate students, and friends of the University, raising public awareness about Berkeley and increasing private philanthropy. UDAR provides overall direction for the campus philanthropic advancement strategies and works to strengthen unit development in partnership with campus fundraisers. The Division handles a range of central activities and services encompassing communications, stewardship, prospect development, data management, gift management, reporting and analytics, and more.

Advancement Operations develops and delivers the centralized business systems and services that support the work of UC Berkeleys 30 decentralized fundraising unitshelping them collaborate and communicate in order to maximize their collective results. Advancement Operations also provides governance through its role as the official source of alumni and fundraising data.

The Advancement Information Services (AIS) unit develops and delivers the primary business data and systems that support the work of UC Berkeleys centralized and decentralized fundraising activities. AIS oversees several interrelated information technology systems and tools, including the Cal Advancement Data System (CADS), the database in which advancement professionals research and record fundraising activity. AIS encompasses three units: Data and Business Solutions, Technology Services, and Strategic Planning and Management.

Data Management (DM) is the office of record for data related to alumni, donors, and friends of UC Berkeley. DM helps the advancement community meet programmatic and campaign goals by providing accurate, complete data that documents the campuss relationship with its constituents. DM protects, archives, enhances, and manages the data and delivers it through efficient processes and effective systems in a secure environment. In addition, DM supports the campus advancement community with customer service on all CADS issues.

Position Summary

The Data Systems Analyst plays a key role in leveraging data to drive alumni engagement, donor relations, and fundraising strategy. This position ensures the integrity, accessibility, and strategic use of advancement data across platforms and tools University Development and Alumni Relations (UDAR) uses daily to engage constituents. Serving as a bridge between technical teams and advancement professionals, the Data Systems Analyst manages and/or develops data practices, guidelines, dictionaries, and services that support data standardization in alignment with UDAR's data and governance strategies. The Data Systems Analyst ensures compliance with data privacy standards (such as FERPA and donor confidentiality) and partners closely with business units to enable data-driven strategies that enhance institutional fundraising and engagement efforts.

Application Review Date

The First Review Date for this job is: July 20, 2026. For full consideration, please apply on or before the first review date.

  • Protects integrity, authenticity, and security of data in all assignment activities.
  • Independently designs and implements regular data audits to identify and resolve issues related to data quality, completeness, consistency, and accuracy.
  • Develops audit methodologies, recommends corrective actions, and collaborates with business partners to ensure continuous data integrity improvements.
  • Independently manages documentation, including data dictionary, business rules, practices, and processes.
  • Translates legacy processes and practices to future state.
  • Provides ongoing CRM training and support for business units as it pertains to data uploads, standards, and maintenance.
  • Independently designs information transformation and upload processes.
  • Utilizes programming skills to clean, prepare, and optimize data sets, creating efficiencies and improving data quality.
  • Conducts complex data management research and plans metadata acquisition for sets of unstructured data objects such as collections of images, documents or recordings.
  • Coordinates student worker schedules and assignments.
  • Systematically reviews their work for accuracy and completeness.
  • Proactively meets with student workers to identify, resolve, and prevent errors.
  • Continuously works on skill building and professional development to advance their role.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Crusoe is on a mission to accelerate the abundance of energy and intelligence. As the only vertically integrated AI infrastructure company built from the ground up, we own and operate each layer of the stack — from electrons to tokens — to power the world's most ambitious AI workloads. When you join Crusoe, you join a team that is building the future, faster.

We're in the midst of the greatest industrial revolution of our time. The demand for AI compute is boundless, and power is a bottleneck. We're solving that — with an energy-first approach that makes AI infrastructure better for the world and faster for the people innovating with AI.

We're looking for problem-solving, opportunity-finding teammates with a sense of urgency, who believe in the scale of our ambition and thrive on a path not fully paved — people who want to grow their careers alongside a team of experts across energy, manufacturing, data center construction, and cloud services.

If you want to do the most meaningful work of your career, help our customers and partners advance their AI strategies, and be part of a high-performing team that believes in each other, come build with us at Crusoe.

About the Role:

Join Crusoe as a Data Analyst, a dedicated hire on our central Data Science and Engineering team. This full-time position is focused on supporting the Data Center Infrastructure Group (DIG) — the arm of Crusoe that builds and operates data centers, spanning real estate, energy, construction, and parts lifecycle management. You will work directly with business stakeholders to own reporting, maintain data accuracy, and ensure that leaders across DIG have timely, reliable information to drive high-stakes operational decisions.

What You'll Be Working On:


Own Reporting & Dashboards Build, maintain, and continuously improve the weekly and monthly reports and dashboards that DIG leadership relies on for operational decision-making. You are the DRI for accuracy and timeliness.
Partner with Business Stakeholders Work directly with leaders across construction, real estate, energy, and procurement to understand their needs and translate business questions into analytical deliverables.
Track Complex Infrastructure Projects DIG runs large, multi-year construction projects with long lead times and many vendors. You will build and maintain tracking for project progress, cost burn-down, timelines, and financial performance.
Champion Data Quality Proactively surface anomalies and inconsistencies in key datasets. When a number is wrong, you own the investigation and follow-through — working with data engineers to resolve root causes.
Communicate Insights to Executive Stakeholders Translate analytical findings into clear, executive-ready narratives. You'll regularly support senior leadership with the data and context they need to make informed decisions.



What You'll Bring to the Team:


3–5 Years of Analytics Experience A proven track record in an analyst role, ideally within an enterprise, infrastructure, or operationally complex environment.
Strong SQL Skills You will be querying BigQuery daily. Comfort working with large, complex datasets across multiple source systems is essential.
Reporting & Dashboard Expertise Hands-on experience building and maintaining operational dashboards and reports. Familiarity with BI tools — we use Sigma as our primary tool, alongside Looker, Power BI, or Tableau experience also welcomed.
Executive Communication Demonstrated ability to present data and insights clearly to senior and executive audiences — written, verbal, and visual.
Stakeholder Management Proven experience working directly with business partners to gather requirements and deliver data products. You're credible in a room with construction project managers and equally comfortable reviewing a report with a finance lead.
Business Acumen You understand why you are building something, not just what. You ask questions until the business context is clear and can explain your work to non-technical stakeholders.
Ownership Track record of being the go-to person for "is this number right?" You take accountability for data accuracy and don't wait to be told when something is off.



Bonus Points


Background in construction, manufacturing, supply chain, or other operationally complex industries.
Experience with ERP systems (SAP, Oracle, Sage Intacct, or similar) and the data that flows from them.
Python for data analysis or workflow automation.
Experience working in a fast-growing company where processes are still being built.



Our Data Stack


Cloud: Google Cloud Platform (GCP)
Data Warehouse: BigQuery
Transformation: dbt (SQL-based)
BI Tools: Sigma (primary), Grafana (operational)



Benefits:


Competitive compensation and equity packages
Restricted Stock Units
Paid time off, paid holidays & leave of absence programs
Comprehensive health, dental & vision insurance
Employer contributions to HSA account
Paid parental leave
Paid life insurance, short-term and long-term disability
Professional development & tuition reimbursement
Mental health & wellness support
Commuter benefits (parking & transit)
Cell phone stipend
401(k) Retirement plan with company match up to 4% of salary
Volunteer time off
Global travel insurance & emergency assistance
Daily meals allowance
Additional perks & programs specific to location



Compensation Range

Compensation will be paid in the range of up to $136,850 -$161,000 + Bonus. Restricted Stock Units are included in all offers. Compensation to be determined by the applicant's knowledge, education, and abilities, as well as internal equity and alignment with market data.

Crusoe is an Equal Opportunity Employer. Employment decisions are made without regard to race, color, religion, disability, genetic information, pregnancy, citizenship, marital status, sex/gender, sexual preference/ orientation, gender identity, age, veteran status, national origin, or any other status protected by law or regulation.

Title: Compensation Data Analyst
Description:
About Compa

Compa is a venture-backed AI startup revolutionizing the future of compensation.

In a dynamic job market with hiring challenges, accountability, and the rise of AI, companies need the best data to stay ahead of industry changes, competition, and costs. Compa has developed the premier real-time compensation data platform, delivering top-tier compensation intelligence to leading enterprise teams.

Compa is a compensation intelligence company built to augment enterprise compensation teams in the era of AI.

Our customers include the world’s biggest companies: NVIDIA, Stripe, DoorDash, Open AI, TMobile, Moderna, Workday, Ulta, Target, and more.

Locations:

Compa headquarters are located in Irvine, California, with growing sites in Denver, Colorado and San Francisco, California. We’re a collaborative, curious, and driven team that values transparency, ownership, and continuous learning and prioritizing in person work where possible.

The Role

The Compensation Data Analyst plays a critical role in maintaining trust in Compa’s compensation insights by monitoring data yields, identifying systemic data issues, resolving customer-specific data problems, and supporting ongoing data releases and quality management.

Key Responsibilities


Monitor the health and recency of compensation data across customer accounts.
Investigate and resolve data inconsistencies to ensure reliable insights in the platform.
Partner with Product, Engineering, and Integrations teams to support stable data pipelines.
Respond to customer- and internally-reported data questions with clear, timely communication.
Execute routine data updates and validate accuracy as part of ongoing data releases.
Perform recurring data quality checks to proactively maintain data integrity.
You’ll work at the intersection of compensation expertise, data operations, and product quality.
The role offers clear ownership and visibility, with meaningful impact on customer trust and product reliability.


Minimum Qualifications


Experience working with structured datasets and performing data validation or quality checks.
At least 3 years of experience working with compensation data and deep knowledge of compensation benchmarking
Familiarity with HR technology systems and comfort working with technology systems.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with attention to detail.
Ability to communicate clearly about data findings and issues with both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Comfort working in a fast-paced, collaborative environment with evolving priorities.


Preferred Qualifications


Exposure to data integrations or APIs.
Data analysis and proficiency in advanced data analysis (SQL, Python, etc.)
Prior experience in a customer-impacting or support-adjacent role, where clear communication and timely resolution were important.


Compensation Range: $100K - $145K
