Title: Analyst - Data Analytics - Digital Workplace
Description:
Job Description

The Digital Workplace Team at American Express is entering into a new phase of technology transformation driven by opportunities to improve Colleague experience, raise productivity and collaboration, and drive operational efficiency of all service and infrastructure operations.

American Express is committed to delivering the world’s best colleague digital experience. As part of the Digital Workplace organization, we measure what matters—ensuring that our technology investments drive meaningful adoption, engagement, and value realization across the enterprise.

We are seeking a Data Analyst who brings strong analytical rigor, business curiosity, and the ability to translate complex data into executive-ready insights. This role will play a critical part in supporting enterprise reporting, adoption measurement, and scorecard development across key digital workplace initiatives.

It is ideal for a practitioner who is comfortable working with large datasets, synthesizing insights into compelling narratives, and partnering closely with product, research, and leadership stakeholders to inform strategic decisions

You are analytical, detail-oriented, and motivated by uncovering patterns in data that help teams make better decisions. You enjoy transforming raw telemetry and usage data into clear insights that influence outcomes. You are comfortable navigating ambiguity and working across multiple data sources to tell a cohesive story.

You balance technical proficiency with business acumen. You know that data alone isn’t enough—impact comes from clarity, context, and communication.

If you enjoy combining analytical depth with strategic storytelling, this role is for you.

Responsibilities

What You’ll Do

As part of our integrated colleague experience team, you will provide analytical expertise to measure program success, inform executive reporting, and support data-driven decision-making across digital workplace initiatives.

Key Responsibilities Include


Analyze adoption, engagement, and behavioral telemetry data to assess product performance and value realization
Develop and maintain executive-level scorecards and reporting frameworks aligned to program success metrics
Partner with Power BI developers and data engineers to ensure data accuracy, consistency, and usability
Identify trends, risks, and opportunities through exploratory data analysis
Translate complex findings into clear, concise insights tailored for both technical and non-technical audiences
Support quarterly business reviews and leadership updates with data-driven narratives
Establish and document measurement methodologies and definitions to ensure reporting consistency
Collaborate with UX researchers and product teams to connect quantitative signals with qualitative insights


Qualifications


Proven experience analyzing large datasets and generating actionable insights
Strong proficiency in data analysis tools such as SQL, Excel, Power BI, or similar platforms
Experience working with telemetry, usage analytics, or enterprise software adoption data
Ability to create executive-ready dashboards, visualizations, and reports
Strong communication skills, with the ability to translate technical findings into business implications
Solid understanding of descriptive statistics and foundational analytical concepts
Experience partnering with cross-functional stakeholders in product, technology, or operations environments
Bachelor’s degree in Data Analytics, Statistics, Business, Information Systems, or a related field, or equivalent practical experience.
Growth Mindset - You are committed to continuously improving your analytical craft. You seek opportunities to deepen your technical expertise while strengthening your ability to influence decisions through insight and storytelling.


Employment eligibility to work with American Express in the United States is required as the company will not pursue visa sponsorship for these positions.

Title: Data Analyst, Data Intelligence
Description:
Austin, TX (Onsite 4 days per week)

Note: This is a full-time role and we do not offer C2C or C2H employment and are not able to sponsor visas for this position.

About Acrisure

Acrisure Enterprise Technology Group is where human expertise meets advanced technology.

A global fintech leader, Acrisure empowers millions of ambitious businesses and individuals with the right solutions to grow boldly forward. Bringing cutting-edge technology and top-tier human support together, we connect clients with customized solutions across a range of insurance, reinsurance, payroll, benefits, cybersecurity, mortgage services – and more.

In the last twelve years, Acrisure has grown in revenue from $38 million to almost $5 billion and employs over 19,000 colleagues in more than 20 countries. Acrisure was built on entrepreneurial spirit. Prioritizing leadership, accountability, and collaboration, we equip our teams to work at the highest levels possible.

The Role

The Data Intelligence team is chartered with advancing data driven decision making across Acrisure. As a member of the Data Intelligence Team, you will be responsible for leading the requirements gathering, design, implementation and delivery of reporting and analytics capabilities for Finance and Accounting Business Functions. You will be comfortable working across a wide variety of people including senior executive leadership, analysts, program management, and technical team members.

Here are some of the ways in which you’ll achieve impact:


Drive the development of efficient and reliable reporting and analytics capabilities with a customer-first mindset.
Advance the transition of users from legacy means of accessing information to the future state strategies of data enablement and self-service.
Create presentation/visualization of data to see beyond the numbers while transforming raw data into actionable insights and recommendations.
Identify, adopt, and evangelize data analysis best practices and technical standards.
Advocate for and identify creative implementations to optimize business impact.
Empower others to grow understanding of the complexity of data by creatively scaling how teams’ knowledge is shared and applied
Help identify better practices, tools, and relevant trends that can improve data quality across the business.
Design and build BI applications with embedded workflows that automate decision points and enable action directly from data — moving teams beyond passive reporting toward active, data-driven operations.


You may be a fit for this role if you have:


5+ years of industry experience as a Data Analyst or Business Intelligence Professional serving the finance and accounting business functions.
A passion for building remarkable things with data.
Demonstrated ability to be a data storyteller and can go deep in building data visualizations and reporting using third party tools (Tableau and PowerBI).
Strong understanding of data models, structures, theories, principles, and practices.
Proficiency working with stakeholders to gather requirements, understand process and translate them to data and create/evaluate associated KPIs.
Experience with SQL, Python, PowerBI, Tableau, dbt, BigQuery, Git, Palantir and data modeling
Demonstrated experience leveraging AI tools for natural language querying, including crafting effective prompts to extract insights from data
Nice to have: Exposure to product management and/or experience building applications or data-driven tools
Experience with agile project execution and business tools such as Jira & ServiceNow
Have excellent communication and leadership skills and can unpack complex problems in order to find solutions.
Ability to understand the business purpose or problem behind a request.
Positive & creative problem solving in the face of data challenges.
Flexibility to successfully multi-task in a fast-paced environment with a positive attitude.
Strong teamwork, solution-centric, and customer-service-oriented attitude.
Possess empathy, kindness towards others, a positive attitude, and self-awareness.
Have a Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Math, Statistics, Business, or related field, or equivalent experience.


Candidates should be comfortable with an on-site presence to support collaboration, team leadership, and cross-functional partnership. 

Benefits and Perks:


Competitive compensation
Generous vacation policy, paid holidays, and paid sick time
Medical Insurance, Dental Insurance, and Vision Insurance (employee-paid)
Company-paid Short-Term and Long-Term Disability Insurance
Company-paid Group Life insurance
Company-paid Employee Assistance Program (EAP) and Calm App subscription
Employee-paid Pet Insurance and optional supplemental insurance coverage
Vested 401(k) with company match and financial wellness programs
Flexible Spending Account (FSA), Health Savings Account (HSA) and commuter benefits options
Paid maternity leave, paid paternity leave, and fertility benefits
Career growth and learning opportunities
…and so much more!


Please note: This list is not reflective of all benefits. Enrollment waiting periods or eligibility criteria may apply to certain benefits. Offerings may vary based on subsidiary entity or geographic location.

Making a lasting impact on the communities it serves, Acrisure has pledged more than $22 million through its partnerships with Corewell Health Helen DeVos Children's Hospital in Grand Rapids, Michigan, UPMC Children's Hospital in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania and Blythedale Children's Hospital in Valhalla, New York.

Acrisure is an Equal Opportunity Employer.

At Acrisure, we firmly believe that an inclusive workforce drives innovation, creativity, and ultimately, our collective success.  

We recruit, hire, employ, train, promote, and compensate individuals based on job-related qualifications and abilities. Acrisure also has a longstanding policy of providing a work environment that respects the dignity and worth of each individual and is free from all forms of employment discrimination. 

Acrisure also provides reasonable accommodation to qualified individuals with disabilities or based on a sincerely held religious belief, in accordance with applicable laws. If you need to inquire about an accommodation, or need assistance with completing the application process, please email leaves@acrisure.com. 

California residents can learn more about our privacy practices for applicants by visiting the Acrisure California Applicant Privacy Policy available at www.Acrisure.com/privacy/caapplicant.

Final candidates will be required to complete post-offer verification processes related to the role and in accordance with applicable laws.

At Acrisure, we’re building more than a business, we’re building a community where people can grow, thrive, and make an impact. Our benefits are designed to support every dimension of your life, from your health and finances to your family and future.

We also offer our employees a comprehensive suite of benefits and perks, including:


Physical Wellness: Comprehensive medical insurance, dental insurance, and vision insurance; life and disability insurance; fertility benefits; wellness resources; and paid sick time.

  • 5+ years of industry experience as a Data Analyst or Business Intelligence Professional serving the finance and accounting business functions.
  • A passion for building remarkable things with data.
  • Demonstrated ability to be a data storyteller and can go deep in building data visualizations and reporting using third party tools (Tableau and PowerBI).
  • Strong understanding of data models, structures, theories, principles, and practices.
  • Proficiency working with stakeholders to gather requirements, understand process and translate them to data and create/evaluate associated KPIs.
  • Experience with SQL, Python, PowerBI, Tableau, dbt, BigQuery, Git, Palantir and data modeling
  • Demonstrated experience leveraging AI tools for natural language querying, including crafting effective prompts to extract insights from data

  • Have excellent communication and leadership skills and can unpack complex problems in order to find solutions.

  • Flexibility to successfully multi-task in a fast-paced environment with a positive attitude.
  • Strong teamwork, solution-centric, and customer-service-oriented attitude.
  • Possess empathy, kindness towards others, a positive attitude, and self-awareness.
  • Have a Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Math, Statistics, Business, or related field, or equivalent experience.

Title: Junior Data Analyst
Description:
Job Summary

We are seeking a detail-oriented and curious Junior Data Analyst to join our team. In this role, you will support data collection, analysis, and reporting efforts to help drive informed business decisions. This is an excellent opportunity for an entry-level professional to develop technical and analytical skills in a data-driven environment.




Key Responsibilities

Collect, clean, and validate data from multiple sources
Perform basic data analysis to identify trends, patterns, and insights
Assist in creating dashboards and reports using tools like Excel, Power BI, or Tableau
Write and maintain SQL queries to extract and manipulate data
Support senior analysts with ad hoc analysis and reporting requests
Ensure data accuracy and integrity across databases and reports
Document processes, data definitions, and analysis methodologies
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to understand data needs




Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree in Data Analytics, Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, or a related field
0–2 years of experience in data analysis or a related role (internships count)
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
Proficiency in Microsoft Excel (formulas, pivot tables, charts)
Basic knowledge of SQL
Familiarity with data visualization tools (Power BI, Tableau, etc.)
Strong attention to detail and organizational skills
Good communication skills to present findings clearly




Preferred Skills

Experience with programming languages such as Python or R
Understanding of data modeling and database concepts
Familiarity with ETL processes and data pipelines
Knowledge of basic statistics and data interpretation techniques

Title: Quantitative Data Analyst
Description:
Job Description

Lazard is one of the world’s preeminent financial advisory and asset management firms. Our people and culture make the difference. While global in presence and reach, ours is a close, collaborativecommunity of just over 3,000 professionals. Lazard is a place of continuous knowledge sharing, skill development and relationship building, where professionals grow and succeed together. Our entrepreneurial culture, flat structure and embrace of individual differences, allow creative ideas, original concepts, and unique perspectives to drive our business forward — and for careers to take flight.

The Advantage Quantitative Equity team is hiring a Quantitative Data Analyst to take ownership of the quality, reliability, and usability of the quantitative datasets that power our research and production investment workflows. These datasets are the direct inputs to factor models, risk models, alpha signals, backtests, and live portfolio construction - data quality here has real investment consequences.

We'll Trust You To


Become a domain owner for key quant datasets (e.g., market data, fundamentals, corporate actions, identifiers/reference data) and develop a detailed understanding of their structure, lineage, known quirks, and intended use in research and production workflows
Onboard new datasets end-to-end: profiling, schema/coverage validation, identifier mapping, cross-source reconciliation, documentation, and support for productionization
Build and maintain automated data validation and monitoring processes (completeness, timeliness, duplication, outliers, stale/missing series, mapping breaks), along with clear quality metrics and dashboards - implemented in code, not spreadsheets
Investigate data anomalies impacting research or production output: triage, isolate root cause, quantify impact, coordinate remediation, and write the code that prevents recurrence
Write Python scripts, pipelines, and utilities (using pandas, NumPy, and related libraries) to automate validation, onboarding, reconciliation, and monitoring workflows; collaborate with quant developers to harden and operationalize your solutions
Maintain high-quality dataset documentation and operational runbooks (definitions, assumptions, known quirks, troubleshooting guidance), improving consistency and conventions across the data ecosystem
Maintain datasets over time through routine checks, backfills, and improvements as vendor definitions, schemas, and business requirements evolve
Engage constructively with internal teams and external vendors when addressing data issues or evaluating new sources


You’ll Need To Have


Bachelor’s degree in a quantitative discipline (e.g., Statistics, Mathematics, Economics, Finance, Computer Science) or equivalent practical experience
Hands-on experience working with quantitative financial datasets - e.g., prices/returns, fundamentals, corporate actions, security master / reference data, factor data, risk model inputs - from vendors such as Bloomberg, Refinitiv/LSEG, Compustat, FactSet, or ICE
Solid understanding of common time-series data quality challenges in a systematic investment context: staleness, point-in-time correctness, survivorship bias, partial trading days, identifier changes (CUSIP/ISIN/ticker), and corporate action adjustments
Experience working with vendor datasets; comfort reconciling across sources and managing schema/definition changes over time
Strong SQL skills: ability to write and optimize queries to validate, reconcile, and investigate issues across large analytical datasets
Strong Python skills: able to write clean, maintainable scripts, pipelines, and reusable utilities independently; comfortable with pandas, NumPy, file I/O, and scheduling
Strong analytical and debugging mindset; able to diagnose data inconsistencies systematically and drive fixes through to completion
Strong communication and collaboration skills; effective in small, close-knit teams with direct stakeholder interaction


It’s a Bonus To Have


Experience at a quantitative asset manager, systematic hedge fund, or similar investment data environment
Experience supporting production data pipelines and incident workflows (monitoring, alerts, runbooks, operational readiness)
Familiarity with modern data warehouses (e.g., Snowflake) and/or analytical engines (e.g., DuckDB, Polars)
Cloud experience, preferably Azure


What We Offer

We strive to enhance the total health and well-being of our employees through comprehensive, competitive benefits. Our goal is to offer a highly individualized employee experience that enables you to balance your commitments to career, family, and community. When you work for Lazard, you are working for an organization that care about your unique talents and passions, and will continue to invest in the development of your career.

We expect the base salary range for this role to be approximately $90,000 - $150,000 USD. Various factors contribute to determining the actual base compensation offered, including but not limited to the applicant’s years of relevant experience, career tenure, qualifications, level of education attained, certifications or other professional licenses held, and relevant skills for the role. Base salary is one component of Lazard’s compensation package, which also includes comprehensive benefits and may include incentive compensation.

Does this sound like you?

Apply! We’ll get in touch and let you know the next steps.

Representation at Lazard

Lazard is an intellectual capital business committed to delivering the best advice and solutions to clients. To achieve these objectives, we focus on attracting, developing and retaining the best talent. We believe that a workforce comprised of people who represent a wide array of backgrounds, experiences and perspectives creates a rich variety of thought that empowers us to challenge conventional wisdom, solve problems creatively and make better decisions.

Lazard was built on the premise that a multicultural firm can best serve a global clientele. As a global firm that has grown organically from local roots in different countries, we have a deep tradition of respecting and appreciating individual differences. Doing so has been core to our success for over 175 years. We are committed to sustaining an environment where every colleague is supported in their professional pursuits, can maximize their individual potential and contribute to our collective success.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Job Title: Data Analyst

Location: Texas, United States

Experience: 0–3 Years

Employment Type: Full-Time







Job Summary

We are seeking a detail-oriented and analytical Data Analyst to join our team in Texas. The ideal candidate will be responsible for collecting, cleaning, analyzing, and interpreting data to support business decisions. This role is suitable for recent graduates and professionals with up to 3 years of experience in data analysis, reporting, or business intelligence.







Key Responsibilities

Collect, organize, clean, and validate data from multiple sources.
Analyze business data to identify trends, patterns, and insights.
Create reports, dashboards, and visualizations for internal stakeholders.
Work with business teams to understand reporting requirements and provide data-driven recommendations.
Develop and maintain Excel reports, SQL queries, and data dashboards.
Support data quality checks and resolve data-related issues.
Prepare weekly, monthly, and ad hoc reports for management.
Assist in improving reporting processes and automation opportunities.
Document data sources, reporting logic, and analysis procedures.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams including operations, finance, marketing, and IT.







Required Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree in Data Analytics, Computer Science, Information Systems, Statistics, Mathematics, Business Analytics, Finance, or a related field.
0–3 years of experience in data analysis, reporting, business intelligence, or a related role.
Strong knowledge of Microsoft Excel, including Pivot Tables, VLOOKUP/XLOOKUP, formulas, and charts.
Basic to intermediate knowledge of SQL for querying and analyzing data.
Familiarity with data visualization tools such as Power BI, Tableau, or Looker.
Understanding of data cleaning, data validation, and reporting processes.
Strong analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills.
Ability to work independently and manage multiple tasks in a fast-paced environment.







Preferred Qualifications

Experience with Python or R for data analysis is a plus.
Familiarity with databases such as SQL Server, MySQL, Oracle, or PostgreSQL.
Knowledge of ETL processes and data warehousing concepts is preferred.
Experience working with CRM, ERP, or business reporting systems is a plus.
Internship, academic project, or hands-on experience in data analytics is preferred for entry-level candidates.

Power BI or Tableau
Data Visualization
Data Cleaning and Validation
Reporting and Dashboard Development
Problem Solving

Attention to Detail

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
About Us:

As a company, we promote a culture of honesty and integrity, and value the trust it allows us to build with customers and employees alike. Our focus on our people is what sets us apart and keeps our customers coming back to work with us!

If you are seeking to be a part of a family, this is the place for you!

Maersk Logistics & Services USA, Inc. handles end-to-end warehousing and distribution logistics needs utilizing our strategic network of storage facilities. Our facilities are capable of receiving, storing, processing and dispatching cargo, so we can build flexibility and resilience into the supply chain process. Our integrated solutions allow us to be prepared for supply chain risks, so we can plan distribution to better achieve lead time and goals despite unexpected conditions. Our extensive experience, connections, and deep expertise across different sectors, industries, and transportation modes, means we can be counted on for process excellence that will save time and money.

Summary

The Data Analyst supports warehouse operations by transforming operational data into actionable insights that improve productivity, quality, labor utilization, and customer service. This role partners closely with operations leadership to identify trends, develop reporting, and drive data-informed decision-making across a fast-paced distribution environment.

The ideal candidate combines strong analytical skills with the ability to communicate findings to business leaders and develop scalable reporting solutions.

Key Responsibilities:


Analyze operational performance data to identify trends, opportunities, and root causes.
Design, develop, and maintain dashboards, scorecards, and recurring reports using business intelligence tools.
Monitor key operational metrics including productivity, quality, throughput, inventory accuracy, labor utilization, and service performance.
Partner with operations managers to support labor planning, staffing analysis, and capacity forecasting.
Conduct ad hoc analyses to support business initiatives and operational improvements.
Translate complex datasets into clear recommendations for frontline leadership and senior management.
Validate data accuracy and investigate discrepancies across multiple systems.
Automate recurring reporting processes to improve efficiency and reduce manual effort.
Support continuous improvement initiatives through statistical analysis and performance measurement.
Present findings and recommendations to cross-functional stakeholders.


Qualifications:


1-3 years of experience in data analytics, business intelligence, supply chain, manufacturing, or distribution operations.
Advanced Microsoft Excel skills include Pivot Tables, Power Query, Power Pivot, and complex formulas.
Experience writing SQL queries to extract and analyze data.
Experience with Power BI, Tableau, or similar business intelligence platforms.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced operational environment.


Company Benefits:


Medical
Dental
Vision
401k + Company Match
Employee Assistance Program
Paid Time Off
Flexible Work Schedules (when possible)
And more!


Pay Range:

$65,000-$72,000*


The above-stated pay range is the anticipated starting salary range for the position. The Company may adjust this range in light of prevailing market conditions and other factors such as location. The Company will work directly with the selected candidate(s) on the final starting salary in accordance with all applicable laws.


#INDEED

You must be authorized to work for any employer in the U.S.

Performance Team is committed to a diverse and inclusive workplace, and we embrace different styles of thinking. Performance Team is an equal opportunities employer and welcomes applicants without regard to race, color, gender, sex, age, religion, creed, national origin, ancestry, citizenship, marital status, sexual orientation, physical or mental disability, medical condition, pregnancy or parental leave, veteran status, gender identity, genetic information, or any other characteristic protected by applicable law. We will consider qualified applicants with criminal histories in a manner consistent with all legal requirements.

 

We are happy to support your need for any adjustments during the application and hiring process. If you need special assistance or accommodation to use our website, apply for a position, or perform a job, please email us at accommodationrequests@maersk.com

Key Responsibilities:

  • Analyze operational performance data to identify trends, opportunities, and root causes.
  • Design, develop, and maintain dashboards, scorecards, and recurring reports using business intelligence tools.
  • Monitor key operational metrics including productivity, quality, throughput, inventory accuracy, labor utilization, and service performance.
  • Partner with operations managers to support labor planning, staffing analysis, and capacity forecasting.
  • Conduct ad hoc analyses to support business initiatives and operational improvements.
  • Translate complex datasets into clear recommendations for frontline leadership and senior management.
  • Validate data accuracy and investigate discrepancies across multiple systems.
  • Automate recurring reporting processes to improve efficiency and reduce manual effort.
  • Support continuous improvement initiatives through statistical analysis and performance measurement.
  • Present findings and recommendations to cross-functional stakeholders.

Qualifications:

  • 1-3 years of experience in data analytics, business intelligence, supply chain, manufacturing, or distribution operations.
  • Advanced Microsoft Excel skills include Pivot Tables, Power Query, Power Pivot, and complex formulas.
  • Experience writing SQL queries to extract and analyze data.
  • Experience with Power BI, Tableau, or similar business intelligence platforms.
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
  • Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced operational environment.

Company Benefits:

  • Medical
  • Dental
  • Vision
  • 401k + Company Match
  • Employee Assistance Program
  • Paid Time Off
  • Flexible Work Schedules (when possible)
  • And more!

Pay Range:

$65,000-$72,000*

  • The above-stated pay range is the anticipated starting salary range for the position. The Company may adjust this range in light of prevailing market conditions and other factors such as location. The Company will work directly with the selected candidate(s) on the final starting salary in accordance with all applicable laws.

#INDEED

You must be authorized to work for any employer in the U.S.

Performance Team is committed to a diverse and inclusive workplace, and we embrace different styles of thinking. Performance Team is an equal opportunities employer and welcomes applicants without regard to race, color, gender, sex, age, religion, creed, national origin, ancestry, citizenship, marital status, sexual orientation, physical or mental disability, medical condition, pregnancy or parental leave, veteran status, gender identity, genetic information, or any other characteristic protected by applicable law. We will consider qualified applicants with criminal histories in a manner consistent with all legal requirements.

 

We are happy to support your need for any adjustments during the application and hiring process. If you need special assistance or accommodation to use our website, apply for a position, or perform a job, please email us at accommodationrequests@maersk.com

Title: Data Analyst Junior
Description:
Data Analyst Junior




Habilidades Requeridas:

Entre 1 y 2 años de experiencia como Data Analyst
Experiencia en Análisis de Datos
Dominio de SQL Server
Dominio de Power BI / Looker
Dominio Avanzado de Excel (tablas dinámicas, buscarv, macros básicas)
Deseable: Domino de Python para análisis de datos (NumPy, Pandas)




Responsabilidades:

Recopilar, depurar y analizar información de negocio proveniente de diversas fuentes relacionadas con el sector asegurador para apoyar la toma de decisiones estratégicas y operativas.
Realizar consultas básicas e intermedias en bases de datos mediante SQL, asegurando la correcta extracción, validación y transformación de la información requerida por las áreas de negocio y tecnología.
Desarrollar y mantener reportes, Dashboards e indicadores de desempeño (KPIs) utilizando herramientas de visualización como Power BI y Looker Studio, facilitando el seguimiento de métricas relevantes para la organización.
Analizar datos operativos y de negocio identificando tendencias, oportunidades de mejora, riesgos potenciales y comportamientos relevantes para las distintas áreas involucradas.
Colaborar con equipos de Arquitectura, Desarrollo y Negocio para comprender requerimientos de información, traducir necesidades de análisis en soluciones de datos y asegurar la calidad de los entregables.
Preparar y transformar datos mediante Excel y Python, optimizando procesos de análisis, depuración y generación de reportes.
Dar seguimiento a requerimientos de información y análisis ad hoc, atendiendo solicitudes de diferentes áreas y proponiendo mejoras basadas en evidencia.
Apoyar en la automatización de procesos de análisis y generación de reportes mediante el uso de herramientas de análisis de datos y scripting básico en Python.




Idiomas Requeridos

Inglés: Intermedio (deseable)




Modalidad de trabajo: Híbrida en CDMX (3 días a la semana en oficina y 2 HO)




Acerca de Softtek: Fundada en 1982, Softtek es un proveedor global de servicios orientados a procesos de TI con 30 oficinas en Norteamérica, Latinoamérica, Europa y Asia. Con 15 Centros de Desarrollo Global en EE.UU., México, China, Brasil, Argentina, Costa Rica, España, Hungría e India. Softtek mejora resultados para grandes empresas en más de 20 países. Softtek es el creador y líder de la industria nearshore. Para más información de lo que hacemos, y de nuestras Oportunidades de carreras, visita www.softtek.com / Síguenos en @Softtek en Twitter, y únete a nuestra FanPage en www.facebook.com/softtek

Title: Data Analyst II
Description:
Job Description

Data Analyst II

We are Lennar

Lennar is one of the nation's leading homebuilders, dedicated to making an impact and creating an extraordinary experience for their Homeowners, Communities, and Associates by building quality homes and providing exceptional customer service, giving back to the communities in which we work and live in, and fostering a culture of opportunity and growth for our Associates throughout their career. Lennar has been recognized as a Fortune 500® company and consistently ranked among the top homebuilders in the United States.

Join a Company that Empowers you to Build your Future

A Data Analyst II is a developing professional with a solid foundation in data analysis and a growing ability to work independently on analytical projects. This role involves executing data analysis work, building and maintaining dashboards and reports, and collaborating with stakeholders to translate business questions into data-driven insights.



A career with purpose.
A career built on making dreams come true.
A career built on building zero defect homes, cost management, and adherence to schedules.



Role Overview



Mid-level position with growing independence
Executes analytical projects with general direction from senior team members.
Proficient in SQL and Tableau with a drive to deepen technical skills



Your Responsibilities On The Team



Analyze business problems and opportunities, providing data-driven findings and recommendations.
Gather and document analytics and reporting requirements in collaboration with stakeholders.
Contribute to the design, creation, maintenance, and rollout of dashboards, reports, and data tools.
Collaborate with stakeholders across teams to understand data needs and communicate findings clearly.
Perform ad-hoc analyses to find answers to business performance questions.
Prepare and present clear reports and analytical summaries for team and stakeholder review.



Requirements



Strong SQL skills with demonstrated ability to write complex queries independently.
Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Business Administration, or a related field. (Master’s degree beneficial).
Minimum 3-4 years of experience in data analysis, business intelligence, or a related role.
Confident with learning new tools, technologies, and methodologies.
Familiarity with data transformation tools such as dbt (a plus, not required).
Proficient in Tableau or similar data visualization tools with hands-on dashboard development experience.
Experience with marketing and sales analytics (media and attribution experience a plus).
Strong communication, interpersonal, and presentation skills.
Curious, detail-oriented, and eager to grow technical and analytical skills over time.



Physical & Office/Site Presence Requirements:

This is primarily a sedentary office position which requires the Data Analyst II to have the ability to operate computer equipment, speak, hear, bend, stoop, reach, lift, and move and carry up to 25 lbs. Finger dexterity is necessary.

This is a position which requires the Data Analyst II frequent walking, standing and climbing stairs in/around homes, models and properties. Must also have the ability to operate computer

equipment, speak, hear, bend, stoop, reach, lift, and move and carry up to 25 lbs. Finger dexterity is necessary.



This information is intended to be a general overview and may be modified by the company due to factors affecting the business.



General Overview of Compensation & Benefits:

We reasonably expect the base compensation offered for this position to range from an annual salary of $95,400.00 - $119,200, subject to adjustment based on business-related factors such as employee qualifications, geographic pay differentials (e.g., cost of labor/living, etc.), and operational considerations.



This position may be eligible for bonuses.
This position may be eligible for commissions.
This position will be eligible for the described benefits listed in the above section in accordance with Company Policy.
This information is intended to be a general overview and may be modified by the Company due to factors affecting the business.



Life at Lennar

At Lennar, we are committed to fostering a supportive and enriching environment for our Associates, offering a comprehensive array of benefits designed to enhance their well-being and professional growth. Our Associates have access to robust health insurance plans, including Medical, Dental, and Vision coverage, ensuring their health needs are well taken care of. Our 401(k) Retirement Plan, complete with a $1 for $1 Company Match up to 5%, helps secure their financial future, while Paid Parental Leave and an Associate Assistance Plan provide essential support during life's critical moments. To further support our Associates, we provide an Education Assistance Program and up to $30,000 in Adoption Assistance, underscoring our commitment to their diverse needs and aspirations. From the moment of hire, they can enjoy up to three weeks of vacation annually, alongside generous Holiday, Sick Leave, and Personal Day policies. Additionally, we offer a New Hire Referral Bonus Program, significant Home Purchase Discounts, and unique opportunities such as the Everyone’s Included Day. At Lennar, we believe in investing in our Associates, empowering them to thrive both personally and professionally. Lennar Associates will have access to these benefits as outlined by Lennar’s policies and applicable plan terms. Visit Lennartotalrewards.com to view our suite of benefits.

Join the fun and follow us on social media to see what's happening at our company, and don't forget to connect with us on Lennar: Overview | LinkedIn<https://www.linkedin.com/company/lennar/> for the latest job opportunities.

Lennar is an equal opportunity employer and complies with all applicable federal, state, and local fair employment practices laws.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
We are

At Synechron, we believe in the power of digital to transform businesses for the better. Our global consulting firm combines creativity and innovative technology to deliver industry-leading digital solutions. Synechron’s progressive technologies and optimization strategies span end-to-end Artificial Intelligence, Consulting, Digital, Cloud & DevOps, Data, and Software Engineering, servicing an array of noteworthy financial services and technology firms. Through research and development initiatives in our FinLabs we develop solutions for modernization, from Artificial Intelligence and Blockchain to Data Science models, Digital Underwriting, mobile-first applications and more. Over the last 20+ years, our company has been honored with multiple employer awards, recognizing our commitment to our talented teams. With top clients to boast about, Synechron has a global workforce of 16,850+, and has 60 offices in 20 countries within key global markets.







Our challenge

We are seeking a skilled Data Analyst with a strong foundation in SQL development and hands-on experience in data analysis, reporting, and dashboarding to support our Investment Banking (IB) Reporting team, primarily working on data migration and reconciliation projects




Additional Information*

The base salary for this position will vary based on geography and other factors. In accordance with law, the base salary for this role if filled within New York City, NY is $140k - $145k/year & benefits (see below).







The Role

Responsibilities:

Write complex SQL queries, stored procedures, views, and performance-tuned scripts.
Extract, transform, and validate data from multiple sources.
Develop and maintain dashboards and reports using Power BI, Tableau, or similar BI tools.
Work on migration from legacy reporting platforms to modern systems.
Perform data reconciliation and ensure data accuracy.
Support dashboarding and reporting needs.
Drive improvements through automation and efficiency initiatives.
Relevant experience in Investment Banking / Financial Services is preferred.




Requirements:

Strong data understanding and analysis skills.
Experience in BI reporting and analytics (Power BI, Cognos, etc.).
Hands-on experience in data migration and reconciliation.
Good knowledge of SQL and data validation.
Exposure to automation initiatives / process improvements.
Strong communication skills and a proactive approach.




Preferred, but not required:

Experience with Python (Pandas, NumPy) for data analysis.
Exposure to ETL tools and data pipelines.







We offer:

A highly competitive compensation and benefits package.
A multinational organization with 60 offices in 20 countries and the possibility to work abroad.
10 days of paid annual leave (plus sick leave and national holidays).
Maternity & paternity leave plans.
A comprehensive insurance plan including medical, dental, vision, life insurance, and long-/short-term disability (plans vary by region).
Retirement savings plans.
A higher education certification policy.
Commuter benefits (varies by region).
Extensive training opportunities, focused on skills, substantive knowledge, and personal development.
On-demand Udemy for Business for all Synechron employees with free access to more than 5000 curated courses.
Coaching opportunities with experienced colleagues from our Financial Innovation Labs (FinLabs) and Center of Excellences (CoE) groups.
Cutting edge projects at the world’s leading tier-one banks, financial institutions and insurance firms.
A flat and approachable organization.
A truly diverse, fun-loving, and global work culture.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Job Title:- Data Analyst

Location:- Remote USA




Job Description

Develop, optimize, and maintain complex SQL queries, stored procedures, views, and ETL processes to extract, transform, and analyze large banking datasets.
Design and develop interactive dashboards and reports using Power BI and Tableau to provide actionable insights on business performance, customer behavior, and operational metrics.
Analyze retail banking, commercial banking, lending, deposits, payments, and transaction data to identify trends, risks, and business opportunities.
Collaborate with business stakeholders, risk teams, finance, and operations to gather reporting requirements and translate them into analytical solutions.
Perform data validation, reconciliation, and quality checks to ensure accuracy, consistency, and integrity of financial data.
Create automated reporting solutions that improve operational efficiency and reduce manual reporting efforts.
Conduct ad hoc analysis to support strategic decision-making, regulatory reporting, and executive management requests.
Utilized Generative AI tools to enhance data analysis, SQL query optimization, report generation, and documentation, improving productivity and decision-making.
Work with large relational databases such as SQL Server, Oracle, MySQL, or PostgreSQL to support enterprise reporting and analytics.
Partner with cross-functional teams to identify process improvement opportunities and implement data-driven solutions.
Prepare executive-level reports and presentations summarizing analytical findings and business recommendations.




Required Skills

Strong proficiency in SQL (complex joins, CTEs, window functions, stored procedures, query optimization).
Hands-on experience with Power BI (DAX, Power Query, data modeling, dashboard development).
Experience developing interactive dashboards using Tableau.
Knowledge of banking products, financial transactions, customer analytics, and regulatory reporting.
Strong analytical, problem-solving, and data visualization skills.
Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
About Us:

American Technology Consulting (ATC) is a service-first tech company dedicated to helping businesses unlock their full potential through innovative technology solutions. We empower organizations to leverage data for smarter decisions and meaningful impact. At ATC, we foster a culture of growth and invite passionate individuals to join our team, contribute to impactful projects, and excel as data-driven problem solvers. We embrace diversity and welcome candidates from all educational and professional backgrounds, including recent Master’s graduates without prior technical experience.




Job Overview:

As an Entry-Level Data Analyst at ATC, you will be at the forefront of turning raw data into actionable insights. This role offers a fantastic opportunity to work on real-world challenges, collaborate with cross-functional teams, and apply your problem-solving skills in a supportive and innovative environment.




Key Responsibilities:

Collect, clean, and organize data to prepare it for analysis.
Identify trends, patterns, and correlations using statistical and analytical tools.
Develop insightful dashboards, visualizations, and reports using tools like Tableau, Power BI, or Excel.
Assist teams in making data-driven decisions by presenting clear and concise insights.
Collaborate with stakeholders to understand data requirements and deliver solutions.
Ensure data accuracy and consistency through validation and quality checks.
Stay updated on industry trends, best practices, and new data tools or technologies.




Qualifications:

Master’s degree.
Strong analytical and critical thinking skills.
Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and a willingness to learn data visualization tools like Tableau or Power BI.
Excellent communication skills to present findings to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Attention to detail and ability to manage multiple priorities effectively.
Candidates must have U.S. work authorization.




Preferred Qualifications:

Internship or project experience in data analysis or visualization.
Familiarity with SQL for querying and managing databases.
Knowledge of statistical concepts and tools like Python or R (a plus but not required).




What We Offer:

Career Growth: Opportunity to advance in a dynamic and supportive work environment.
Mentorship: Guidance and training from experienced professionals in the field.
Diversity in Projects: Work across various industries and innovative technologies.
Comprehensive Benefits: Competitive salary, health insurance, and other perks.




Work Authorization Support:

H1B sponsorship or related visa support for eligible candidates.
Applications from individuals on F1/OPT are welcome.




Equal Opportunity Commitment:

ATC is proud to be an equal-opportunity employer, fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace where all employees can thrive.

This role offers a fantastic opportunity to work on real-world challenges, collaborate with cross-functional teams, and apply your problem-solving skills in a supportive and innovative environment.


Key Responsibilities:

  • Collect, clean, and organize data to prepare it for analysis.
  • Identify trends, patterns, and correlations using statistical and analytical tools.
  • Develop insightful dashboards, visualizations, and reports using tools like Tableau, Power BI, or Excel.
  • Assist teams in making data-driven decisions by presenting clear and concise insights.
  • Collaborate with stakeholders to understand data requirements and deliver solutions.
  • Ensure data accuracy and consistency through validation and quality checks.
  • Stay updated on industry trends, best practices, and new data tools or technologies.


Qualifications:

  • Master’s degree.
  • Strong analytical and critical thinking skills.
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and a willingness to learn data visualization tools like Tableau or Power BI.
  • Excellent communication skills to present findings to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
  • Attention to detail and ability to manage multiple priorities effectively.
  • Candidates must have U.S. work authorization.


Preferred Qualifications:

  • Internship or project experience in data analysis or visualization.
  • Familiarity with SQL for querying and managing databases.
  • Knowledge of statistical concepts and tools like Python or R (a plus but not required).


What We Offer:

  • Career Growth: Opportunity to advance in a dynamic and supportive work environment.
  • Mentorship: Guidance and training from experienced professionals in the field.
  • Diversity in Projects: Work across various industries and innovative technologies.
  • Comprehensive Benefits: Competitive salary, health insurance, and other perks.


Work Authorization Support:

  • H1B sponsorship or related visa support for eligible candidates.
  • Applications from individuals on F1/OPT are welcome.

ATC is proud to be an equal-opportunity employer, fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace where all employees can thrive.

Title: Data Analyst, Sr. (Healthcare)
Description:
About The Company

Sentara Healthcare is a leading integrated healthcare organization dedicated to providing exceptional patient care, advancing health research, and fostering community well-being. With a rich history of innovation and a commitment to excellence, Sentara operates a network of hospitals, outpatient facilities, and research centers across multiple states. The organization emphasizes diversity, inclusion, and continuous professional development, creating an environment where employees can thrive and make meaningful contributions to healthcare. Sentara's mission to improve health every day is reflected in its comprehensive services, cutting-edge research initiatives, and a culture that values collaboration, integrity, and compassion.

About The Role

The Senior Healthcare Data Analyst is a vital member of the Research Analytics and Data Literacy (RAD) team within Sentara Enterprise Analytics. This fully remote position supports Sentara's research initiatives in partnership with the Sentara Health Research Center (SHRC). The role focuses on retrospective clinical and population health research, providing end-to-end analytic support from study conception to manuscript development and dissemination. The Senior Analyst collaborates closely with physician investigators, clinical leaders, and research stakeholders to assess project feasibility, extract and curate data from various sources such as Epic and Databricks, and perform advanced statistical analyses. This position requires a strong foundation in healthcare data, research methodology, and analytics, combined with a service-oriented mindset to facilitate impactful research outputs that advance healthcare knowledge and practice.

Qualifications


Bachelor's degree in public health, epidemiology, biostatistics, or related field (Required)
Master's Degree in a related field (Preferred)
A minimum of 3 years of healthcare analytics or research experience
Proficiency in SQL; experience with Excel, Databricks, Python, or R preferred
Knowledge of Electronic Health Records (EHR) data, clinical coding, and research methods
Experience with REDCap, IRB processes, and manuscript support is advantageous
Background in academic medicine or health system research is preferred


Responsibilities


Assess research feasibility and translate clinical questions into detailed analytic plans
Extract, validate, and manage data from Epic, Databricks, and other sources
Perform data cleaning, quality assurance, and statistical analyses to ensure accuracy and reliability
Support Institutional Review Board (IRB) submissions and ensure compliance with data governance standards
Generate tables, figures, and visualizations to support manuscripts and presentations
Collaborate with investigators on data interpretation, abstract development, and dissemination of research findings


Benefits


Comprehensive medical, dental, and vision insurance plans
Reimbursement for adoption, fertility, and surrogacy expenses up to $10,000
Paid time off and sick leave to promote work-life balance
Paid parental and family caregiver leave
Emergency backup care services
Long-term and short-term disability insurance, along with critical illness plans
Life insurance coverage
401(k)/403(b) retirement plans with employer matching contributions
Tuition assistance up to $5,250 annually and access to discounted educational programs through Guild Education
Student debt repayment support of up to $10,000
Reimbursement for professional certifications and free access to continuing education units (CEUs)
Pet insurance and legal resources plans
Opportunity to earn annual discretionary bonuses based on system performance and eligibility


Equal Opportunity

Sentara Healthcare is an equal opportunity employer committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive workforce. We celebrate the unique backgrounds, perspectives, and experiences of our employees and are dedicated to creating an environment where everyone can thrive. We do not discriminate based on race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, disability, or any other protected status. Our organization values equity and belonging, ensuring all team members are respected and supported in their professional journey. In support of our mission to improve health every day, Sentara maintains a tobacco-free environment.

Title: BI Engineer I
Description:
Opportunity

Job Description:

We are seeking an entry level Business Intelligence Engineer to join our dynamic team. As a key member of our Customer Care data analytics department, you will play a pivotal role in supporting our data-driven decision-making. This role is perfect for someone who loves working with SQL, Python, and modern visualization tools, and who wants to grow their skills in streaming data, automation, and cloud-based analytics. You'll work in a fastmoving, data-driven environment where you’ll help build scalable insights solutions, optimize reporting, and contribute to our mission of making data-driven decisions easier and more accessible for everyone.

Responsibilities

Develop and optimize queries to extract and analyze data from large-scale databases.

Work with real-time and batch data processing to support operational reporting and alerting.

Build dynamic dashboards and self-service analytics solutions using modern visualization tools (Tableau, Streamlit, Plotly, etc.).

Automate data collection and reporting workflows, reducing manual effort for the business. Assist in designing data pipelines for customer care analytics, ensuring data is structured for ease of use and scalability. Collaborate with cross-functional teams (engineering, operations, product, and customer experience) to identify and solve business problems using data. Implement data validation, monitoring, and quality checks to ensure clean, reliable data for stakeholders.

Stay up to date on emerging trends in BI, data engineering, and cloud computing to improve our analytics stack.

Interest in empowering teams with self-service dashboards and analytics tools.

Ability to automate manual reporting tasks to drive efficiency. Strong communication and collaboration skills—able to work effectively with business and technical teams.

Support senior analysts in conducting data analysis projects.

Learn and apply data analysis techniques and best practices.

Participate in team meetings and contribute to knowledge sharing.

Qualifications

Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, or a related field.

Understanding of SQL and relational databases.

Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.

Excellent communication and collaboration skills.

Eagerness to learn and develop data analysis skills

Nice To Have

Experience with cloud computing platforms (e.g., AWS, Azure, GCP).

Experience with Python

Familiarity with data visualization tools (e.g., Tableau, Power BI, Streamlit, Plotly).

Knowledge of retail and e-commerce. Experience with A/B testing, statistical analysis, or predictive modeling is a bonus.


The specific salary offered to a candidate may be influenced by a variety of factors including but not limited to the candidate’s relevant experience, education, and work location. In addition, this position is eligible for 401k and a new hire and annual equity grant. C08+ positions may also be eligible for annual bonus.


We offer different types of insurance and benefits, such as medical/Rx, vision, dental, life, disability, hospital indemnity, critical illness, and accident. We offer parental leave, family services benefits, backup dependent care, flexible spending accounts, telemedicine, pet adoption reimbursement, employee assistance program, and many discounts including 10% off pet insurance and 20% off at Chewy.com.

Exempt salary team members have unlimited PTO, subject to manager approval. Team members will receive six paid holidays per year. Team members may be eligible for paid sick and family leave in compliance with applicable state and local regulations.

Chewy is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, ancestry, national origin, gender, citizenship, marital status, religion, age, disability, gender identity, results of genetic testing, veteran status, as well as any other legally-protected characteristic. If you have a disability under the Americans with Disabilities Act or similar law, and you need an accommodation during the application process or to perform these job requirements, or if you need a religious accommodation, please contact CAAR@chewy.com.

To access Chewy's California CPRA Job Applicant Privacy Policy, please click here.

Title: Data Engineer(BI Reporting)
Description:
Job Description (Posting)

Job Title

Data Engineer(BI Reporting)

Job Summary

The Project Manager (SAFe Product Owner/Product Manager) is responsible for overseeing projects from initiation through delivery, ensuring that the projects are completed on time, within scope, and within budget. As a SAFe Product Owner/Product Manager, the individual will be responsible for managing and prioritizing the product backlog, representing the customer, and working closely with the development team to ensure successful product delivery.

Key Responsibilities


Lead and manage cross-functional project teams to deliver high-quality projects within the defined constraints.
Develop and maintain project plans, budget tracking, and resource allocation.
Communicate with stakeholders to ensure alignment on project scope, timelines, and deliverables.
Facilitate team meetings, including sprint planning, reviews, retrospectives, and daily standups.
Collaborate with Product Owners and Scrum Masters to prioritize features and resolve dependencies.
Act as a liaison between the business stakeholders and the development team to ensure clear communication and understanding.
Identify and mitigate project risks, proactively resolving issues to keep projects on track.
Monitor project progress and performance metrics, providing regular updates to leadership.


Skill Requirements


Strong understanding of Agile methodology and the SAFe framework.

Title: Business Intelligence & Analytics Specialist
Description:
Company Description

Ramboll is a global architecture, engineering, and consultancy company. As a foundation-owned people company, founded in Denmark, we believe that the purpose of sustainable change is to create a thriving world for both nature and people. So, that’s where we start – and how we work. Our history is rooted in a clear vision of how a responsible company should act and being open and curious is a cornerstone of our culture. Ramboll in the Americas has thousands of experts working across more than 70 offices. Ramboll experts deliver innovative solutions across Environment & Health, Water, Energy, and Planning & Urban Design.

Job Description

This is a hybrid-based position in our Syracuse, NY office.

Applicants must be currently authorized to work in the United States on a full-time basis. No sponsorship is available for this position.

Are you eager to develop your excellence in an inclusive, collaborative, and empowering community? Are you excited about joining a company that strives to make a difference – for its employees, clients, and society?  

We are looking for a hands-on Analyst with strong SQL, VBA, and .NET skills who can turn business performance data into reliable, actionable reporting. The role requires intermediate SQL query expertise (temporary tables, indexes, joins, expressions) and practical experience with SQL Server, SSMS, Stored Procedures and Table-Valued Functions.

Your new role 

Ramboll is seeking a Business Intelligence & Analytics Specialist for an open position in our Syracuse office. The ideal candidate will be working in a challenging and dynamic environment that will include a detail-oriented team and independent work.

Your key responsibilities will be:



SQL Data Sourcing
Extract data from corporate databases.
Analytics & Business Intelligence
Translate complex business requirements from Client, Sales, and Operations teams into actionable KPIs and metrics.
Design, build, and deploy interactive dashboards that empower leadership to make data-driven decisions.
Optimize and support Legacy Applications
Maintain and make functional enhancements to existing Excel-based project management tools utilizing VBA.
Proactively identify opportunities to migrate legacy, manual spreadsheets into automated, centralized BI solutions.
Document tool architecture and logic to ensure business continuity and straightforward system updates.
AI-Driven Process Improvement
Collaborate cross-functionally to identify operational bottlenecks and manual workflows.
Research, test, and implement emerging AI tools to streamline day-to-day operations.

Qualifications

About you



Bachelor's degree in a technical field and 5 years of relevant experience
Intermediate SQL Server skills (temporary tables, indexes, joins, expressions)
Proficiency with SSMS and familiarity with Stored Procedures and Table-Valued Functions
Intermediate experience with Visual Studio 2022 or newer. Working knowledge of VB.Net and ability to edit, compile and distribute existing .NET applications
Experience with VBA (Excel and MS Access) for reporting/automation
Understanding of business practices for project costs and revenue, plus basic knowledge of financial and project management systems
Demonstrated experience or eagerness to become fluent in BI reporting
Understanding of AI prompt engineering and development of AI-enabled applications
Familiarity with Remote Desktop and basic server app management
Comfort navigating large networks and related infrastructure constraints



Additional Information

What we can offer you



Investment in your development
Being valued for the unique person you are
Never being short of inspiration from colleagues, clients, and projects
The long-term thinking of a foundation-owned company



Salary Transparency Statement

At Ramboll, we are committed to fair and transparent compensation practices that support our people and our purpose of creating sustainable societies. The anticipated base salary range for this position is $82,029-$121,505 per year. This range reflects the compensation we reasonably expect to offer at the time of posting. Actual compensation will vary based on factors such as location, skills, experience, education, and internal equity. Because we operate across multiple U.S. markets, pay may differ depending on the cost of labor in specific locations. In addition to base salary, this position may be eligible for bonus, overtime or other forms of compensation and we offer a comprehensive benefits package that supports well-being, professional development, and work-life balance. Our recruiters are happy to share more about the specific salary range and total rewards package applicable to your location during the hiring process.

Ready to join us?

Please submit your application with your up-to-date CV. We invite diversity in all its forms and encourage applicants from all groups to apply.

Thank you for taking the time to apply! We look forward to receiving your application.

Work at the heart of sustainable change with Ramboll in the Americas

Ramboll is a global architecture, engineering, and consultancy company. As a foundation-owned people company, founded in Denmark, we believe that the purpose of sustainable change is to create a thriving world for both nature and people. So, that’s where we start – and how we work. Our history is rooted in a clear vision of how a responsible company should act and being open and curious is a cornerstone of our culture. Ramboll in the Americas has thousands of experts working across more than 70 offices. Ramboll experts deliver innovative solutions across Environment & Health, Water, Energy, and Planning & Urban Design.

Where People Flourish

Our mission is to create sustainable societies where people and nature flourish. This means that a culture of inclusion is embedded in everything we do. Our people bring diverse backgrounds and experiences to the company, enabling us to deliver innovative and forward-thinking solutions to our clients. We also know how important it is to achieve the right balance of where, when, and how much you work. At Ramboll, we offer flexibility as part of our positive and inclusive approach to work. We are committed to equal employment opportunity, regardless of age, disability or medical condition, gender identity, marriage and domestic partnership, pregnancy and maternity, race, ancestry, or national origin, religion or belief, sex and sexual orientation, military service and veteran status, or any other protected characteristic. Ramboll wants to ensure opportunities are accessible to candidates with disabilities. So, please let us know if there are any changes we could make to the application process to make it more comfortable for you. You can contact us at job.advert.accessibility@ramboll.com with such requests.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Why USAA?

At USAA, our mission is to empower our members to achieve financial security through highly competitive products, exceptional service and trusted advice. We seek to be the #1 choice for the military community and their families.




Embrace a fulfilling career at USAA, where our core values - honesty, integrity, loyalty and service - define how we treat each other and our members. Be part of what truly makes us special and impactful.




We are proud to support active-duty military spouses. USAA roles may offer remote or hybrid flexibility for active-duty military spouses consistent with applicable policy and business needs.




The Opportunity

This role is remote eligible in the continental U.S. with occasional business travel. However, individuals residing within 60 mile radius of a USAA office will be expected to work on-site 4 days per week.




We have multiple exciting opportunities available on our Modeling Factory and Insights & Development teams. As a dedicated Actuarial Analyst I, you will be a near-credentialed analyst responsible for independently applying complex technical and actuarial methodologies as well as requiring expert knowledge in one actuarial practice area (pricing, reserving, modeling, catastrophe risk, claims analytics, product management, etc.) to complete unstructured projects.




What you'll do:

Independently applies complex technical and actuarial methodologies, as well as proficient actuarial knowledge, to complete unstructured projects (e.g., selects and ensures quality of data used to perform trend analysis supports rate level indications; translates business problem into technical requirements; runs catastrophe models and interprets and communicates results to support business activities while executing and enhancing a strong control environment with guidance from an experienced catastrophe modeler.
Utilizes actuarial, mathematical, or statistical techniques to augment actuarial work product.
Creates and delivers training to teammates on mechanics of actuarial tools and processes.
Applies understanding of products supported, stakeholders' needs, and industry trends to solve unstructured business problems.
Identifies compliance gaps and process improvements and recommends develop solutions.
Leads unit initiatives and participates in cross-functional efforts as a technical subject matter expert.
Effectively communicates insights and solutions to all audiences, including executive leadership.
Synthesizes complex and conflicting information into a clear and influential value proposition.
Ensures risks associated with business activities are effectively identified, measured, monitored, and controlled in accordance with risk and compliance policies and procedures.




What you have:

Bachelor's degree; OR 4 years of related Actuarial/business/analytical experience (in addition to the minimum years of experience required) may be substituted in lieu of degree.
4 years of Actuarial or analytical business experience.
No more than 2 Casualty Actuarial Society (CAS) exams away from Associate of the Casualty Actuarial Society (ACAS) Credential and All Validation of Education Experience (VEEs) completed.
Demonstrated competency with relevant actuarial, mathematical, and statistical techniques and approaches used to support fact-based decision-making.
Intermediate knowledge of data analysis tools, data visualization, developing analysis queries and procedures in Python, R, SQL, SAS, BI tools or other analysis software, and relevant industry data & methods and ability to connect external insights to business problems.
Experience communicating complex solutions and analysis to both technical and nontechnical audiences.




What sets you apart:

A passion for creative and innovative work that improves existing processes and creates new, repeatable and automated workflows that enhance team productivity.
Basic understanding of GLM or GBM modeling algorithms.
Intermediate knowledge of Model Risk Management, Model Governance, and Regulatory requirements.
US military experience through military service or a military spouse/domestic partner.




Compensation range: The salary range for this position is: $103,450 - $186,210.




USAA does not provide visa sponsorship for this role. Please do not apply for this role if at any time (now or in the future) you will need immigration support (i.e., H-1B, TN, STEM OPT Training Plans, etc.).

Compensation: USAA has an effective process for assessing market data and establishing ranges to ensure we remain competitive. You are paid within the salary range based on your experience and market data of the position. The actual salary for this role may vary by location.




Employees may be eligible for pay incentives based on overall corporate and individual performance and at the discretion of the USAA Board of Directors.




The above description reflects the details considered necessary to describe the principal functions of the job and should not be construed as a detailed description of all the work requirements that may be performed in the job.




Benefits: At USAA our employees enjoy best-in-class benefits to support their physical, financial, and emotional wellness. These benefits include comprehensive medical, dental and vision plans, 401(k), pension, life insurance, parental benefits, adoption assistance, paid time off program with paid holidays plus 16 paid volunteer hours, and various wellness programs. Additionally, our career path planning and continuing education assists employees with their professional goals.




For more details on our outstanding benefits, visit our benefits page on USAAjobs.com.




Applications for this position are accepted on an ongoing basis, this posting will remain open until the position is filled. Thus, interested candidates are encouraged to apply the same day they view this posting.




USAA is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, or status as a protected veteran.

A passion for creative and innovative work that improves existing processes and creates new, repeatable and automated workflows that enhance team productivity.

US military experience through military service or a military spouse/domestic partner.

Title: Data Analyst - Finance (Remote)
Description:
Role: Data Analyst - Finance (Remote)
Location: Remote (Work from Anywhere)
Job Type: Contract
Payout: $40 - $65/hour




Role Overview:

We are hiring for one of our clients, seeking a Financial Data Analyst to work on a contract basis. This role involves analyzing financial data to support decision-making and reporting. The position requires expertise in financial modeling, data visualization, and regulatory compliance.




Key Responsibilities:

• Collect, process, and analyze financial data from multiple sources to identify trends and anomalies.

• Develop and maintain financial models to forecast revenue, expenses, and cash flow.

• Prepare detailed reports and dashboards for stakeholders to support strategic planning.

• Ensure data accuracy and compliance with accounting standards and regulatory requirements.

• Collaborate with cross-functional teams to integrate financial insights into business operations.




Required Skills & Qualifications:

• Proficiency in financial data analysis tools such as Excel, SQL, or Python.

• Experience with financial modeling and forecasting techniques.

• Strong understanding of accounting principles and financial reporting standards.

• Ability to create visualizations using tools like Tableau or Power BI.

• Familiarity with data cleaning and validation processes.




More About the Opportunity:

This role offers a unique opportunity to work with a global leader in the financial services industry, contributing to accurate financial reporting and strategic decision-making.




Equal Opportunity Employer:

We hire based on skills and expertise. All qualified candidates are welcome regardless of background, experience, or prior employment history. Applications are reviewed solely on demonstrated technical ability and qualifications.

Title: Technology Associate - Relocation to Dubai
Description:
Job Purpose

Are you a fresh graduate ready to shape the future of technology and travel with one of the world’s leading aviation brands?

As a Technology Associate, you will join a fast-paced one-year development experience within Emirates Group IT, gaining hands-on exposure, mentorship, and opportunities across diverse technology specialisations including AI, cloud, cybersecurity, and software engineering.

The Technology Associate is part of the Emirates Group IT team and supports the design, development, implementation and maintenance of technology solutions and products that enable and enhance Emirates Group business operations.




In This Role, You Will




Perform relevant technical tasks with a goal of developing scalable and sustainable solutions using established technology and engineering principles.
Participate in analysing business and system requirements to support the technical design process and contribute to the development of technical solutions aligned with agreed standards.
Develop, configure, test and implement technology solutions within a collaborative team environment to meet both functional and technical requirements.
Research, diagnose, troubleshoot and identify solutions to resolve system or application issues. Follow standard procedures for proper escalation of unresolved issues to the appropriate internal teams.
Collaborate with members of cross-functional teams to identify areas of inefficiency and propose improvements, while adhering to Emirates Group IT standards, guidelines and best practices.
Contribute to unit testing, validation and quality assurance activities with guidance from senior team members.
Support the maintenance of Technology Playbooks, standards documentation and other governance artefacts.




Qualifications

To be considered for this role, you must meet the below requirements:




Bachelor’s degree in information technology, computer science, computer engineering, software engineering or technology related field.
CGPA: 3.0 or higher (or equivalent).
1+ years of experience through exposure to current technologies in coursework or through internships in technology related fields.




This role is designed as an entry point for a university graduate in a technology related field of study as mentioned above.




Skills/Knowledge




Strong problem-solving skills with the ability to analyse technical challenges and contribute to well-designed, efficient and quality technology solutions.
Demonstrated ability to work collaboratively within cross-functional teams, with open communication and a positive team mindset.
Foundational understanding of technology delivery methodologies (e.g., Agile, DevOps practices) and quality assurance concepts including unit testing, functional testing and test automation.
Demonstrable passion for exploring and learning new technologies aligned to business needs.
Exposure to one or more contemporary technology domains (e.g., application development, cloud, enterprise platforms, integration, data, digital or security technologies), with foundational understanding relevant to business requirements.




Salary & Benefits




Join us in Dubai and enjoy an attractive tax-free salary and travel benefits that are exclusive to our industry, including discounts on flights and hotel stays around the world.

This is a full-time, office-based opportunity located in our Dubai headquarters. Successful candidates will be supported with a comprehensive relocation package, with Emirates arranging and assisting throughout the relocation process to ensure a smooth transition to Dubai.
