Title: Virtual Assistant - Remote
Description:
About The Company

Decentralized Masters is a pioneering organization in the field of DeFi education with a global footprint. Established just two years ago, the company has rapidly expanded from a small startup to a thriving enterprise with over 150 dedicated professionals. Recognized as one of the fastest-growing entities in the decentralized finance sector, Decentralized Masters is on a trajectory to become a unicorn by 2030, driven by innovative strategies and a bootstrap funding model. The company is projected to generate approximately $50 million in revenue this year, reflecting its strong market presence and growth potential.

At Decentralized Masters, the focus extends beyond mere educational offerings; the organization has successfully empowered over 4,500 investors to enter and thrive in the DeFi ecosystem. The company prides itself on fostering a vibrant community, leveraging cutting-edge technology, and assembling a team of leading experts in DeFi and blockchain technology. Its core mission is to deliver high-quality, long-term resources that enable members to safeguard and grow their financial futures in the rapidly evolving world of Web3 and digital finance.

Driven by an ambitious vision, Decentralized Masters aims to build the largest and most influential DeFi ecosystem globally. Its commitment to setting the gold standard in DeFi education underscores its transformative approach to digital finance, positioning it as a key player shaping the future of blockchain-based financial systems.

About The Role

The Virtual Assistant role at Decentralized Masters offers an exciting opportunity to be at the heart of a dynamic and innovative organization. This position involves managing communications, supporting customer onboarding, and assisting with administrative tasks to ensure smooth operations across the company. As a vital link between clients and the organization, the Virtual Assistant will be responsible for maintaining clear and consistent communication, escalating customer queries when necessary, and preparing reports and presentations to support strategic decision-making.

This role requires a proactive individual with excellent organizational skills, a customer-centric mindset, and the ability to work independently in a fully remote environment. The ideal candidate will be a native English speaker or possess a high level of professional proficiency, based in South America, and experienced in customer service and administrative support. Familiarity with DeFi or cryptocurrencies is a plus, but not mandatory. The position offers the chance to contribute to a fast-growing company that is revolutionizing digital finance and Web3 education.

Qualifications


High school diploma or equivalent; additional qualifications as an Administrator or Executive Assistant are a plus
Proven experience as a Virtual Assistant or in a similar administrative/customer service role
Customer service experience is essential
Native English speaker or professional-level proficiency in English
Must be located in South America
Familiarity with DeFi or cryptocurrency concepts is advantageous
Excellent proficiency with word-processing and spreadsheet software (Google Suite, MS Excel)
Experience with online calendars and scheduling tools (e.g., Google Calendar)
Strong communication skills via phone, email, and instant messaging
Exceptional time management and organizational skills
Ability to work independently and remotely with minimal supervision


Responsibilities


Manage and respond to emails and other communication channels promptly and professionally
Handle escalation of customer queries to ensure timely resolution
Assist with onboarding new customers, providing necessary information and support
Draft and prepare reports, presentations, and other documentation as needed
Maintain clear and continuous communication with clients and internal teams
Perform general administrative duties to support daily operations
Coordinate scheduling and calendar management for team members and clients
Support team with various tasks to enhance overall productivity and efficiency


Benefits


Competitive salary package
Full-time employment with a fully remote work environment
Unlimited paid time off (PTO) to promote work-life balance
Participation in team off-site events and company retreats
Dynamic and youthful company culture with team members across more than 30 countries
Flexible working location—work from anywhere in the world
Opportunity to be part of a fast-growing company at the forefront of DeFi and Web3 innovation


Equal Opportunity

Decentralized Masters is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. We do not discriminate based on race, ethnicity, gender, age, sexual orientation, disability, or any other protected characteristic. We encourage applications from candidates of all backgrounds who meet the qualifications and are passionate about contributing to the future of DeFi and blockchain technology.

Title: Customer Insights Analyst
Description:
CUSTOMER INSIGHTS & ANALYTICS ANALYST




GENERAL PURPOSE OF THE POSITION:

Provie internal and external data to help uncover single and multi-price point white space and assortment opportunities across the Dollar Tree categories to drive incremental traffic and sales. As the organization looks to increase and improve customer relative assortment, it is necessary to deliver support to the merchants to understand performance of current market items and potential incremental opportunities. Dedicated individuals to the mining of this data are imperative to the successful integration of the right new items for our shopper.




ESSENTIAL ACTIVIES & RESPONSIBILITIES:

Support the development and execution of merchandising strategies and decision making by delivering timely, accurate, and actionable insights derived from syndicated data and customer research.
Support strategic initiatives with rigorous analytics, utilizing third party data sources including Nielsen, Numerator, 1010, Circana, and customer research.
Assist in the development and maintenance of daily and weekly standardized reporting.
Organize and evaluate large data sets to assist with the development of “white space” and assortment recommendations to drive traffic and sales.
Assist with senior leader presentations by preparing data driven content.
Assist in customer research initiatives to inform marketing strategies and optimize campaign effectiveness.
Help lead training efforts with merchandising and marketing resources to promote best practices in data interpretation and usage.
Identify, prioritize, and initiate data collection process improvements to improve team efficiency.




EXPERIENCE AND EDUCATION REQUIRED:

Bachelors Degree (BA / BS/ BFA) or equivalent, and 1-2 years experience.
Excellent analytical and problem solving skills. Able to generate and evaluate hypotheses and create narratives for senior leaders.
Detail-oriented. Comfortable working with numerical data and information.
Strong written and verbal communication skills.
Demonstrated initiative, independent thinker and worker.
Team player in a collaborative environment.
Deadline oriented. Ability to anticipate and address conflicts, to skillfully negotiate, and deliver great outcomes.
Advanced skills in Microsoft Office products, especially Excel and PowerPoint.
Must be willing to relocate to Chesapeake, VA




Start Rate = $75,000-$84,500K based on experience plus 10% bonus.

The company offers Health, Dental, & Vision, a flexible spending account, life and disability insurance benefits, a 401k plan, PTO, paid holidays annually, and an employee stock purchase plan.

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
Roles & Responsibilities

Design, develop, and maintain scalable ETL/data integration pipelines using AWS Glue, PySpark, and Python.
Develop and optimize data transformation workflows to process large datasets from multiple source systems.
Utilize Attunity for real-time and batch data replication between on-premises and cloud environments.
Build and manage data lakes and storage solutions using Amazon S3, ensuring data quality, security, and governance.
Implement serverless data processing solutions using AWS Lambda and integrate with other AWS services.
Configure and manage IAM roles, policies, and permissions to ensure secure access to AWS resources.
Automate infrastructure provisioning and deployment using AWS CloudFormation.
Monitor, troubleshoot, and optimize ETL jobs, data pipelines, and cloud resources for performance and reliability.
Collaborate with business, analytics, and application teams to gather requirements and deliver data solutions.
Support CI/CD processes, perform code reviews, and maintain technical documentation following best practices.

Generic Managerial Skills, If any

Planning & Execution – Effectively prioritize tasks, estimate effort, and ensure timely delivery of project milestones while balancing multiple commitments.
Stakeholder Management – Build strong relationships with business teams, clients, and leadership through proactive communication and expectation management.
Communication – Convey technical and non-technical information clearly through meetings, presentations, documentation, and status updates.
Collaboration – Foster teamwork across cross-functional groups, encourage knowledge sharing, and contribute to a positive team environment.
Decision Making – Analyze options, assess risks, and make informed decisions that align with both technical and business objectives.
Leadership & Ownership – Take accountability for deliverables, drive initiatives proactively, and lead by example to achieve team goals.
Risk & Issue Management – Identify potential risks early, address blockers proactively, and implement mitigation strategies to ensure smooth project execution.
Customer & Business Focus – Understand customer needs and business priorities, ensuring technical solutions deliver measurable value.
Mentoring & Coaching – Support team growth through guidance, code reviews, knowledge sharing, and skill development initiatives.
Project & Change Management – Adapt effectively to evolving requirements, manage priorities during change, and ensure successful delivery outcomes.

  • Collaboration – Foster teamwork across cross-functional groups, encourage knowledge sharing, and contribute to a positive team environment.
  • Decision Making – Analyze options, assess risks, and make informed decisions that align with both technical and business objectives.
  • Leadership & Ownership – Take accountability for deliverables, drive initiatives proactively, and lead by example to achieve team goals.
  • Risk & Issue Management – Identify potential risks early, address blockers proactively, and implement mitigation strategies to ensure smooth project execution.
  • Customer & Business Focus – Understand customer needs and business priorities, ensuring technical solutions deliver measurable value.
  • Mentoring & Coaching – Support team growth through guidance, code reviews, knowledge sharing, and skill development initiatives.
  • Project & Change Management – Adapt effectively to evolving requirements, manage priorities during change, and ensure successful delivery outcomes.

Title: Business Intelligence (BI) Engineer
Description:
Robert Half is partnering with an innovative organization to hire a Senior Business Intelligence Analyst for a direct hire opportunity. This is an excellent opportunity for an experienced BI professional who enjoys building scalable data solutions, transforming complex data into actionable insights, and partnering with business stakeholders to drive data-informed decision making.




What You'll Do

Design, develop, and maintain scalable data models, data warehouses, and ETL/ELT pipelines using Snowflake.
Build reliable data validation processes including reconciliation, record count validation, anomaly detection, and other data quality controls.
Write and optimize advanced SQL queries to support reporting, analytics, and business intelligence initiatives.
Develop interactive dashboards and reports using Power BI or similar visualization platforms.
Translate business requirements into technical data solutions that provide meaningful insights.
Partner with stakeholders across multiple business functions to gather requirements and deliver BI solutions.
Lead Business Intelligence projects from planning through implementation, including documentation, timelines, and stakeholder communication.
Establish and promote BI best practices, governance standards, and data visualization strategies.
Provide end-user training, support, and guidance to encourage adoption of reporting and analytics tools.
Identify opportunities to improve reporting processes, data accessibility, and overall analytics capabilities.





Qualifications

Bachelor's degree in Data Analytics, Computer Science, Information Systems, Business Analytics, or a related field.
5+ years of experience in Business Intelligence, Data Analytics, Business Analysis, or a similar role.
Advanced SQL skills with experience working with relational databases.
Hands-on experience with Snowflake data modeling and modern data warehousing concepts.
Experience building dashboards and reports in Power BI or similar BI platforms.
Strong understanding of data modeling, ETL/ELT processes, and data quality methodologies.
Experience using Python, APIs, AI-enabled analytics tools, or other modern data technologies is a plus.
ERP reporting experience is beneficial.
Excellent analytical, organizational, problem-solving, and communication skills.
Experience managing projects and collaborating with both technical and non-technical stakeholders.

Preferred Skills

Snowflake
SQL
Power BI
Data Warehousing
Data Modeling
ETL / ELT
DAX
Python
APIs
AI Technologies
Relational Databases

Additional Information

This position requires U.S. citizenship due to project and data access requirements. Candidates will be required to provide proof of U.S. citizenship during the hiring process.

Title: Technical AIP Data Analyst
Description:
Founded in 2011, Altenar Technology Limited is a leading igaming software provider inspired by the vision of our customers. As our platform has scaled we have begun to generate high volumes of transactional data which can be potentially mined to generate additional insights. The Altenar Intelligence Platform (AIP) is the long term vision for unlocking this potential.




We are seeking a Technical AIP Data Analyst to join the Technology team to work closely with various Product and Business stakeholders to focus on using data pipelines and various analytical techniques to analyse business procedures. The role requires outstanding communication skills, proven ability to build and manage medium-scale modelling projects, define scope of existing infrastructure, identify data requirements, build methodology and tools that are well researched, undertake feasibility studies, and support with documentation. The candidate will be expected to demonstrate familiarity with concepts such as ETL, machine learning and statistics.




The successful applicant should illustrate comfort working in a startup environment, possessing high intellectual curiosity with ability to quickly learn new concepts/frameworks, algorithms and technology that powers Alternar business objectives. We seek someone with a high degree of ownership that can effectively examine flows of data, researching and evaluating to consistently deliver superior quality results on-time. The successful candidate should be able to rapidly prototype analytical models with the goal of generating strategic business insights as per the needs of the AIP roadmap.




Finally, the applicant is expected to display a high degree of independence working across multiple programs and initiatives to un-earth opportunities. The role demands strong data organisation, data structure, and data hierarchy knowledge, and may be suited to incumbents with extensive Data Analysis and/or computing programming experience.




The activities of a Technical AIP Data Analyst include but are not limited to:




Continuously updating core knowledge and skills in SQL and Python or R as applied to mathematical analysis
Liaises with internal stakeholders including the Solution Architect and Business Analysts, and directly with key Product and Business owners on topics for exploration and their description
Liaises with internal stakeholders including Data Engineers, Infrastructure Engineers and Data Services Engineers on data collection, movement and storage strategies
Analyse and Build analytical applications in a structured manner that can be tested and deployed based on stakeholder requirements
Undertake feasibility studies and various types of analytics including descriptive, diagnostic, predictive or prescriptive analytics.
Communicate the impact of technologies by delivering views, dashboards and report configurations using BI tools as required by internal stakeholders
Supporting software architecture processes with data handling and visualisation specifications
Participation in the Architecture Review process (including by providing advice and assistance) to ensure new initiatives or major changes to the Altenar product portfolio have data flow considerations represented at the inception phase
Examine existing business models and flows of data and design functional specifications by participating in the research, test, design of new data services tools for online or warehoused data
Work in a fast-paced, dynamic, multinational, multicultural environment
Creating and maintaining documentation for the solutions provided




Qualifications

Experience working with python and pandas
Strong SQL querying skills
Knowledge of noSQL and ETL operations considered a plus
Strong analytical and forecasting skills using offline methods such as spreadsheets
Strong math and statistics background (relevant Degree education required)
Proven experience with one or more BI tools (Metabase, Apache Superset, Redash, PowerBI, Tableau, Qlikview)
Ability to quickly write ad-hoc scripts to analyze small to medium datasets.
Strong research and validation skills
Motivated and self-driven to work in an environment with minimal supervision
Strong presentation skills
Proficient in spoken and written English
Experience in Technical Data Analytics, Data Science (Computing), and/or Product Management roles
Graduate-level education in Computer Science, Engineering, Math, Statistics, Economics, or other relevant field




Preferred Experience

Experience with Spark core functionality such as Spark SQL, Spark Structured Streaming is very desirable
Some experience in using Git version control in the context of documentation/source code management
Knowledge of Linux CLI tools to perform "quick and dirty" command line analysis to serve as first exploratory review of data
Some knowledge of MLaaS such as Amazon Machine Learning services, Azure Machine Learning, Google AI Platform, and IBM Watson Machine Learning
Experience in building, testing and validating machine learning algorithms
Prior direct or indirect involvement in data warehouse design, tool selection and support
Experience with Data Lab concepts with Hue, Zeppelin, Jupyterhub
Experience with Hadoop/HDFS - CLI, HDFS access from scripts
Knowledge of C# or Java is considered an asset

Title: Business Intelligence Analyst
Description:
Casumo is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.

Join us at Casumo, where you are invited to be your authentic YOU-MO!

Company Overview

Welcome to Casumo, your passport to a world of fun, excitement, and responsible gaming. We're an international online casino company with a knack for creating unforgettable gaming experiences. Our secret sauce? A blend of innovation, security, and a dash of playful charm.

Nowadays, we're on the hunt for an analytical and collaborative Business Intelligence Analyst!

Position Overview

The Business Intelligence Analyst is responsible for developing, maintaining, and improving reporting and analytics solutions that support business decision making across the organization. The role works closely with Business Intelligence Analyst Lead, Senior Business Analysts, Data Engineers and business stakeholders to deliver accurate, scalable, and user-friendly reports. The BI Analyst translates defined business requirements into dashboards, reports and analyses.

Responsibilities


Design, develop, and maintain dashboards, reports, and self-service reporting solutions primarily using Tableau.
Develop and maintain SQL queries, reporting views, calculations, and supporting reporting logic.
Validate reporting accuracy and ensure KPI logic is implemented correctly.
Apply dimensional modelling and reporting best practices in analytics solutions
Ensure dashboards and reports are visually clear, user-friendly, and aligned with the best reporting practices.
Support the maintenance and enhancement of existing reporting solutions.
Perform ad-hoc analyses to support operational and tactical business decision-making.
Investigate data discrepancies, incidents, and reporting issues.
Analyze datasets to identify trends, anomalies, and actionable insights.
Collaborate with Business Analyst Lead and Senior Business Analysts to deliver reporting and analytics solutions based on defined business requirements.
Communicate with stakeholders regarding reporting functionality, enhancements, and issue resolution.
Provide support and guidance to end-users on reporting usage and available data sources.
Maintain documentation for reports, dashboards, and reporting logic
Identify opportunities to automate and improve recurring reporting processes.


Requirements


A bachelor’s degree in business administration, finance, computer science, data science, economics, statistics, or a related field.
2+ years of experience in data analysis, business analysis, or a related field.
Good knowledge of the iGaming industry including key operational business processes, metrics, and key performance indicators (KPIs).
Strong SQL skills including joins, aggregations, subqueries, and reporting logic development.
Experience developing dashboards and reports using Tableau.
Good understanding of data warehousing and dimensional modelling concepts.
Familiarity with ETL concepts and reporting-oriented data structures.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
Ability to interpret data and provide actionable insights.
Good understanding of business KPIs and reporting requirements.
Strong communication skills with both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Ability to manage multiple tasks and priorities in a fast-paced environment.
Experience working in SCRUM or agile environments is considered a plus.


Think we're a good match? Apply now!

Malta Office

Private health insurance

Flexible national holidays: public holidays mean more time off, choose how and when to enjoy them!

ASPIRE

At Casumo, "aspire" means pushing beyond the ordinary and transforming obstacles into stepping stones. Challenges are our breakfast of champions, and comfort zones are out of bounds. Mediocrity? Left behind. Our mantra? Dream big, aim high, and always be ready for the next adventure in innovation.

BELIEVE

Belief at Casumo isn't just a feel-good sticker; it's the glue that binds us. Turning "me" achievements into "we" victories, we're a tight-knit crew of dreamers, doers, and relentless supporters. Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Join our Business Intelligence team, where data is transformed into insights that drive business performance and strategic decisions. You'll work with data from multiple sources, partnering with stakeholders across the business to define meaningful KPIs, build and maintain Tableau dashboards, and use SQL to uncover trends, validate data, and deliver actionable insights. Your work will directly influence decision-making, helping teams better understand performance, identify new opportunities, and continuously improve the way we use data to grow the business. If you're curious, analytical, and enjoy turning complex data into clear, impactful solutions, we'd love to hear from you.




Core responsibilities

Use SQL to extract, transform, and manage data from internal sources, ensuring it’s accurate and ready for analysis.
Analyze complex business problems, uncovering trends, root causes, and opportunities to improve performance.
Build and enhance Tableau reports and dashboards, turning data into clear, actionable insights for stakeholders.
Partner with product, management, and cross-functional teams to define KPIs and support data-driven decision-making.
Collaborate closely with BI teammates and data engineers to improve data pipelines, reduce technical debt, and scale the Data Warehouse.
Proactively explore data beyond assigned requests. Anticipate surface trends, risks, and opportunities to bring insights before issues arise.




Experience and key competencies

At least 2 years of experience in a Data Analyst, BI Analyst, or similar analytical role, supported by a relevant educational background (e.g. Data Science, Statistics, Mathematics, or a related field).
Strong SQL skills, including querying, transforming, and validating large datasets, writing complex queries, and optimizing performance. Knowledge of Python or R is an advantage.
Hands-on experience building and maintaining dashboards in Tableau (or a similar BI tool), with the ability to present complex data in a clear and actionable way.
Solid understanding of data modeling, KPI design, metrics documentation, and statistical methods, with the ability to apply them to real business challenges.
Strong stakeholder management and communication skills, with the ability to translate business needs into analytical solutions and communicate insights effectively.
Analytical mindset with the ability to balance attention to detail while keeping the bigger picture in focus.
Self-driven and adaptable, with strong ownership, attention to detail, and the ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment.
Fluency in verbal and written communication in English is essential, as it's our primary means of interaction.




Think of this job ad as more of a friendly guide than a rigid 'must-have' list. We're well aware that no one will tick all the boxes, so feel free to apply even if you're not a perfect match – we love close matches too!




Our benefits, Your happiness

Enjoy 28 days of vacation with the option to use your balance on business days only, effectively giving you up to 10 extra days off. Combined with flexible working hours, you can make the most of your time off.
Prioritize your well-being with monthly sports/health compensation, full sick leave coverage for the first 8 days, our in-house gym, and a chill games room to unwind.
Invest in your professional growth with an annual training budget that can be used for courses, certifications, workshops, and learning opportunities relevant to your role.
Our office is a cozy (and dog-friendly) haven right in the heart of the city. Enjoy complimentary fruits and beverages and free parking. It's the perfect place to be productive and feel right at home.
Be part of the most legendary events and make amazing connections with colleagues from across the entire company.
Thrive in a diverse, multicultural environment where innovation and new ideas are welcomed, independence is encouraged, success is celebrated, and you'll have the freedom to make a real impact alongside talented people from across the globe.
Comprehensive relocation support, including arranged travel and initial accommodation, to help you settle in smoothly from day one.
..plus, if you're not already a pro, we'll teach you how to play poker!




Recruitment flow

Interview - Task - Second Interview - Background check - Offer
Start date: ASAP
Open to candidates based in the EU.
Applications are open until we find the one - take the leap and apply now!

HTML:
Join our Business Intelligence team, where data is transformed into insights that drive business performance and strategic decisions. You'll work with data from multiple sources, partnering with stakeholders across the business to define meaningful KPIs, build and maintain Tableau dashboards, and use SQL to uncover trends, validate data, and deliver actionable insights. Your work will directly influence decision-making, helping teams better understand performance, identify new opportunities, and continuously improve the way we use data to grow the business. If you're curious, analytical, and enjoy turning complex data into clear, impactful solutions, we'd love to hear from you.


Core responsibilities

  • Use SQL to extract, transform, and manage data from internal sources, ensuring it’s accurate and ready for analysis.
  • Analyze complex business problems, uncovering trends, root causes, and opportunities to improve performance.
  • Build and enhance Tableau reports and dashboards, turning data into clear, actionable insights for stakeholders.
  • Partner with product, management, and cross-functional teams to define KPIs and support data-driven decision-making.
  • Collaborate closely with BI teammates and data engineers to improve data pipelines, reduce technical debt, and scale the Data Warehouse.
  • Proactively explore data beyond assigned requests. Anticipate surface trends, risks, and opportunities to bring insights before issues arise.


Experience and key competencies

  • At least 2 years of experience in a Data Analyst, BI Analyst, or similar analytical role, supported by a relevant educational background (e.g. Data Science, Statistics, Mathematics, or a related field).
  • Strong SQL skills, including querying, transforming, and validating large datasets, writing complex queries, and optimizing performance. Knowledge of Python or R is an advantage.
  • Hands-on experience building and maintaining dashboards in Tableau (or a similar BI tool), with the ability to present complex data in a clear and actionable way.
  • Solid understanding of data modeling, KPI design, metrics documentation, and statistical methods, with the ability to apply them to real business challenges.
  • Strong stakeholder management and communication skills, with the ability to translate business needs into analytical solutions and communicate insights effectively.
  • Analytical mindset with the ability to balance attention to detail while keeping the bigger picture in focus.
  • Self-driven and adaptable, with strong ownership, attention to detail, and the ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment.
  • Fluency in verbal and written communication in English is essential, as it's our primary means of interaction.


Think of this job ad as more of a friendly guide than a rigid 'must-have' list. We're well aware that no one will tick all the boxes, so feel free to apply even if you're not a perfect match – we love close matches too!


Our benefits, Your happiness

  • Enjoy 28 days of vacation with the option to use your balance on business days only, effectively giving you up to 10 extra days off. Combined with flexible working hours, you can make the most of your time off.
  • Prioritize your well-being with monthly sports/health compensation, full sick leave coverage for the first 8 days, our in-house gym, and a chill games room to unwind.
  • Invest in your professional growth with an annual training budget that can be used for courses, certifications, workshops, and learning opportunities relevant to your role.
  • Our office is a cozy (and dog-friendly) haven right in the heart of the city. Enjoy complimentary fruits and beverages and free parking. It's the perfect place to be productive and feel right at home.
  • Be part of the most legendary events and make amazing connections with colleagues from across the entire company.
  • Thrive in a diverse, multicultural environment where innovation and new ideas are welcomed, independence is encouraged, success is celebrated, and you'll have the freedom to make a real impact alongside talented people from across the globe.
  • Comprehensive relocation support, including arranged travel and initial accommodation, to help you settle in smoothly from day one.
  • ..plus, if you're not already a pro, we'll teach you how to play poker!


Recruitment flow

  • Interview - Task - Second Interview - Background check - Offer
  • Start date: ASAP
  • Open to candidates based in the EU.
  • Applications are open until we find the one - take the leap and apply now!


We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, ancestry, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender, gender identity or expression, age, disability, medical condition, pregnancy, marital status.


Compensation

At Coolbet, we carefully consider a wide range of factors when determining your salary, including market benchmarks, specific job family, background, location, skills, and experience. The typical range for this role is 3000€-3500€ gross per month. This reflects total compensation.


Location

Tallinn, Estonia

We offer sportsbook, casino, and poker experiences to players in 8 countries across 3 continents and provide advanced B2B solutions to operators in the US. Part of the Sega Sammy Creation gaming segment, our core team of 500+ experts collaborates across borders to create world-class experiences for both players and operators. With a strong presence at our Tallinn HQ, home to a diverse team representing over 50 nationalities, we bring different perspectives together to shape the future of gaming through continuous product development, a collaborative culture, and a strong commitment to responsible play. BettingJobs is working with a very well established land-based resorts company who also operate a successful online casino brand. They are looking for an experienced Data Analyst to join their growing team in Malta.







About The Role




The Data Analyst is accountable for:




Deliver high-quality reporting and insights across online gaming products and platforms in regulated markets
Design and implement automated reporting, dashboards, and scalable data solutions to improve efficiency and reliability
Analyse complex datasets to identify trends, risks, and opportunities that inform product, commercial, and operational decisions
Monitor and improve data quality, working across internal teams and external vendors to ensure accuracy, integrity, and compliance







About You




You are a commercially minded and detail‑driven Data Analyst with strong experience working in complex, data‑rich digital environments. You thrive on turning large, messy datasets into clear, actionable insights and take pride in building reliable, scalable reporting solutions.




You are a self‑starter who enjoys working across systems, stakeholders, and vendors, and you bring a high level of rigour to data accuracy, documentation, and governance. You are confident engaging with both technical and non‑technical audiences, and you’re comfortable operating in regulated industries where compliance and precision are critical.




You bring proven experience in building dashboards, automating reporting, and analysing trends to support business decisions, ideally within online gaming or a similar regulated digital environment.




What’s in it for you?




Work on high impact digital initiatives shaping the company’s online future
A supportive, flexible, and inclusive workplace where you can thrive
Opportunities for development and career growth
Great company benefits

Title: Business Intelligence Analyst
Description:
Casumo is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.

Join us at Casumo, where you are invited to be your authentic YOU-MO!

Company Overview

Welcome to Casumo, your passport to a world of fun, excitement, and responsible gaming. We're an international online casino company with a knack for creating unforgettable gaming experiences. Our secret sauce? A blend of innovation, security, and a dash of playful charm.

Nowadays, we're on the hunt for an analytical and collaborative Business Intelligence Analyst!

Position Overview

The Business Intelligence Analyst is responsible for developing, maintaining, and improving reporting and analytics solutions that support business decision making across the organization. The role works closely with Business Intelligence Analyst Lead, Senior Business Analysts, Data Engineers and business stakeholders to deliver accurate, scalable, and user-friendly reports. The BI Analyst translates defined business requirements into dashboards, reports and analyses.

Responsibilities


Design, develop, and maintain dashboards, reports, and self-service reporting solutions primarily using Tableau.
Develop and maintain SQL queries, reporting views, calculations, and supporting reporting logic.
Validate reporting accuracy and ensure KPI logic is implemented correctly.
Apply dimensional modelling and reporting best practices in analytics solutions
Ensure dashboards and reports are visually clear, user-friendly, and aligned with the best reporting practices.
Support the maintenance and enhancement of existing reporting solutions.
Perform ad-hoc analyses to support operational and tactical business decision-making.
Investigate data discrepancies, incidents, and reporting issues.
Analyze datasets to identify trends, anomalies, and actionable insights.
Collaborate with Business Analyst Lead and Senior Business Analysts to deliver reporting and analytics solutions based on defined business requirements.
Communicate with stakeholders regarding reporting functionality, enhancements, and issue resolution.
Provide support and guidance to end-users on reporting usage and available data sources.
Maintain documentation for reports, dashboards, and reporting logic
Identify opportunities to automate and improve recurring reporting processes.


Requirements


A bachelor’s degree in business administration, finance, computer science, data science, economics, statistics, or a related field.
2+ years of experience in data analysis, business analysis, or a related field.
Good knowledge of the iGaming industry including key operational business processes, metrics, and key performance indicators (KPIs).
Strong SQL skills including joins, aggregations, subqueries, and reporting logic development.
Experience developing dashboards and reports using Tableau.
Good understanding of data warehousing and dimensional modelling concepts.
Familiarity with ETL concepts and reporting-oriented data structures.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
Ability to interpret data and provide actionable insights.
Good understanding of business KPIs and reporting requirements.
Strong communication skills with both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Ability to manage multiple tasks and priorities in a fast-paced environment.
Experience working in SCRUM or agile environments is considered a plus.


Think we're a good match? Apply now!

The Perks

Malta Office

Being a part of the Casumo group provides an unparalleled experience. You’ll find yourself surrounded by the brightest minds within the most inspiring and collaborative office spaces! In addition to that, you’ll enjoy:


Private health insurance
Wellness incentives, including a fitness allowance and mental well-being services
Flexible national holidays: public holidays mean more time off, choose how and when to enjoy them!
2 weeks Work From Anywhere (10 days), increased to 4 weeks (20 days) after longer duration of employment within the Company: explore the world while working remotely
Gourmet lunches and healthy snacks prepared by our in-house chef
Variety of discounts from local vendors
Access to some of the greatest tools and platforms for developing your professional skills and building success within your role
A range of training courses, known as Casumo College, for continuous learning and growth
Social events for building strong relationships with colleagues from all across the organisation


Macedonia Office


Private health insurance
Wellness incentives, including a fitness allowance and mental well-being services
2 weeks Work From Anywhere (10 days), increased to 4 weeks (20 days) after longer duration of employment within the Company: explore the world while working remotely
Office lunches - three times per week
Access to some of the greatest tools and platforms for developing your professional skills and building success within your role
A range of training courses, known as Casumo College, for continuous learning and growth
Social events for building strong relationships with colleagues from all across the organisation


Zagreb Office


Private health insurance
Wellness incentives, including a fitness allowance and mental well-being services
2 weeks Work From Anywhere (10 days), increased to 4 weeks (20 days) after longer duration of employment within the Company: explore the world while working remotely
Food allowance
Access to some of the greatest tools and platforms for developing your professional skills and building success within your role
A range of training courses, known as Casumo College, for continuous learning and growth
Social events for building strong relationships with colleagues from all across the organisation


Our ABC Values

ASPIRE

At Casumo, "aspire" means pushing beyond the ordinary and transforming obstacles into stepping stones. Challenges are our breakfast of champions, and comfort zones are out of bounds. Mediocrity? Left behind. Our mantra? Dream big, aim high, and always be ready for the next adventure in innovation.

BELIEVE

Belief at Casumo isn't just a feel-good sticker; it's the glue that binds us. Turning "me" achievements into "we" victories, we're a tight-knit crew of dreamers, doers, and relentless supporters. With a high-five arsenal and a trusty cheerleading squad, we're on a mission to prove that together, we're not just strong; we're Casumo strong.

CARE

Care is our secret ingredient, the cherry on top of our game. It's not only about ensuring our players have a blast (responsibly, of course); it's about weaving a fabric of support so tight, even the toughest challenges can't tear us apart. From tailoring player experiences to being there for each other, we're all about creating memorable moments.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Are you ready to join our digital revolution journey?

At Aleph , we’re not just part of the digital advertising landscape—we’re shaping its future. Representing the world’s leading platforms, including TikTok, Amazon, Google, and nearly 55 others, we operate in 130+ markets across new and existing geographies.

Our mission is to empower advertisers and brands to unlock the full potential of these platforms' advertising capabilities. By fostering long-lasting partnerships, we create limitless opportunities for people and businesses to advertise effectively at both local and global levels.

With a presence spanning continents, Aleph offers you the chance to be part of a fast-growing, innovative team where your work makes a direct impact. If you’re ambitious, forward-thinking, and eager to thrive in a dynamic, global environment, Aleph is the perfect place to build your career.

Role Overview

We are looking for a highly analytical Data Analyst to join our Business Intelligence team. You will act as a critical "backend engine," building the data foundations and analytical models that enable data-driven decisions for both our supply (platforms) and demand (sales) sides.

You will dive deep into complex datasets, build automated reporting pipelines, and uncover actionable insights that drive our strategy with global platforms like TikTok, Microsoft, Google, X, Spotify, and others.

What You 'll Do



Extract and analyze complex data.
Query, clean, and transform large datasets from multiple sources to uncover trends, performance metrics, and market insights that support strategic decision-making across the organization.
Build and maintain BI dashboards.
Design, develop, and maintain automated dashboards and reports using Looker Studio to provide real-time visibility into business performance for executive and partner discussions. Support strategic projects.
Conduct deep-dive analyses for platform RFP responses, Quarterly Business Reviews (QBRs), and strategic initiatives that shape Aleph’s partnerships and revenue strategy. Collaborate with cross-functional stakeholders.
Work closely with strategists, sales teams, and regional leadership to define data requirements, understand their business challenges, and deliver quantitative solutions.
Translate data into actionable recommendations.
Identify performance anomalies, revenue opportunities, and market risks by packaging raw data into structured, easy-to-consume analytical models and insights.
Ensure data integrity and governance.
Establish clear data validation frameworks, optimize queries, and maintain high standards for data accuracy and documentation internally.



Who You Are



Highly analytical and problem-solving oriented.
You have a strong quantitative background and enjoy tackling complex business questions through rigorous data analysis.
Technically proficient in data tools.
You possess strong SQL skills for data extraction and manipulation, and you are highly proficient in at least one major BI visualization tool (e.g., Power BI, Looker Studio). Advanced Excel/Google Sheets skills are a must; experience with Python a strong plus. Business-minded.You don’t just pull numbers; you understand the business context behind the data and can connect your analysis directly to revenue growth, efficiency, and key opportunities.
A clear communicator. You can translate complex technical findings and data models into clear, actionable insights for non-technical stakeholders and leadership
Structured and detail-oriented. You are meticulous about data accuracy, organize your code and queries logically, and ensure all deliverables meet a high-quality standard before they reach stakeholders.
Interested in digital media or advertising.
Experience in digital advertising analytics, media agencies, ad-tech, or similar environments is a strong plus, though not strictly required.



What You'll Love About Us



We care about your individuality by giving you freedom to grow and create within the company, regardless of your position.
Work in a dynamic team in a fast-paced industry of an international company with multi-cultural teams around the world .
Learn from the best: Our state of the art workshops guarantee the latest insights into digital advertising.
In line with the EU Pay Transparency Directive, we are committed to fair and open compensation practices. For this role, the minimum gross annual base salary is 35,000 EUR.



In addition to base salary, this role includes a variable component, paid annually. The final offer will reflect your experience, skills, and the scope of the role.

#ALEPH

Why Join Us?

Aleph is a place of many cultures, perspectives, and talents. We support each other, creating an environment of giving and receiving. We value partnership and communication because we believe it takes a group of people to achieve great things. We are energized by our ever-changing industry, our curiosity keeps us learning and seeking out new opportunities.

We stay flexible and adaptable, and believe in moving with the speed of change. We encourage everyone to strive for more, providing our internal talent with growth opportunities and the ability to learn together.

#ALEPH

We use Lever, our recruitment management platform, which includes AI-powered features that may assist our hiring team in reviewing applications, analyzing CVs, and identifying relevant qualifications. These tools support - but do not replace - human judgement. All hiring decisions are made by people. For more information about how your personal data is processed, including your rights in relation to AI-assisted processing, please see our Candidate Privacy Policy or contact us at [email protected] .

Title: Supply Chain AI & Automation Analyst
Description:
Over 50,000 customers globally trust our end-to-end, cloud-driven networking solutions and rely on our top-rated services and support to accelerate their digital transformation efforts and deliver progress like never before and with double digit growth year over year, no provider is better positioned to deliver better outcomes on scale, than Extreme.

Come become part of something big with us! We are a global leader, with hubs in Europe, North America, South America, Asia Pacific, and the Middle East.

Supply Chain AI & Automation Analyst

About The Role

We are seeking a “Supply Chain AI & Automation Analyst” to join our Extreme Networks Global Operations organization, where you'll turn complex operational data into clear, actionable insight that drives decisions and automation across our Global Supply Chan and Order Management teams. This role sits at the intersection of analytics, AI, and supply chain strategy, partnering with cross-functional teams to identify opportunities, build analytical products, and tell the stories behind the numbers. The ideal candidate is a curious, technically strong analyst who is equally comfortable writing SQL, using AI tools, framing a business problem, managing a project, and presenting findings to stakeholders. You should be excited about the rapid evolution of AI and agentic workflows and how they can be applied to supply chain decision-making and you should be actively using AI tools in your work today.

A Note On Experience

This role requires commercially delivered analytical work. We will ask you at interview to walk through a specific project from problem through to business outcome that you owned and delivered in a professional setting. Academic or course-based projects do not qualify. If your experience is primarily from an academic programme, this role is not the right fit at this time

Key Responsibilities


Partner with supply chain stakeholders to scope problems and translate them into structured, measurable, automated workstreams.
Build and maintain python/SQL/Snowflake-based data models, dashboards, and self-service analytics for our global operations teams
Help operationalize AI and agent-based solutions to automate recurring analyses, augment decision support, and supply chain processes.
Apply statistical methods to inform data driven decisions across our global operations function.
Manage your projects end-to-end: scoping, stakeholder alignment, data discovery, modelling, validation, and rollout.
Translate analytical findings into compelling narratives (written, visual, and verbal) tailored to operational, business, and technical audiences.
Define and track KPIs that measure supply chain performance and flag interventions when metrics drift.
Contribute to ongoing improvements in data quality, documentation, and analytical standards across the team.


Required Skills & Qualifications


3-5 years experience in business analytics, data analytics, or a comparable quantitative role.
Demonstrated use of AI tools such as Claude, MS Copilot, or ChatGPT – in an analytical workflow, with a genuine appetite to develop agentic and automation capabilities in role.
Strong python skills and hands-on experience with Snowflake (or a comparable cloud data warehouse such as BigQuery, Redshift, or Databricks).
Demonstrated project management ability, owning deliverables across multiple stakeholders and timelines.
Solid grounding in descriptive statistics, and analytics fundamentals.
Strong data storytelling skills: the ability to distil complexity into clear visuals and narratives that drive action.
Supply chain domain knowledge: familiarity with demand and supply planning, inventory optimization, order management, logistics, procurement, or manufacturing operations.
Bachelor's degree in a quantitative field (e.g., Statistics, Operations Research, Engineering, Physics, Economics, Data Science, Supply Chain, Mathematics, or related).


Technical Skills Required


Programming for analytics: Python or R for modelling, automation, and analytics workflows.
BI and visualization tools: Tableau, Sigma, Power BI, Looker, or similar — both for building and for design literacy.
ERP / source systems: Exposure to Oracle Fusion Cloud or similar transactional systems.


Preferred / Additional Skills: These are skills that would meaningfully strengthen a candidate for this role:


Practical experience applying agentic workflows (e.g., LLM-based assistants, retrieval-augmented analytics, automated reasoning agents) to analytical or operational problems.
Master's or PhD in a quantitative field (Statistics, Operations Research, Engineering, Physics, Economics, Data Science, Supply Chain, Mathematics, or related).
Forecasting and optimization: time-series methods as well as other optimization techniques.
Machine learning fundamentals: classification, regression, clustering, and an understanding of when ML is and isn't the right tool.
Process improvement methodologies: Lean, Six Sigma, or equivalent structured problem-solving frameworks.
Stakeholder management and influencing: navigating ambiguity, managing competing priorities, and building trust with non-technical partners.


What Success Looks Like


Within 3 months: deeply understand our supply chain data landscape, key stakeholders, and top-priority business questions.
Within 6 months: deliver at least one high-impact analytical product or AI-enabled workflow that changes how a decision is made.
Within 12 months: become a trusted analytics partner to supply chain stakeholders and a contributor to the broader analytics team's standards and practices.


Salary Is Based On Qualifications And Experience Between 60-70K

Title: Digital Analyst - Product
Description:
Patrianna is a fast-scaling product development company headquartered in Gibraltar, with a dynamic, global team powering our growth. We operate at the intersection of technology and entertainment, building innovative solutions that shape the future of social gaming and deliver outstanding experiences to millions of players worldwide.

We're driven by speed, ambition, and bold ideas. At our core, we're product creators and problem-solvers who thrive in a high-performance environment. We're looking for exceptional talent—smart, adaptable, and motivated individuals eager to make an impact, scale business functions at pace, and continuously improve.

The Role

Transform data into actionable insights that drive product decisions and enhance player experiences across our gaming platforms.

What You Will Be Doing


Guide product team members through complex data landscapes, providing valuable insights that support critical decision-making
Conduct deep-dive analyses of user behavior, app performance, and product features to boost player acquisition, engagement, and retention
Ensure top-tier behavioral data collection by collaborating with analytics developers on tracking implementation and issue resolution
Develop and maintain dashboards, reports, and alerting systems for real-time monitoring of key conversion metrics
Present insights clearly and effectively to stakeholders across the organization
Debug data issues and optimize tracking mechanisms to maintain data integrity


What We Are Looking For


Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities with exceptional attention to detail
Critical thinking mindset with proactive approach and high reliability
Solid SQL skills for querying and manipulating databases
Experience creating detailed reports and dashboards using tools like Looker or Tableau
Familiarity with digital analytics platforms such as Snowplow and GA4, plus tracking mechanisms and data layers
Experience with data modeling in Dataform and session replay/autotracking platforms is a bonus


Why you will love it

You'll be at the heart of product innovation, where your insights directly shape gaming experiences for millions of players. In our high-growth environment, you'll have the freedom to take ownership of impactful projects while collaborating with brilliant minds who value your expertise. Every analysis you deliver will drive real business decisions and help us stay ahead in the competitive gaming landscape.

Equal Opportunities

We hire based on skills, drive, and ideas—nothing else. Your background, gender, age, race, ethnicity, disability, sexual orientation, religion, neurodiversity, or educational path will never be a barrier to joining us. We also welcome candidates from non-traditional career journeys and value diverse perspectives that challenge conventional thinking. Diversity fuels our innovation, collaboration, and growth, and we're committed to creating an environment where everyone can contribute their best work and thrive.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
About Euromonitor:

Euromonitor International leads the world in data analytics and research into markets, industries, economies and consumers. We provide truly global insight and data on thousands of products and services; we are the first destination for organisations seeking growth. With our guidance, our clients can make bold, strategic decisions with confidence.

The Analytics team is hiring a Data Analyst to help improve the analytics processes and code underpinning Euromonitor's internal Forecast Model, supporting updates to our Passport data.

The role requires a strong programming foundation in Python or R (preferred), an understanding of cloud computing, and a genuine interest in data analytics. Alongside internal forecasting work, the role may also support client consulting projects, applying analytical solutions from problem definition through to insight delivery.

Key Responsibilities


Develop and enhance code for data processing, mathematical transformations, and statistical analysis
Contribute to peer reviews and solution design discussions
Translate analytical outputs into clear conclusions
Stay up to date with modern programming and cloud technologies
Maintain clear documentation and share best practices across the team


Requirements


Advanced Python knowledge
Proficiency with R (preferred)
Advanced knowledge of SQL
Ability to automate complex data analysis processes
Understanding of data flows, migrations, ETLs, pipelines, and other similar tools
Understanding of process scaling and parallelization
Understanding on how to write a production-ready code
A knowledge in econometrics, statistics and economics.
Knowledge of Dockers, Rest API, JSON data format would be an advantage
Knowledge of cloud computing services platforms providers (Azure, GCS, other)
Experience in SPARQL, JavaScript, HTML or similar would be an advantage
A genuine interest in Artificial Intelligence and related fields like data science and Machine learning would be an advantage.


Nice to have:


M.A. degree in computer science, statistics, econometrics, data science, mathematics, economics, physics or similar field. Highly skilled candidates with a B.A. will also be considered.


Benefits


Flexible work time with the perfect work/life balance
Competitive salary with wide variety of benefits like longer holidays, additional health insurance, social responsibility initiatives you can take part in, office perks
Intelligent and fun colleagues working in a constructive and inspiring atmosphere
Salary: EUR 30,000 - EUR 35,000


Why work for Euromonitor

Our values


We act with integrity
We are curious about the world
We are stronger together
We seek to empower
We find strength in diversity


International: not only do we have a very multinational workforce in each office but we are all dealing with our 16 offices worldwide on a daily basis. With 16 offices globally there are regular opportunities for international transfer.

Hardworking but sociable: our staff know how to work hard but also how to enjoy themselves! We pride ourselves on creating an appropriate work-life balance, with flexible hours and regular socialising including frequent after work meet ups, summer and Christmas parties and a whole range of sports and other groups to be involved with.

Committed to making a difference: We think that people are looking for something worthwhile in a company beyond the workplace. Our extensive Corporate Social Responsibility Programme gives each member of staff two volunteering days a year in addition to holidays. It sees us reaching out into the local community with our mentoring, group volunteering, and fundraising initiatives as well as supporting international charities through our website sales, matching staff sponsorship fundraising, and carbon offsetting all our flights, amongst many other activities.

Excellent benefits: we offer highly competitive salaries, healthcare insurance, food vouchers, saving fund, plus generous holiday allowances and in many offices a Core Hours policy allowing flexible start and finish times to each day.

Opportunities to grow: we offer extensive training and development opportunities at all levels. The vast majority of our managers and directors have been promoted from within and many have moved across departments as well as upwards. We pride ourselves on identifying and rewarding talent.

Equal Employment Opportunity Statement: Euromonitor International does not discriminate in employment on the basis of race, colour, religion, sex, national origin, political affiliation, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status, disability and genetic information, age, membership in an employee organization, or other non-merit factor.

Title: Data Reporting Analyst
Description:
We are seeking a Reporting/Data Analyst with expertise in data transformation and Power BI to join our growing analytics team. The ideal candidate will be responsible for transforming complex datasets into actionable insights and visually compelling dashboards. This role also involves regular interaction with end users to gather reporting requirements and ensure solutions align with business needs. Strong organizational skills and basic project management capabilities are essential to prioritize tasks and manage the full reporting lifecycle—from requirements gathering through to final delivery.

Key Responsibilities:

Develop, maintain, and optimize Power BI reports, dashboards, and visualizations to support business decision-making.
Perform data transformation and cleaning using tools such as Power Query, DAX, and SQL to ensure data accuracy and consistency.
Design and maintain data models and ETL processes to support reliable and scalable reporting solutions.
With assistance from other internal teams, collaborate with business users and stakeholders to define reporting needs, gather requirements, and translate them into technical specifications.
Manage reporting projects from initial requirements gathering through to development, testing, delivery, and post-deployment support.
Use basic project management skills to prioritize tasks, manage timelines, and track progress across multiple requests or initiatives.
Ensure adherence to data governance, security, and quality standards.
Troubleshoot and resolve issues related to data integrity, report performance, and usability.
Maintain documentation of data sources, models, processes, and dashboards for continuity and knowledge sharing.




Education/Experience:

Bachelor’s degree in Data Science, Computer Science, Information Systems, Statistics, or a related field.
1+ years of experience in a data analyst or reporting analyst role.
Strong hands-on experience with Power BI, including DAX, Power Query, and the Power BI Service.
Proven ability in data transformation, data preparation, and data modelling.
Proficient in SQL for querying and transforming data from relational databases.
Comfortable interacting with business stakeholders and translating business needs into technical requirements.
Experience working independently on reporting projects from start to finish.




Desired Skills:

Experience with ETL tools such as Power BI Service Dataflows, AWS Glue, etc.
Microsoft Power BI certification.
Experience in Agile or Scrum environments.
Experience in Power BI Paginated Reports a distinct advantage

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
About The Team

Kiloverse is the operational force that helps ventures grow — building the systems, teams, and infrastructure that turn ideas into scalable businesses. Behind every fast-growing product, there’s a group of people who think like owners, act fast, and execute with precision.

Here, you’ll find people who want to break big in their careers — working side by side with entrepreneurs and founders to build ventures that redefine the health, wellness, beauty, or travel industries.

Data team, as part of Kiloverse, the operational force behind every impactful Kilo solution, provides advanced data engineering, modelling and reporting solutions for the whole group.

Currently, we are looking for an experienced data analyst to work on product data modelling and reporting to help scale the business even further.

Get ready to


Employ GCP tools (dbt, Airflow and Looker) to enhance data quality, efficiency, and delivery of accurate and timely data
Identify, investigate and solve data issues: data quality, data discrepancies, missing data
Contribute to the development and improvement of data solutions
Leverage the power of dbt for overcoming complex modelling problems with a focus on performance, robustness and scalability
Work on prevention and alerting solutions
Adopt and refine our best practices e.g. naming convention, data modeling, and data quality testing
Communicate with cross-functional teams and non-technical stakeholders in a clear and structured manner
Assist and support other team members in the design, development, and implementation of data warehousing, reporting, and analytics solutions
Take ownership of tasks and initiatives


We expect you to


Have at least 2 years of proven SQL skills: ability to join and manipulate data of various types (String, Integer, JSON, Array), write parameterized scripts, debug SQL code
Know ETL and warehousing concepts
Have strong communication skills: timely, clear, and consistent sharing of information, work progress, bottlenecks and findings
Have curious and growth-oriented mindset towards constant learning
Be capable to understand, tackle, and communicate problems from both technical and business perspectives
Be fluent in English and Lithuanian


Salary

Gross salary range is 3000 - 4000 EUR/month.

Location

We have plenty of amazing workspaces you can choose from: our awesome headquarters in Vilnius, and super cool hubs in Kaunas, Klaipėda, and Riga!

For Vilnius: 4 days onsite, 1 flexible day remote. Meanwhile, in Kaunas: onsite, hybrid, or remote options are available depending on the role.

Speaking of perks:

Own your wellness

Stay sharp and well in your own way – health insurance (after probation), on-site physiotherapist, office gym, and fitness classes keep you moving. Plus 7 extra days off, 3 for weddings, hybrid work, and the freedom to work 2 months from anywhere in the world.

Boost your daily life Own your wellness

Every breakthrough needs a spark and our daily setup reflects that. Pet-friendly office, fresh breakfasts, hot lunches, drawers and fridges stocked with snacks and food – for a quick bite or even a team-cooked meal together. Rooftop and cellar events keep things lively, while 10 social clubs give space to dive into passions and try new things. Add a public transport card for Vilnius and Perks.lt perks for smoother days.

Shape your growth path

Builders never stop learning. Coaching, mentorship, training, conferences, online courses, subscriptions, books – you’ve got a €1100 yearly budget to fuel personal or team growth. You pick the path – we’ll back you all the way.

Drive global impact

This is where ambition scales. Build impact across 30+ brands worldwide. Launch bold projects. Even step in as a co-founder – we reward those who push further. And if you bring in more great people, you get €1500 for every successful referral.


additional conditions apply based on your residence location.

This is where ambition scales. Build impact across 30+ brands worldwide. Launch bold projects. Even step in as a co-founder – we reward those who push further. And if you bring in more great people, you get €1500 for every successful referral.

  • additional conditions apply based on your residence location.

Title: BI Engineer
Description:
Description

Position at LeoVegas Group

About The Role

Are you driven by data and have a talent for transforming insights into actionable strategies? We're seeking a talented BI Engineer to join our innovative team. In this role, you'll be instrumental in designing and developing business intelligence solutions that empower our organization to make informed decisions.

If you have a passion for data analytics, a knack for problem-solving, and a desire to drive business growth, apply now to be a part of our data-driven journey!

YOU WILL BE RESPONSIBLE FOR:


Creating complex functions and stored procedures, analyse queries, develop security protocols and resolve problems based on business needs
Ensuring data accuracy, performance, and usability of existing dashboards, implementing improvements as needed.
Working closely with data engineers to define data requirements, optimise data pipelines, and ensure efficient data delivery.
Utilising Tableau to create compelling visualisations that effectively communicate insights to stakeholders at all levels.
Adhering to and promoting data quality standards, ensuring data integrity and security.
Developing and maintaining robust BI solutions that meet the evolving needs of the business, exploring and integrating new technologies as needed.
Effectively communicating data-driven insights and recommendations to both technical and non-technical audiences.
Working closely with various departments (e.g., marketing, finance, product) to understand their data needs and provide actionable insights.
Continuously learning and exploring new technologies and methodologies in the BI landscape, sharing relevant advancements with the team.


OUR SUCCESSFUL CANDIDATE WILL HAVE THE FOLLOWING:

Essential Skills


Experience in the iGaming industry.
2-5 years of relevant experience in a similar role and strong academic background.
Ability to explain complex technical concepts to diverse stakeholders, including non-technical team members and senior management.
Ability to produce clear and concise documentation, ensuring that technical details were easily understandable by all parties involved.
Advanced knowledge of SQL, preferably knowledge of Python and Airflow.
Experience with BI tools, preferably Tableau.


NICE TO HAVES


Experience working with different database systems.


Who We Are

At the core of LeoVegas Group is Team Leo. Our culture is our foundation and is what enables us to innovate, build, and lead as we trailblaze our way through the igaming industry. We’re a team of over 2000 innovators, initiators, and groundbreakers working in a fast-paced and agile environment across 19 offices worldwide.

BENEFITS:


Hybrid work policy
4 weeks of Workation (T&C apply)
Well-being allowance to support your active lifestyle
Private health insurance
Discounts across a range of retailers, gyms, bars & restaurants
We offer an employee assistance program that can provide help and guidance during challenging moments.
For non-Malta residents, we offer a relocation package with 3 weeks of accommodation and flights.


JOIN US!

In our pride, we empower our teammates to find their roar and run with their wildest ideas. We don’t wait for things to happen; we pounce and make it happen!

Would you be a good fit for the Leo Pride - give us a roar!

Title: Commercial Analytics Specialist
Description:
KM Malta Airlines Limited, the national airline of the Maltese Islands, is seeking to recruit a Commercial Analytics Specialist within the Commercial department.




Purpose of Position




The Commercial Analytics Specialist is a key conduit of KPI management and all commercial analytics development that relies on utilising and interpreting data to make effective, reliable business decisions based on accurate information.
Accountable for the setting and management of all metrics for good control of revenue performance and strategic compliance across commercial.
The role is to act as a hub-and-spoke model that delivers analytics and algorithms that deliver competitive advantage.




Responsibilities




Develop all aspects of corporate strategy, data, information and analytics strategy.
Actively contribute to the development of the overall commercial strategy and business plan for the airline.
Take responsibility for construct and distribution of revenue budget targets by partnering with Network Sales and Revenue Management.
Own, create, and develop all technical aspects related to data tracking, delivery, and reporting.
Ensure that regulatory compliance and data quality are maintained throughout the design and management of data and information systems, set standards to manage both physical and logical data.
Create, document and present technical designs, technical solutions to relevant stakeholders.
Ensure communication of sales actions and results within the team and to stakeholders in Sales, Revenue Management, Network Planning, Distribution, and Finance.
Develop and foster relationships with reporting and analytics communities across the organisation identifying opportunities to share best practice and develop collaborative strategies that deliver group benefits.




Experience, Qualifications and Skills




Extensive background in data mining and / or statistical analysis.
Able to understand various data structures and common methods in data transformation.
Experience with programming languages, such as Java / Saas / Python / SQL.
Familiarity in Revenue Forecasting, Revenue & Route Planning, Commercial Data Architectures, Airline Data Warehouse, Revenue Accounting, NDC, One Order.
Experience in the airline industry is desired.
Degree MQF Level 6 or higher or other professional qualification in Computer Science, Statistics, Applied Math / Data Science or related field (essential).
Computing & Analytics: Requires a basic knowledge on Cloud platforms, SQL & NoSQL, Tableau, PowerBI, MicroStrategy, Project Management, Business Analysis, Agile, Data Modeling & Warehousing, Data Mining.
Strong analytical skills, problem-solving and decision-making capability.




Jobsplus Permit: 283/2024




Closing Date: 02nd August 2026

Title: Growth Analyst
Description:
About Bolt

Bolt is shaping a future where cities are built for people, not cars. What started as an ambitious project of a 19-year-old in 2013 has grown into a global mobility platform used by more than 200 million customers and 4.5 million driver and courier partners across 50 countries.

From ride-hailing and food delivery to scooters, e-bikes, and car-sharing, Bolt helps people move through cities every day. Small, autonomous teams drive this work, combining the speed and ownership of a startup with the scale of a global technology company backed by more than €1bn in funding.

And we're just getting started.

Why join Bolt?


Build the future of mobility and help transform how people live and move in cities.
Tackle challenges at scale that span technology, operations, regulation, and growth.
Take real ownership early. Small, lean teams mean your decisions matter from day one, and you'll learn fast alongside some of the best people in the industry.
Join one of Europe's leading technology companies, backed by world-class investors including Sequoia.


About The Role

As a Growth Analyst, you will take ownership of our campaign spending decisions and contribute to the marketplace's success by meticulously analysing the return on investment (ROI). With your expertise, we'll be able to accurately determine the most strategic allocation of resources, including the exact location, timing, method, and amount spent on campaigns.

You will be working closely with other team members of the Growth domain, as well as Expansion Managers, Marketing and Operations teams. A Data Analyst will need to have good attention to detail and communication skills to succeed. Solid business sense and analytical mindset? You might be the campaign magician we’ve been waiting for.

Main Tasks And Responsibilities


Own campaign spending decisions for your assigned markets and optimise such decisions to help the country team reach their growth targets
Wrangle complex data sets to come up with testing hypotheses, setting up A/B tests (and other experiments) and analysing results using statistical techniques in order to guide your decision-making
Proactively working together with Expansion Managers, Marketing, Operations and local teams to drive collaboration and get the most out of our investments
Identify valuable opportunities in the team and contribute to global campaigns team playbook, processes and product requests


About You


BS in Mathematics, Economics, Computer Science, Information Management or Statistics
At least 3 years of experience in an analytical role dealing with large volumes of data. Experience in consulting, pricing/discount strategy and/or on-demand is a plus
Solid grasp of statistics and demonstrated experience with A/B testing
Amazing SQL and Excel skills. Experience with Python or R is a plus
Experience with some BI tools and data visualisation like Tableau, Power BI or Looker
Fluent in English


Why You’ll Love It Here


Accelerate your professional growth with unique career opportunities.
Enjoy a rewarding salary and stock options, knowing that as Bolt succeeds, so do you.
Take care of your physical and mental health with our wellness perks.
Celebrate 5 years at Bolt with a 1-month paid sabbatical to recharge.
Connect with colleagues at annual company events and smaller team gatherings.
Balance flexibility and in-person collaboration with our hybrid model, including at least 12 monthly in-office days.


Some perks may differ depending on your location and role.

At Bolt, using AI daily is a baseline expectation for everyone. If there’s a faster, smarter way to do your work, we expect you to find it. You’re still responsible for the output, so the standard stays high when using AI tools.

Experience is great, but what we really look for is drive, intelligence, and integrity. So even if you don’t tick every box, please consider applying!

Everyone is welcome

Bolt is an equal opportunity employer. We are committed to building a diverse and inclusive workplace where all qualified applicants are considered for employment without regard to race, colour, nationality, religion, gender or gender identity, sexual orientation, age, or disability.

We welcome applications from all backgrounds and are happy to make reasonable accommodations throughout our hiring process. If you require any adjustments, please let us know.
