Title: Analytics Engineer
Description:
At Jacobs, we're challenging today to reinvent tomorrow by solving the world's most critical problems for thriving cities, resilient environments, mission-critical outcomes, operational advancement, scientific discovery and cutting-edge manufacturing, turning abstract ideas into realities that transform the world for good.

Your impact

This is a great time to join Jacobs’ Dublin team as we scale our analytics capability within transportation. You’d be working on BusConnects, a major infrastructure programme that will directly improve daily commutes and make the city more sustainable. It’s a real greenfield opportunity as the team is still maturing, so you’ll help shape processes, influence how analytics is done, and have a tangible impact from day one. There’s strong growth in the pipeline, with leadership heavily investing in this space.

With a chance to make a real difference to real peoples day-to-day lives, why wouldn't you want to make an impact?

Day to Day


Frame ambiguous business problems into structured data analysis questions and testable hypotheses
Apply core Analytics techniques including descriptive, inferential, and predictive analysis
Perform statistical analysis using regression, hypothesis testing, sampling, and confidence intervals
Conduct exploratory data analysis to identify patterns, trends, and anomalies in complex datasets
Build reproducible analytical workflows using Python and notebook environments
Use AI tools to enhance data analysis, code generation, and insight discovery with careful validation
Query, validate, and manipulate data using SQL across multiple data sources
Create clear data visualisations and insight-led narratives tailored to different audiences
Communicate analytical findings in a way that supports decision-making without overstating certainty
Collaborate with stakeholders to refine data requirements and ensure outputs are relevant and accurate
Assess data quality, bias, and limitations, and clearly articulate what the data can and cannot support
Work within data governance, privacy, and ethical frameworks at all times



Technical Stack


Python for data analysis including pandas, numpy, scipy, statsmodels
SQL for data extraction, transformation, and validation
Jupyter or similar notebook tools for reproducible data analysis
Azure-based data platforms including Fabric, Synapse, and Databricks
Data pipeline tooling such as Azure Data Factory
AI and copilot tools to support and accelerate analytical workflows
Data visualisation using matplotlib, seaborn, Plotly or similar
Version control and CI/CD practices using Git



What’s In It For You


Apply your Data Analyst skills to deliver insight on major infrastructure and transformation programmes
Accelerate your career as a Data Analyst in a high-growth, high-visibility environment
Work alongside experienced analysts, data professionals, and domain experts
Develop both your technical data skills and your stakeholder-facing consulting capability
Be part of a collaborative culture where data-driven thinking is valued and encouraged
Gain exposure to diverse datasets, sectors, and analytical challenges
Learn to apply data analysis in complex, real-world environments with measurable impact
Take ownership of your work and grow your capability in a supportive, forward-thinking team



Benefits


Competitive salary aligned to experience
33 days paid holiday per annum with the option to purchase more
Stock purchase options
Inclusive employee networks and diverse community
Family planning support including fertility services
Enhanced parental leave and support programmes
Income protection for long-term security
40 hour working week with flexible working patterns
Wellbeing hub with access to apps such as Calm and Daily Burn
Professional development, paid memberships, and CPD opportunities
Charity match funding, volunteering time, and donations



#Digital&Data

Here's what you'll need

Key Skills & Experience Required


Strong experience with
SQL
Python
Microsoft Fabric, Synapse, and Databricks
Exposure to AI and advanced analytics
Familiarity with Generative AI (AI augmentation concepts)
Experience using analytical tools that support AI-driven insights
Communication & Storytelling
Strong ability to communicate insights clearly
Capable of translating data into meaningful business stories
Data Management & Governance
Solid understanding of data quality and assurance principles
Knowledge of data completeness and data lineage
Engineering Best Practices
Experience with version control (e.g. Git)
Familiarity with CI/CD pipelines and deployment practices
Cloud Environment
Primary focus on Microsoft Azure
Experience with AWS or Google BigQuery is transferable
Leadership & Growth
Will be expected to support and mentor others

Joining Jacobs not only connects you locally but globally. Our values stand on a foundation of safety, integrity, inclusion and belonging. We put people at the heart of our business, and we truly believe that by supporting one another through our culture of caring, we all succeed. We value positive mental health and a sense of belonging for all employees.

With safety and flexibility always top of mind, we’ve gone beyond traditional ways of working so you have the support, means and space to maximize your potential. You’ll uncover flexible working arrangements, benefits, and opportunities, from well-being benefits to our global giving and volunteering program, to exploring new and inventive ways to help our clients make the world a better place. No matter what drives you, you’ll discover how you can cultivate, nurture, and achieve your goals – all at a single global company. Find out more about life at Jacobs.

We aim to embed inclusion and belonging in everything we do. We know that if we are inclusive, we’re more connected and creative. We accept people for who they are, and we champion the richness of different perspectives, lived experiences and backgrounds in the workplace, as a source of learning and innovation. We are committed to building vibrant communities within Jacobs, including through our Jacobs Employee Networks, Communities of Practice and our Find Your Community initiatives, allowing every employee to find connection, purpose, and belonging. Find out more about our Jacobs Employee Networks here.

Jacobs partners with VERCIDA to help us attract and retain talent from a wide range of backgrounds. For greater online accessibility please visit https//www.vercida.com/uk/employers/jacobs to view and access our roles.

As a disability confident employer, we will interview disabled candidates who best meet the criteria. We welcome applications from candidates who are seeking flexible working and from those who may not meet all the listed requirements for a role.

We value collaboration and believe that in-person interactions are crucial for both our culture and client delivery. We empower employees with our hybrid working policy, allowing them to split their work week between Jacobs offices/projects and remote locations enabling them to deliver their best work.

Your application experience is important to us, and we’re keen to adapt to make every interaction even better. If you require further support or reasonable adjustments with regards to the recruitment process (for example, you require the application form in a different format), please contact the team via Careers Support.

HTML:
At Jacobs, we're challenging today to reinvent tomorrow by solving the world's most critical problems for thriving cities, resilient environments, mission-critical outcomes, operational advancement, scientific discovery and cutting-edge manufacturing, turning abstract ideas into realities that transform the world for good.

Your impact

This is a great time to join Jacobs’ Dublin team as we scale our analytics capability within transportation. You’d be working on BusConnects, a major infrastructure programme that will directly improve daily commutes and make the city more sustainable. It’s a real greenfield opportunity as the team is still maturing, so you’ll help shape processes, influence how analytics is done, and have a tangible impact from day one. There’s strong growth in the pipeline, with leadership heavily investing in this space.

With a chance to make a real difference to real peoples day-to-day lives, why wouldn't you want to make an impact?

Day to Day

  • Frame ambiguous business problems into structured data analysis questions and testable hypotheses
  • Apply core Analytics techniques including descriptive, inferential, and predictive analysis
  • Perform statistical analysis using regression, hypothesis testing, sampling, and confidence intervals
  • Conduct exploratory data analysis to identify patterns, trends, and anomalies in complex datasets
  • Build reproducible analytical workflows using Python and notebook environments
  • Use AI tools to enhance data analysis, code generation, and insight discovery with careful validation
  • Query, validate, and manipulate data using SQL across multiple data sources
  • Create clear data visualisations and insight-led narratives tailored to different audiences
  • Communicate analytical findings in a way that supports decision-making without overstating certainty
  • Collaborate with stakeholders to refine data requirements and ensure outputs are relevant and accurate
  • Assess data quality, bias, and limitations, and clearly articulate what the data can and cannot support
  • Work within data governance, privacy, and ethical frameworks at all times


Technical Stack

  • Python for data analysis including pandas, numpy, scipy, statsmodels
  • SQL for data extraction, transformation, and validation
  • Jupyter or similar notebook tools for reproducible data analysis
  • Azure-based data platforms including Fabric, Synapse, and Databricks
  • Data pipeline tooling such as Azure Data Factory
  • AI and copilot tools to support and accelerate analytical workflows
  • Data visualisation using matplotlib, seaborn, Plotly or similar
  • Version control and CI/CD practices using Git


What’s In It For You

  • Apply your Data Analyst skills to deliver insight on major infrastructure and transformation programmes
  • Accelerate your career as a Data Analyst in a high-growth, high-visibility environment
  • Work alongside experienced analysts, data professionals, and domain experts
  • Develop both your technical data skills and your stakeholder-facing consulting capability
  • Be part of a collaborative culture where data-driven thinking is valued and encouraged
  • Gain exposure to diverse datasets, sectors, and analytical challenges
  • Learn to apply data analysis in complex, real-world environments with measurable impact
  • Take ownership of your work and grow your capability in a supportive, forward-thinking team


Benefits

  • Competitive salary aligned to experience
  • 33 days paid holiday per annum with the option to purchase more
  • Stock purchase options
  • Inclusive employee networks and diverse community
  • Family planning support including fertility services
  • Enhanced parental leave and support programmes
  • Income protection for long-term security
  • 40 hour working week with flexible working patterns
  • Wellbeing hub with access to apps such as Calm and Daily Burn
  • Professional development, paid memberships, and CPD opportunities
  • Charity match funding, volunteering time, and donations


#Digital&Data

Here's what you'll need

Key Skills & Experience Required

  • Strong experience with
    • SQL
    • Python
    • Microsoft Fabric, Synapse, and Databricks
  • Exposure to AI and advanced analytics
    • Familiarity with Generative AI (AI augmentation concepts)
    • Experience using analytical tools that support AI-driven insights
  • Communication & Storytelling
    • Strong ability to communicate insights clearly
    • Capable of translating data into meaningful business stories
  • Data Management & Governance
    • Solid understanding of data quality and assurance principles
    • Knowledge of data completeness and data lineage
  • Engineering Best Practices
    • Experience with version control (e.g. Git)
    • Familiarity with CI/CD pipelines and deployment practices
  • Cloud Environment
    • Primary focus on Microsoft Azure
    • Experience with AWS or Google BigQuery is transferable
  • Leadership & Growth
    • Will be expected to support and mentor others

Title: Analytics Engineer
Description:
Ocean Finance is a portfolio brand of Intelligent Lending Limited, a consumer credit broker powerhouse operating the TotallyMoney, Ocean Finance, CredAbility Luv Car Loans, and Binq brands. Our mission is to become the UK’s leading credit platform for people not served by the mainstream — helping them make informed decisions, access the right products, and improve their financial wellbeing over their lifetime. We do this by helping our customers make informed decisions and access personal finance products.

If our mission sounds interesting, please keep reading!




You’ll join the Central Intelligence Unit (CIU) within our Data team. CIU sources the most relevant intelligence and ensures its optimal deployment — through machine learning, analytics and decisioning that shape product and corporate strategy. Specifically, you’ll sit in the Ocean Squad, working alongside data scientists, analytics and AI engineers on the analytical frameworks, decisioning models that both elevate our product, and propel our marketing efforts for Ocean.




This is a hybrid role that sits between analytics engineering and data analysis. Roughly half your time will be spent building and maintaining the data foundations the team relies on — clean, reliable, well-modelled datasets — and the other half turning those datasets into dazzling dashboards and gleaning insights that directly inform business decisions.

It’s ideal for someone early in their career (a recent graduate, up to ~2 years’ experience) who enjoys both the engineering craft of making data effective as well as trustworthy, and the curiosity of digging into it to answer real commercial questions. You’ll learn modern data tooling and analytics practice in a fast-moving environment, with strong support from experienced engineers and scientists around you.

The mission statement of CIU is “To improve customer and business outcomes by ensuring the sourcing of the most relevant intelligence, and its optimal deployment”. We aim to be a leading force in creating value for the customer and the business, through best-in-class analytics and decisioning that shape the product offering, as well as corporate strategy.




A day in the life of this role could look like…

Analyzing complex datasets to uncover customer behavior patterns and product performance insights, translating these findings into clear and actionable commercial recommendations for stakeholders
Presenting insights and recommendations to senior stakeholders, telling compelling stories with data that drive action
Writing and optimizing SQL queries to extract and analyze data from multiple sources, ensuring accuracy and efficiency
Collaborating with business teams to understand their analytical needs and develop solutions that drive business value
Designing and helping to build automated dashboards and reports in tools like Power BI or Databricks to track key business metrics and support data-driven decision making
Build and maintain data models and transformation pipelines that turn raw data into clean, well-documented, analysis-ready datasets.
Creating and maintaining BI chatbots that leverage curated datasets to empower non-technical business stakeholders to self-serve
Analysing market trends and competitive landscape




You’ll also have the chance to get involved in other projects like:

Evaluating the potential impact of greenfield projects, such as the integration of new products, verticals, or customer journeys
Set up analytical framework for new products or enhance the existing ones by suggesting new KPIs / analyses
Understanding the key drivers behind past business performance and succinctly articulating the story to the board
Contributing to the design and implementation of our machine learning and predictive analytics initiatives




What we’re looking for

A degree in a quantitative or technical discipline (e.g. Data Science, Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Economics, Engineering) — or equivalent practical experience.
0–2 years’ experience in an analytics, data or engineering role, including internships, placements or strong personal/academic projects.
Solid SQL skills, and confidence working with data to answer questions.
Some exposure to a scripting language (ideally Python) for data manipulation and analysis.
An eye for data quality and structure — you care about getting things right, not just an answer.
Clear written and verbal communication; able to explain analysis simply.
Curiosity and commercial awareness — you want to understand the “why” behind the numbers.
Proactive and ownership-minded; comfortable in a fast-paced environment.




Nice to have

Experience with a cloud data warehouse / lakehouse (e.g. Databricks, BigQuery, Snowflake, Redshift).
Familiarity with a BI / visualisation tool (e.g. Looker, Power BI, Tableau).
Exposure to version control (Git) and collaborative analytics workflows.
An interest in the credit market, financial services or consumer finance.




Most importantly, you’ll live and breathe our values: Openness, Trust, Humility, Professionalism, Respect, and Passion.




Benefits

Pay you competitively and review your salary every year
Welcome you into our awesome team (dare we say the best? We do, yes!)
Bring the kind of vibe where we want you to share your ideas, even if it’s not “your area”
Provide you with all the kit you need to work from home. Of course, you can also work from our Manchester or Cyprus offices, too.
Help you plan for life after work with our pension scheme
Take care of your health with eye test vouchers, and our employee assistance programme
Give you 25 days holiday (to start with), as well as local public holidays and a day off on your birthday
Support you with training, mentoring and anything else you need to further your development and hit your career goals
Invite you to regular online and in-person team socials, as well as the occasional team retreat somewhere exotic
Help you help others with optional “community” days for volunteering
Provide you flexibility of benefit with the Ocean Health Plan

  • Invite you to regular online and in-person team socials, as well as the occasional team retreat somewhere exotic
  • Help you help others with optional “community” days for volunteering

Title: Data Analyst (Dealing / Trading Analytics)
Description:
We are a leading trading platform that is ambitiously expanding to the four corners of the globe. Our top-rated products have won prestigious industry awards for their cutting-edge technology and seamless client experience. We deliver only the best, so we are always in search of the best people to join our ever-growing talented team.

We are hiring a Data Analyst to partner closely with our Dealing team to improve trading and operational decision-making through data. This person will build and maintain analytics and reporting capabilities, translate business needs into well-scoped data solutions, and deliver actionable insights to support product evaluation and day-to-day operations. Strong SQL and Python skills are required, along with proven experience in trading/fintech/financial markets.

Responsibilities:


Partner day-to-day with the Dealing team to support trading and operational workflows with data and insights
Build and enhance analytics and reporting capabilities to support product evaluation, trading performance, and operational monitoring
Gather business requirements and apply market/trading context to scope data requests, define metrics, and recommend practical solutions
Analyze large, disconnected datasets to synthesize insights and highlight risks/opportunities
Collaborate with business leaders and cross-functional stakeholders to enable market-facing solutions powered by data
Communicate findings clearly (written and verbal) and deliver solutions reliably and on time
Ensure data quality by validating inputs, monitoring key metrics, and improving definitions/documentation where needed



Requirements:


Strong SQL and database experience (querying, joining, modeling/structuring data for analysis)
Advanced working knowledge and experience with Python
Basic statistical knowledge and ability to apply it appropriately in analysis and reporting
Demonstrated experience manipulating, processing, and extracting value from large, messy, and/or disconnected datasets
Strong understanding of trading/dealing concepts and market microstructure (execution, pricing, liquidity, slippage, exposure, hedging basics)
Familiarity with anti-fraud / trading abuse patterns and investigation workflows
Experience working with API connections (REST/WebSocket)
Experience with machine learning / deep learning / data science methods (nice to have)



What you will get in return:


Competitive Salary: We believe great work deserves great pay! Your skills and talents will be rewarded with a salary that makes you feel valued and motivated
Work-Life Harmony: Join a company that genuinely cares about you - because your life outside of work matters just as much as your time on the clock
Generous Time Off: Need a breather? Our annual leave policy lets you recharge and enjoy life outside of work without a worry
Employee Referral Program: Love working here? Share the love! Bring your talented friends on board and get rewarded for growing our awesome team
Comprehensive Health & Pension Benefits: From medical insurance to pension plans, we’ve got your back. Plus, location-specific benefits and perks!
Workation Wonderland: Live your digital nomad dreams with 30 extra days to work remotely from anywhere in the world (some restrictions apply). Adventure awaits!
Volunteer Days: Make a difference! Take two additional paid days each year to support causes you care about and give back to the community



Be a key player at the forefront of the digital assets movement, propelling your career to new heights! Join a dynamic and rapidly expanding company that values and rewards talent, initiative, and creativity. Work alongside one of the most brilliant teams in the industry.

Title: Analytics Engineer, Marketplace Strategy & Planning
Description:
Brief Info About Vinted

Our mission is to make second-hand the first choice, and we're looking for people who want to help us get there. Every day, we work together to help our members buy and sell pre-loved clothing and lifestyle items, giving each piece a second life – or even a third.

The Vinted Group is made up of three business units that support this mission:

Vinted Marketplace is Europe’s leading platform for second-hand fashion and a go-to destination for all kinds of pre-loved items, with a growing range of categories. Our platform connects millions of members across 20+ markets, helping great items find a new life.

Vinted Go enhances the shipping experience with a vast network of over 500,000 pick-up and drop-off points, partnering with more than 60 carriers across Europe, with added services like item verification for peace of mind on high-value pieces.

Vinted Pay is the newest part of the Vinted Group, dedicated to bringing secure, reliable payments to buyers and sellers across Europe. Seamlessly integrated into the Vinted app, it helps keep every transaction safe, efficient, and easy for our members.

Founded in 2008 in Lithuania, Vinted began as a way for friends to find new homes for clothes they no longer needed. In 2019, we became Lithuania's first unicorn! Today, our headquarters remain in Vilnius, and we've grown with offices across Europe, supported by a team of over 2,000 people.

Information about the position

As an Analytics Engineer in the Marketplace Strategy & Planning team, you will build analytical data models, dashboards, and other data products that provide insight into the performance of Vinted’s Marketplace and help shape our strategy.


About the team: Marketplace Strategy & Planning domain identifies growth opportunities for Vinted’s Marketplace in different markets, categories and other business opportunities. As the data team inside Strategy & Planning we quantify and measure our Marketplace strategy, including setting OKRs, defining metrics, tracking markets and competition, and forecasting of our performance. We work on highly visible strategic projects, with a wide range of stakeholders and data sources, and deliver the data products to support strategic decision making.
About the role: This is an end-to-end analytical role, where you will architect, build and maintain complete data products. You will work closely with two other Analytics Engineers in the Marketplace Strategy & Planning domain and be part of Vinted’s broader 40+ person Analytics Engineering Guild, benefiting from shared best practices, tooling, and regular knowledge sharing sessions.


In this position, you’ll


Architect and build scalable and reliable data models and pipelines in dbt, BigQuery, and Looker that turn internal and external source data into datasets and dashboards used for reporting, forecasting, and analyses.
Implement data quality checks, tests, and controls to improve the reliability and trustworthiness of our data.
Translate stakeholder needs into technical delivery by gathering requirements, aligning metric definitions, and turning them into well-documented models and metrics.
Create and enable data products that help stakeholders understand marketplace and competitor performance and make informed decisions.


About You


2+ years of working experience (or alternative activities resulting in a similar level of proficiency) as an Analytics Engineer or a closely related role (e.g. BI Engineer, Data Engineer or Data Analyst) with ownership of production pipelines
Strong technical skills, comfortable in our analytics engineering tech stack, which includes SQL/Google BigQuery, dbt, Python, Looker/LookML, Git.
Experience with data modeling and interested in developing and maintaining data products at scale, while balancing scalability, efficiency and reliability.
Excellent attention to detail. You care about data quality, edge cases, and consistency across pipelines, metrics and dashboards. You value documentation and clean, tidy code
Proactive and independent: you look for ways to iterate on and improve the data products you and your team own, and improve stakeholder experience.
You communicate clearly and proactively, and can be a reliable partner for business and technical stakeholders both within the domain and the broader organisation. You are comfortable in ambiguous and complex situations.


Studies show that women and members of underrepresented communities often apply for jobs only if they meet 100% of the qualifications. If you don’t recognise yourself in all of the above, you might still be an excellent candidate for this position. If this role excites you, we encourage you to apply.

Work perks


The opportunity to benefit from our share options programme
25 working days of holiday
Access to all the tools & tech needed for work
Home office support: we provide IT workstation equipment and a personal budget of up to €540 for home workplace furniture
Private health insurance
Digital mental and emotional health support and Employee Assistant Program (EAP)
Frequent team-building events
A personal monthly budget for shopping on Vinted
The opportunity to spend up to 90 days per year - 21 of which can be spent working outside of the EU - on workation
A dog-friendly office
In Vilnius office: Gym & in-house meals at friendly prices
In Kaunas office: a monthly lunch allowance, and a once-a-week provided in-house lunch and breakfast


Working at Vinted

Individual Learning Budget

We invest in your professional growth! As part of our commitment to continuous learning, we offer an annual learning budget to support your personal and career development through courses, certifications, workshops and more.

Hybrid Work

We’ve adopted a hybrid workplace model where 2 days in the office are recommended but not enforced. It’s up to you and your team to decide on the exact days you’ll spend working together in person.

Equal Opportunity

The Vinted Group is committed to building an inclusive workplace where people from all walks of life feel a sense of belonging. We welcome applications from people of all backgrounds, identities and life experiences. At Vinted, all applicants are treated fairly without regard to their race, age, religion or belief, sex, national origin, citizenship, gender identity, sexual orientation, disability, or any other protected characteristic.

The gross monthly salary range for this position is:

€3.433—€4.650 EUR

You will work closely with two other Analytics Engineers in the Marketplace Strategy & Planning domain and be part of Vinted’s broader 40+ person Analytics Engineering Guild, benefiting from shared best practices, tooling, and regular knowledge sharing sessions.

In this position, you’ll

  • Architect and build scalable and reliable data models and pipelines in dbt, BigQuery, and Looker that turn internal and external source data into datasets and dashboards used for reporting, forecasting, and analyses.
  • Implement data quality checks, tests, and controls to improve the reliability and trustworthiness of our data.
  • Translate stakeholder needs into technical delivery by gathering requirements, aligning metric definitions, and turning them into well-documented models and metrics.
  • Create and enable data products that help stakeholders understand marketplace and competitor performance and make informed decisions.

About You

  • 2+ years of working experience (or alternative activities resulting in a similar level of proficiency) as an Analytics Engineer or a closely related role (e.g. BI Engineer, Data Engineer or Data Analyst) with ownership of production pipelines
  • Strong technical skills, comfortable in our analytics engineering tech stack, which includes SQL/Google BigQuery, dbt, Python, Looker/LookML, Git.
  • Experience with data modeling and interested in developing and maintaining data products at scale, while balancing scalability, efficiency and reliability.
  • Excellent attention to detail. You care about data quality, edge cases, and consistency across pipelines, metrics and dashboards. You value documentation and clean, tidy code
  • Proactive and independent: you look for ways to iterate on and improve the data products you and your team own, and improve stakeholder experience.
  • You communicate clearly and proactively, and can be a reliable partner for business and technical stakeholders both within the domain and the broader organisation. You are comfortable in ambiguous and complex situations.

Studies show that women and members of underrepresented communities often apply for jobs only if they meet 100% of the qualifications. If you don’t recognise yourself in all of the above, you might still be an excellent candidate for this position. If this role excites you, we encourage you to apply.

Work perks

  • The opportunity to benefit from our share options programme
  • 25 working days of holiday
  • Access to all the tools & tech needed for work
  • Home office support: we provide IT workstation equipment and a personal budget of up to €540 for home workplace furniture
  • Private health insurance
  • Digital mental and emotional health support and Employee Assistant Program (EAP)
  • Frequent team-building events
  • A personal monthly budget for shopping on Vinted
  • The opportunity to spend up to 90 days per year - 21 of which can be spent working outside of the EU - on workation
  • A dog-friendly office
  • In Vilnius office: Gym & in-house meals at friendly prices
  • In Kaunas office: a monthly lunch allowance, and a once-a-week provided in-house lunch and breakfast

Working at Vinted

Individual Learning Budget

We invest in your professional growth! As part of our commitment to continuous learning, we offer an annual learning budget to support your personal and career development through courses, certifications, workshops and more.

Hybrid Work

We’ve adopted a hybrid workplace model where 2 days in the office are recommended but not enforced. It’s up to you and your team to decide on the exact days you’ll spend working together in person.

Equal Opportunity

The Vinted Group is committed to building an inclusive workplace where people from all walks of life feel a sense of belonging. We welcome applications from people of all backgrounds, identities and life experiences. At Vinted, all applicants are treated fairly without regard to their race, age, religion or belief, sex, national origin, citizenship, gender identity, sexual orientation, disability, or any other protected characteristic.

The gross monthly salary range for this position is:

€3.433—€4.650 EUR

Title: Supply Chain Master Data Analyst
Description:
The Company

Dexcom Corporation (NASDAQ DXCM) is a pioneer and global leader in continuous glucose monitoring (CGM). Dexcom began as a small company with a big dream: To forever change how diabetes is managed. To unlock information and insights that drive better health outcomes. Here we are 25 years later, having pioneered an industry. And we're just getting started. We are broadening our vision beyond diabetes to empower people to take control of health. That means personalized, actionable insights aimed at solving important health challenges. To continue what we've started: Improving human health.

We are driven by thousands of ambitious, passionate people worldwide who are willing to fight like warriors to earn the trust of our customers by listening, serving with integrity, thinking big, and being dependable. We've already changed millions of lives and we're ready to change millions more. Our future ambition is to become a leading consumer health technology company while continuing to develop solutions for serious health conditions. We'll get there by constantly reinventing unique biosensing-technology experiences. Though we've come a long way from our small company days, our dreams are bigger than ever. The opportunity to improve health on a global scale stands before us.

Meet The Team

New ideas have a way of becoming not only great products but also best in class practices that can significantly enhance the customer experience. At Dexcom you can make a difference in the lives of millions across the world!

Reporting to the Sr Supply Chain Manager, this role contributes to the coordination of Engineering Change Orders (ECOs) and supports product lifecycle activities, ensuring that system data is accurate and updated in a timely manner. You will help enable effective planning, scheduling, warehouse and execution processes aligned with Dexcom’s operational standards.

Where You Come In


You will collect, validate, and update master data required for Purchasing (Buy Items), Production Planning (Make Items), Warehousing and Inventory processes
You will ensure data accuracy and completeness within ERP systems
You will support coordination and implementation of Engineering Change Orders across make and buy items, assist with updating system data in line with approved changes, Track ECO progress and escalate issues where required
You will assist in managing Phase-in / phase-out of parts, Product revision changes
You will ensure updates are reflected accurately in systems to support production and procurement readiness
You will provide accurate data inputs to support MRP and planning activities
You will support resolution of data-related issues impacting Supply Chain operations
You will identify and support improvements in data accuracy and processes
You will follow established data governance and change control procedures


What Makes You Successful


You have had exposure to master data, planning and purchasing processes
You have familiarity with item master data structures
You are an advanced user of Microsoft Excel, Copilot, Outlook, Teams and Smartsheets is preferable
You have experience in the medical device or CPG industry
You have experience with Oracle EBS, Oracle Fusion, PLM and Anaplan systems networks is advantageous


Experience And Education Requirements


Typically requires a Bachelor’s degree in Supply Chain Management, Business Administration, Operations Management, or a related field and a minimum of 2-5 years of related experience.


What You’ll Get


A front row seat to life changing CGM technology.
A full and comprehensive benefits program.
Growth opportunities on a global scale.
Access to career development through in-house learning programs and/or qualified tuition reimbursement.
An exciting and innovative, industry-leading organization committed to our employees, customers, and the communities we serve.


Travel Required


0-5%


To all Staffing and Recruiting Agencies: Our Careers Site is only for individuals seeking a job at Dexcom. Only authorized staffing and recruiting agencies may use this site or to submit profiles, applications or resumes on specific requisitions. Dexcom does not accept unsolicited resumes or applications from agencies. Please do not forward resumes to the Talent Acquisition team, Dexcom employees or any other company location. Dexcom is not responsible for any fees related to unsolicited resumes/applications.

Title: Senior IT Data Analyst
Description:
Job Description

Senior Data Analyst

The Role

As a Senior Data Analyst, you will work closely with business and technical teams to drive the creation of scalable and documented data design solutions. They drive adoption of enterprise and industry standard modeling methodologies. They partner with project teams to deliver practical & resilient data model designs that meet business needs and are capable of easily adapting to future business requirements.

The Expertise and Skills You Bring

Requirements And Analysis


Engage with business and technology partners to understand the business requirements, analyze data gaps, and recommend appropriate solutions.
Assess objectives, use cases, data requirements and specifications documents created by other analysts
Analyze and resolve major data issues that arise during the course of projects or in production environments


Data Design


You are an experienced enterprise data modeler who can define conceptual and logical data models, with strong definitions, to capture organizational data needs across multiple subject areas
Create physical design from logical data models for different types of systems such as operational systems, data warehouses, data lakes and others
Research to identify effective data designs, new tools and methodologies for data analysis
Document and present database solutions to product partners for approval.
Promote common data API adoption and reuse.
Map Technical terms to Business terms in data catalog tool.


Technical Leadership


Ensure technology decisions align with enterprise standards and strategy.
Collaborate with DBAs, architects, subject area owners to create scalable data models.
Find opportunities to utilize enterprise data model for new projects to minimize work and data redundancy


Education And Experience

A Bachelor’s or Master's degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or equivalent experience


5+ years’ experience with data design, analysis and development. Development experience weighted towards PL/SQL and data loading tool/concepts.
Demonstrated experience in the following areas: Data API design and implementation, PL/SQL, data warehouses, data modeling and data profiling.


Skills And Knowledge


Demonstrable experience with SAP Power Designer or similar data modeling tool.
Proficient analytical and problem solving skills
Solid understanding of the database development process including analysis, design, coding, testing, problem resolution and planning, and full competency in all relevant coding environments, languages, tools, and platforms
Good interpersonal skills and strong verbal and written skills to effectively communicate with technical and non-technical associates in the company
Proven interpersonal skills, including the ability to work across the organization and interact/influence/negotiate optimally at all levels of management and peers.
Experience working in cross-functional agile development teams.
Understanding of Financial Services.


The Team

Asset Management Technology is seeking a Senior level candidate in the analysis and data modeling organization. The successful candidate will be responsible for leading analysis and data modeling across different projects with diverse scope and complex business and technical requirements in a highly integrated data environment. This role requires working under experienced leaders, for achieving organizational strategic data goals. You must be comfortable working in a fast paced and highly ambiguous agile environment where there is a focus on timely delivery of data models and other data artifacts.

AM Tech is a global organization, with technologists located across the United States, London, India, and Ireland. The team supports a broad range of functions within the Investment Management and Advisory domain, including Portfolio Management, Research, and Trading, by providing reliable, high impact technology solutions.

We are currently working in a hybrid model with some flexibility for remote working. However, Fidelity is gradually transitioning towards a full-time return to the office. We believe this will help strengthen our culture and enhance both the associate and customer experience. At present, this transition is expected from 2027 onwards, with no fixed timeline in place.

Category

Information Technology

Title: BI Analyst
Description:
Position: BI Analyst (Tableau & Revenue Operations)

Location: On site (Larnaca or Limassol)

Department: Data & Analytics




About Us

Our company is a leading provider of cloud contact center solutions that enable businesses of all sizes to deliver exceptional customer experiences. Our innovative platform offers a full range of features and capabilities, including intelligent routing, IVR, call recording, analytics, and more. We are growing rapidly and expanding our global footprint, and we are looking for a talented Sales professionals to help us expand our client base.




Role Overview

We are looking for a proactive BI Analyst to become the core owner of our Tableau ecosystem. Our organization is heavily sales-driven, meaning your work with data will directly impact our revenue growth. You will be responsible for building high-quality interactive dashboards, managing our Tableau Server, and working closely with the Revenue team to track the sales pipeline and conversion rates.




The ideal candidate is someone who is technically skilled in Tableau and SQL but also has the "business curiosity" to understand how our operations works and where we can improve.




Key Responsibilities

1. Tableau Development & Admin

Dashboarding: Create functional, interactive, and visually polished dashboards in Tableau that stakeholders actually enjoy using.
Server Maintenance: Manage Tableau Server (handling user access, permissions, and organizing the project structure).
Data Freshness: Manage and monitor Tableau Extracts and subscriptions to ensure data is always up-to-date and accurate.

2. Sales & Revenue Support

Funnel Analysis: Build and maintain reports on sales pipeline, conversion stages, and lead velocity.
Ad-hoc Reporting: Provide quick, accurate data insights using Excel or Tableau to support urgent business decisions.
Proactivity: Don’t just build what is asked — analyze the data and suggest where the business might be missing opportunities.

3. Collaboration & Processes

Requirements Gathering: Communicate with business users to understand their needs and turn them into clear technical requirements.
Data Pipeline: Coordinate with data engineers to explain what datasets you need to build the best possible dashboards.




Requirements

Experience: 2–4 years of experience in BI or Data Analytics.
Tableau Stack: Proficiency in Tableau Desktop (LODs, Parameters, Sets) and experience with Tableau Server administration.
SQL: Solid ability to write queries to extract and transform data.
Excel: Advanced user (Pivot tables, complex formulas, data cleaning).
Business Mindset: Understanding of basic sales metrics (Pipeline, Conversion, Win Rate, Churn, MRR).
Language: Professional working proficiency in English.




Nice to have:

Python: Basic scripting for data tasks.
Statistics: Understanding of core statistical concepts.
Vibe Stack: Cursor for development, n8n for workflow automation, and an understanding of how to prompt/deploy AI agents for data tasks.




Key Competencies

Attention to detail: You double-check your numbers before showing them to the manager.
Organization: You can manage multiple requests without losing track of priorities.
Communication: You can explain "what the data says" to someone who isn't a data person.




What We Offer:

Play a crucial role in growing our product.
Attractive compensation and benefits.
Top-notch work gear.
Embrace a flexible hybrid work model.
Free lunch and healthy snacks every day.
Medical insurance.
A team of talented and fun colleagues from different countries.




At Voiso, we are proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to fostering an inclusive environment for all team members.

Title: CRM BI Analyst
Description:
About Patrianna

Patrianna is a fast-growing product development company headquartered in Gibraltar, with a globally distributed team delivering seamless, engaging, and scalable gaming experiences for millions of players worldwide.

We're building a world-class operational engine and seeking talented professionals in CRM, fraud & risk, payments, marketing, and player support who excel in fast-paced, high-growth settings. Our teams prioritize smart execution, continuous improvement, and cross-functional collaboration—empowering you to drive real impact from day one.

What You Will Do


Analyze gaming economy health and player behavior to identify revenue optimization opportunities
Partner with CRM teams to design, track, and analyze campaigns, building player segmentation models to maximize lifetime value
Develop advanced Tableau and Looker dashboards plus automated reports for real-time player lifecycle visibility
Translate data science models into actionable monetization solutions for leadership
Validate features via A/B testing and statistical analysis to champion a testing culture
Forecast impacts of gamification features on engagement and spend, then measure results


What We Need


Gaming industry experience in BI/data analysis for monetization, gamification, or CRM analytics
Proven support for CRM teams on reporting and lifecycle strategies
Advanced SQL for large-scale data, plus Tableau, Looker, or Power BI visualization skills
Knowledge of player behavior, virtual economies, and gamification mechanics
Strong communication to deliver clear recommendations to non-technical stakeholders
Proactive problem-solving for trends and data integrity


Why Join Us

Be the analytical backbone for revenue impact across multiple gaming brands, shaping experiences for millions. Enjoy autonomy to innovate, own critical insights, and influence strategy in a high-growth environment. Collaborate with passionate teams valuing execution and learning, with competitive compensation and career growth.

Title: Commercial Data Analyst
Description:
Epic is one of the largest telecommunications companies in Cyprus and a member of a dynamic European family of telecommunications companies. With ability, innovation and agility at our core, we are offering a state-of-the-art network recognized by multiple awards in Cyprus.




At Epic we take care of our people and we enhance a people centric and learning culture for your professional growth. We are always looking for ambitious employees to be a part of our award-winning organisation!

Our team is growing so join us to take your career to the next level and become a part of our team of more than 350 professionals.




The role:

As a Commercial Data Analyst, you'll turn data into actionable insights that shape commercial and marketing decisions. You'll work closely with cross functional teams to analyze customer behavior, identify opportunities, and deliver targeted marketing campaigns that drive customer engagement, increase value, and improve retention. Your insights will directly influence commercial strategies and contribute to the success of the business.




Your Responsibilities:

Analyze large datasets to identify trends, patterns, and insights that inform business decisions
Development, implementation and maintenance of weekly/monthly KPI’s for commercial segments stakeholders (Postpaid, Prepaid, Fixed).
Preparation of data & analysis of all marketing and sales campaigns: Cross Sell, Up Sell, Churn Prevention
Collaborate with broader Sales, Marketing, Finance, and Operations teams to develop and implement strategic initiatives that drive revenue/LTV growth and market expansion
Identify and implement process improvements to enhance data accuracy, reporting efficiency, and overall analytical capabilities.
Provide commercial insight on the month end results, trends and follow up actions. Clear and concise articulation of standard KPIs to segment stakeholders.
Acting as an internal consultant, using analytical skills to answer critical business questions.







Our proposition

Competitive remuneration package
Annual bonus scheme
13th salary
Attractive benefits with comprehensive medical and life insurances and provident fund
Company telecom products with free mobile and internet lines and exclusive discounts for our services with maximum monthly total value of 130 euros
Opportunity for a sustainable career and continuously building skills and expertise in a dynamic technology sector
Hybrid workplace model







Your Profile

2+ years of professional experience as an analyst/senior analyst, preferably in contract-based industry, market or consumer research with a particular focus on business data analysis, insights generation and planning
BSc in Computer Science (Data Science), Business Intelligence, Economics or related field
Postgraduate degree in any data science related field will be considered as strong advantage
Proficient SQL skills and data manipulation with Python (preferred) or R
Experience with any analytical/visualization tool (Power BI, Tableau)
Comfortable utilizing AI tools to improve efficiencies and integrate their usage in daily work
Understanding of data warehouses and ETL principles
Experience with an ETL platform such as Airflow, Informatica etc.
Willingness to learn new skills on the job
Fluent in English, with strong verbal and written communication skills




Interested to join a team of passionate professionals? Apply now! Send your CV to career@epic.com.cy stating the job title in the subject line.

  • Identify and implement process improvements to enhance data accuracy, reporting efficiency, and overall analytical capabilities.
  • Provide commercial insight on the month end results, trends and follow up actions. Clear and concise articulation of standard KPIs to segment stakeholders.
  • Acting as an internal consultant, using analytical skills to answer critical business questions.



Our proposition

  • Competitive remuneration package
  • Annual bonus scheme
  • 13th salary
  • Attractive benefits with comprehensive medical and life insurances and provident fund
  • Company telecom products with free mobile and internet lines and exclusive discounts for our services with maximum monthly total value of 130 euros
  • Opportunity for a sustainable career and continuously building skills and expertise in a dynamic technology sector
  • Hybrid workplace model



Your Profile

  • 2+ years of professional experience as an analyst/senior analyst, preferably in contract-based industry, market or consumer research with a particular focus on business data analysis, insights generation and planning
  • BSc in Computer Science (Data Science), Business Intelligence, Economics or related field
  • Postgraduate degree in any data science related field will be considered as strong advantage
  • Proficient SQL skills and data manipulation with Python (preferred) or R
  • Experience with any analytical/visualization tool (Power BI, Tableau)
  • Comfortable utilizing AI tools to improve efficiencies and integrate their usage in daily work
  • Understanding of data warehouses and ETL principles
  • Experience with an ETL platform such as Airflow, Informatica etc.
  • Willingness to learn new skills on the job
  • Fluent in English, with strong verbal and written communication skills


Interested to join a team of passionate professionals? Apply now! Send your CV to career@epic.com.cy stating the job title in the subject line.

Title: Decision Scientist, Trust & Safety (Limited-Time Relocation Bonus)
Description:
Brief Info About Vinted

Our mission is to make second-hand the first choice, and we're looking for people who want to help us get there. Every day, we work together to help our members buy and sell pre-loved clothing and lifestyle items, giving each piece a second life – or even a third.

The Vinted Group is made up of three business units that support this mission:

Vinted Marketplace is Europe’s leading platform for second-hand fashion and a go-to destination for all kinds of pre-loved items, with a growing range of categories. Our platform connects millions of members across 20+ markets, helping great items find a new life.

Vinted Go enhances the shipping experience with a vast network of over 500,000 pick-up and drop-off points, partnering with more than 60 carriers across Europe, with added services like item verification for peace of mind on high-value pieces.

Vinted Pay is the newest part of the Vinted Group, dedicated to bringing secure, reliable payments to buyers and sellers across Europe. Seamlessly integrated into the Vinted app, it helps keep every transaction safe, efficient, and easy for our members.

Founded in 2008 in Lithuania, Vinted began as a way for friends to find new homes for clothes they no longer needed. In 2019, we became Lithuania's first unicorn! Today, our headquarters remain in Vilnius, and we've grown with offices across Europe, supported by a team of over 2,000 people.

Information About The Position

You’ll join Trust & Safety’s Actioning team, whose mission is to protect the Vinted community from harmful activities and content. The team owns critical mechanisms, including user reporting, moderation workflows, policy enforcement, and interventions against malicious users.

We’re looking for a Data Analyst / Decision Scientist to join the team and help shape better product and policy decisions through data. In this role, you’ll be embedded in a cross-functional product team, working closely with product managers, engineers, and other decision scientists to solve meaningful problems and turn insights into action.

This is a great opportunity for someone who enjoys combining analytical depth with real product impact. You’ll lead hypothesis-driven experimentation, uncover opportunities, measure outcomes, and help your team make confident, data-driven decisions.

In this position, you’ll


Partner closely with your product team and stakeholders to identify opportunities, answer important business questions, and guide decision-making with data.
Drive product experimentation from hypothesis to analysis, helping the team validate ideas and understand their real impact.
Analyse moderation, reporting, policy, and sanctioning flows to uncover insights and improve how we protect the Vinted community.
Define, calculate, and monitor key performance indicators, spot issues early, and investigate root causes.
Build dashboards and self-service solutions that help teams independently access and use data.
Work with other decision scientists to raise the bar for analytics, experimentation, and data-driven thinking across the domain.


About You


Have 3+ years of experience in Data Analytics, Decision Science, or a similar role.
Are strong in analytical thinking and problem-solving, and know how to balance rigor with business relevance.
Have a solid understanding of statistical methods, including A/B testing (designing, running, and interpreting A/B tests), and other inferential techniques.
Have hands-on experience with SQL and Python for data analysis, or a strong willingness to learn them quickly.
Are familiar with BI tools such as Looker, Tableau, or Power BI, or a strong willingness to learn them quickly.
Enjoy working in iterative environments where priorities evolve and learning quickly matters.
Can communicate clearly with both technical and non-technical audiences.
Are fluent in English, both written and spoken.
Nice to have: Experience in Trust & Safety, fraud prevention, or a related area.


Work perks


This position is eligible for Limited-time Relocation Package which includes €30.000 bonus + 2 months of temporary housing covered by Vinted (check our website for more info)
The opportunity to benefit from our share options programme
25 working days of holiday
Access to all the tools & tech needed for work
Home office support: we provide IT workstation equipment and a personal budget of up to €540 for home workplace furniture
Private health insurance
Mental and emotional health support through the Mindletic app
Frequent team-building events
A personal monthly budget for shopping on Vinted
The opportunity to spend up to 90 days per year - 21 of which can be spent working outside of the EU - on workation
A dog-friendly office
In Vilnius office: Gym & in-house meals at friendly prices
In Kaunas office: a monthly lunch allowance, and a once-a-week provided in-house lunch and breakfast


Working at Vinted

Limited-time Relocation Package

Your location shouldn’t be a stopper! In addition to our standard package, for this position we’ve increased the bonus to €30,000 gross. If you think Vinted is the ideal cultural fit for you, we invite you to apply! For further information, please refer to our Relocation page.

Individual Learning Budget

We invest in your professional growth! As part of our commitment to continuous learning, we offer an annual learning budget to support your personal and career development through courses, certifications, workshops and more.

Hybrid Work

We’ve adopted a hybrid workplace model where 2 days in office are recommended but not enforced. It’s up to you and your team to decide on the exact days you’ll spend working together in person.

Equal Opportunity

The Vinted Group is committed to building an inclusive workplace where people from all walks of life feel a sense of belonging. We welcome applications from people of all backgrounds, identities and life experiences. At Vinted, all applicants are treated fairly without regard to their race, age, religion or belief, sex, national origin, citizenship, gender identity, sexual orientation, disability, or any other protected characteristic.

The gross monthly salary range for this position is:

€4.608—€6.233 EUR

Title: People Data & Automation Specialist
Description:
Omniva connects people, businesses, and communities across the Baltics and beyond. We’ve grown from Estonia’s trusted postal service into a modern, technology-driven logistics company supporting the growth of e-commerce and global trade.

Every day, we help our customers send, receive, and grow — with care, precision, and reliability. Our people are at the heart of everything we do: caring, open, and driven to deliver experiences that feel personal and effortless.

At Omniva, every delivery carries possibility — and every team member helps make that happen.

About The Role

We’re looking for a People Data & Automation Specialist to join our Baltic People & Culture team and help shape how data and technology drive our people’s decisions.

This is a high-impact, future-facing role at the intersection of people analytics, automation, and digital enablement. You will build the foundations of reliable People data, transform it into actionable insights, and design smart, scalable solutions that reduce manual work and improve how our People team operates. You’ll play a key role in moving us from reactive reporting to proactive, data-driven decision-making, while embedding automation and AI into everyday People processes.

This role is ideal for someone who enjoys building, simplifying, and improving systems, and who wants to make People data genuinely useful and operationally impactful.

What You’ll Do


Build and maintain a reliable People data ecosystem across the Baltics; define key metrics and standardize reporting
Create and manage dashboards (e.g. Power BI) and deliver actionable insights to support business decisions
Implement low-code/no-code, AI, and automation solutions to reduce manual work and improve accuracy
Optimize and automate employee lifecycle processes (hiring, onboarding, exits, reporting)
Develop simple internal tools and scalable solutions to improve team efficiency
Collaborate with leadership, People Partners, and engineering teams while continuously replacing manual tasks with smarter ways of working


What You Bring


Experience in People/HR Analytics or data analysis, with understanding of HR data and employee lifecycle processes
Strong Excel skills (data structuring, validation, modelling)
Hands-on experience with Power BI or similar tools
Experience with automation, workflows, or process optimisation; familiarity with HR systems (HRIS, ATS, payroll) is a plus
Interest in or experience with low-code/no-code tools, scripting, or AI-assisted development
Comfortable designing and building solutions from scratch


Mindset & Ways of Working


Ownership: You take responsibility and follow through
Self-direction: You manage priorities independently and act proactively
Continuous improvement: You constantly look for better, simpler ways of working
Clarity in communication: You turn complex data into understandable insights
Collaboration: You build strong relationships and work effectively across teams
Reliability: You deliver consistently and can be trusted to get things done
Curiosity: You stay open to learning and exploring new tools and approaches
Adaptability: You’re comfortable in evolving environments where not everything is predefined


Why you’ll love working here

At Omniva, we live our values every day.

We care — about our people, our customers, and the world we connect.

We are open — to new ideas, innovation, and learning from each other.

We are trustworthy — we do what we promise and take pride in getting it right.

And we enjoy our work — celebrating progress, teamwork, and shared achievements.

You’ll also benefit from:


A meaningful role in a growing, technology-driven logistics company
Hybrid work model with office location Pallasti 28, Tallinn with office and flexible environment
Health insurance or sports compensation
Five additional vacation days after two years
Opportunities for personal and professional growth


Compensation & Growth

For this position, the gross monthly salary range is EUR 3000 – 3800 gross, depending on your experience and qualifications.

At Omniva, we believe in fair and transparent compensation that reflects your contribution and supports your growth.

✉️ How To Apply

Ready to make your mark?

Apply now and help shape the future of logistics and cross-border commerce with Omniva.

We are only considering candidates currently located in Estonia.


Please note: Applications must be submitted in English, which is Omniva’s global working language.

Title: CRM BI Analyst
Description:
About Patrianna

Patrianna is a fast-growing product development company headquartered in Gibraltar, with a globally distributed team delivering seamless, engaging, and scalable gaming experiences for millions of players worldwide.

We're building a world-class operational engine and seeking talented professionals in CRM, fraud & risk, payments, marketing, and player support who excel in fast-paced, high-growth settings. Our teams prioritize smart execution, continuous improvement, and cross-functional collaboration—empowering you to drive real impact from day one.

What You Will Do


Analyze gaming economy health and player behavior to identify revenue optimization opportunities
Partner with CRM teams to design, track, and analyze campaigns, building player segmentation models to maximize lifetime value
Develop advanced Tableau and Looker dashboards plus automated reports for real-time player lifecycle visibility
Translate data science models into actionable monetization solutions for leadership
Validate features via A/B testing and statistical analysis to champion a testing culture
Forecast impacts of gamification features on engagement and spend, then measure results


What We Need


Gaming industry experience in BI/data analysis for monetization, gamification, or CRM analytics
Proven support for CRM teams on reporting and lifecycle strategies
Advanced SQL for large-scale data, plus Tableau, Looker, or Power BI visualization skills
Knowledge of player behavior, virtual economies, and gamification mechanics
Strong communication to deliver clear recommendations to non-technical stakeholders
Proactive problem-solving for trends and data integrity


Why Join Us

Be the analytical backbone for revenue impact across multiple gaming brands, shaping experiences for millions. Enjoy autonomy to innovate, own critical insights, and influence strategy in a high-growth environment. Collaborate with passionate teams valuing execution and learning, with competitive compensation and career growth.

Title: Business Analyst - Masterdata Governance
Description:
Job description:



Business Analyst – Masterdata Governance

Hybrid working

 

 

Tirlán

Tirlán, is a world-class food and nutrition co-operative, with a diverse portfolio of quality ingredients, leading consumer and agri brands.

Tirlán owns leading brands such as Avonmore, Kilmeaden Cheese, Millac, Solmiko, Truly Grass Fed, Premier Milk, Wexford, mymilkman.ie and GAIN Animal Nutrition.

We nurture a talented team across Ireland, the US, MEA and China, delivering annual revenues of circa €3 billion, and circa 2,000 people to manage our network of 11 processing plants and 52 agri branches. Our purpose is to nourish the world, while safeguarding our environment for future generations.

 

Role Profile

We have an opportunity for a Business Analyst to support implementation of a Master Data Governance strategy, with a strong focus on SAP master data analysis, data quality control, governance assurance, and process improvement. This is a highly analytical, control-focused role working across business and IT functions to strengthen the accuracy, consistency, and reliability of master data.

 

Responsibilities

Data Analysis & Quality

Analyse SAP master data to identify data quality issues, including missing values, inconsistencies, duplicates, outliers and invalid records
Perform root-cause analysis to understand if issues are caused by system configuration, process gaps, control weakness, user behaviour or data migration activity
Support data cleansing and remediation activity, validating outcomes through profiling, reconciliation and post-remediation checks

Data Controls & Governance

Analyse large datasets from SAP and integrated systems to identify trends, exceptions, control gaps, and data integrity risks
Design and maintain data quality controls, exception checks, and validation routines to support proactive master data governance
Support master data cleansing, remediation, and ongoing control monitoring across key master data objects

Analytics & Control Monitoring

Use SAP Analytics Cloud, BI, Excel, and other analytical tools to monitor data quality, control effectiveness, and remediation progress

Hybrid working

 

 

Tirlán

Tirlán, is a world-class food and nutrition co-operative, with a diverse portfolio of quality ingredients, leading consumer and agri brands.

Responsibilities

Data Analysis & Quality

Data Controls & Governance

  • Analyse large datasets from SAP and integrated systems to identify trends, exceptions, control gaps, and data integrity risks
  • Design and maintain data quality controls, exception checks, and validation routines to support proactive master data governance
  • Support master data cleansing, remediation, and ongoing control monitoring across key master data objects

Analytics & Control Monitoring

  • Use SAP Analytics Cloud, BI, Excel, and other analytical tools to monitor data quality, control effectiveness, and remediation progress

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Company Overview

Holafly is a high-growth scale-up revolutionising how businesses and travellers connect to the internet abroad. Since 2018, we’ve empowered travellers in over 200 destinations worldwide with secure and reliable eSIM solutions. With a team of 500+ professionals across multiple countries, we are scaling globally to support travellers with seamless, unlimited data connectivity.

We’re not just connecting people—we’re enabling freedom and peace of mind, ensuring our users stay connected from the second they land, wherever their journey takes them.

About The Role

We are seeking a highly analytical and detail-oriented Data Analyst to join our team. In this role, you will be responsible for transforming complex data into understandable and conclusive insights. You will play a key role in training different business areas on the effective use of our Business Intelligence (BI) tools and advising them on how to maximize the value derived from our data. If you have a passion for data storytelling, strong communication skills, and enjoy empowering others with data, we encourage you to apply.

Responsibilities


Collect, clean, analyse, and interpret large datasets to identify trends, patterns, and insights.
Create clear, concise, and impactful visualisations, reports, and dashboards that effectively communicate findings to various stakeholders.
Develop and deliver training sessions to educate different business units on the functionality and best practices of BI tools.
Provide expert advice and guidance to the business area on how to leverage data to answer key business questions and drive informed decision-making.
Collaborate with data engineers and other teams to ensure data accuracy, accessibility, and consistency.
Identify opportunities for process improvements and data optimisation.
Stay up-to-date with industry best practices and emerging trends in data analysis, BI tools, and data visualisation.


Qualifications


Proven experience as a Data Analyst or in a similar analytical role.
Strong proficiency in SQL.
Extensive experience with Business Intelligence (BI) tools (e.g., Looker Studio, Looker, Tableau, Power BI, Qlik Sense).
Excellent data visualization skills and the ability to present complex information clearly and concisely.
Strong analytical, problem-solving, and critical thinking abilities.
Exceptional communication and presentation skills, with the ability to explain technical concepts to non-technical audiences.
Ability to work independently and collaboratively in a fast-paced environment.
Detail-oriented with a strong commitment to data accuracy and integrity.
Bachelor's degree in a quantitative field such as Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Economics, Business Analytics, or a Business related discipline.


Benefits & Perks


25 Days of PTO
5% pension contribution
€ 3000 for Medical/Health coverage
Self development budget of $ 500


During a trip to Thailand, Pedro and Lidia, the founders of Holafly, experienced the frustration of not having an internet connection.

Inspired by their own experiences, they came up with a simple but brilliant idea: why not allow travellers to buy data plans for any destination from their home country?

Our passion for providing easy and convenient connectivity to travellers around the world drives everything we do.

We love our work and we do it with pride. Stay connected wherever you go.

Title: Data Analyst/Steward
Description:
WHO WE ARE…

Our Mission is to help those in need with our out-of-this-world superpowers. Avalanche Laboratory’s purpose is to be a reliable and long-term business partner to our customers—offering them customized software solutions tailored for their business processes.

We work on a project basis (across the EU and beyond). To date, we’ve delivered over 320 successful projects, with another 45 currently in progress. Our clients span industries from financial services, fintech, and government, to manufacturing and technology, partnering with us to solve complex transformation challenges.




ABOUT THIS ROLE

BI Data Steward (Risk Area)

We are looking for a BI Data Steward to join an Agile delivery team within the Risk domain in a leading banking environment. This role requires proven hands-on experience working in Agile environments, including the ability to clearly explain Agile principles and demonstrate their practical value in IT delivery teams.




The team owns several key Risk services that are critical to both ongoing operations and upcoming initiatives:

BI Forbearance (data sourcing and outputs)
BI Riskgrade Consolidated (BIS)
BI Data Governance Registry
BI Exposure




These services form foundational components used across multiple Risk value delivery teams. As several initiatives are actively building new features on these platforms, the role involves managing dependencies and operating effectively under delivery pressure.




Key Responsibilities and Requirements

We are looking for a highly hands-on professional with a strong passion for data and data stewardship. The ideal candidate combines technical expertise, analytical thinking, and strong collaboration skills.




Must-have qualifications:

Strong, practical experience in SQL with the ability to work deeply with data.
Proven experience in data governance, data management, or similar roles (e.g., Data Steward, Business Analyst, Reporting Analyst, IT System Analyst).
Solid experience working in banking or financial services, preferably within the Risk domain.
Strong understanding of banking products, processes, and IT systems.
Advanced SQL skills, including analyzing large datasets, identifying data quality issues, and understanding complex data structures.
Hands-on experience with data warehousing and reporting concepts, including data profiling techniques.
Solid understanding of database structures and principles.
Proven ability to gather, analyze, and document business, functional, and data requirements.
Experience defining and maintaining business data definitions, glossaries, metadata, and data quality rules.
Experience designing data mappings, transformation logic, and business rules aligned with enterprise data architecture.
Experience monitoring data quality issues, performing root cause analysis, and coordinating remediation across teams.
Strong proficiency with JIRA and Confluence.
Excellent communication and collaboration skills, with a proactive and engaged approach to teamwork.




Experience Level

4–6 years of relevant experience in a similar role.
Strong competence in solving complex problems and understanding interconnected systems.
Ability to guide and support others, corresponding to a senior consultant or equivalent level.




Location and Working Model

Location: Baltics
Requirement: On-site presence three days per week is mandatory to ensure effective collaboration with the team.




Why join us…

Work on a high-impact, large-scale migration project in a pan-European environment
Be part of a strong, focused team of senior engineers and consultants
Compensation: Employment in Estonia up to 3,600 EUR gross, in Latvia up to 3,900 EUR gross, or a B2B rate of 30–35 EUR/hour depending on seniority and experience. In Lithuania, only the B2B option is available.
