Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Back to all jobs

Data Analyst

About Nexters

Nexters is an international game development company that strives to introduce the joy of core gaming experiences to casual players. In 2021 we went public and listed on the NASDAQ (GDEV). Nexters is part of the GDEV holding, which includes not only Nexters, but also Cubic Games, Royal Ark, Game Gears studios.Our people work worldwide from offices in Cyprus, Armenia, and Kazakhstan or remotely. We want brilliant professionals who love building great games in a team with one of the best cultures.

About Your Role

We are seeking an experienced Data Analyst at the intersection of data and marketing to join the Data Ecosystem team and help transform data into actionable business tools. This role is not about traditional analytics and ad-hoc requests, but about building solutions - from research and prototyping to implementation of tools that are actively used by the marketing team in their daily work.

What You’ll Be Doing


Developing and improving data-driven tools to increase marketing team efficiency
Designing and prototyping automated solutions (including AI-based ones) to accelerate creative production and traffic acquisition
Working on algorithmic approaches to media buying and campaign optimization
Integrating with external data sources and advertising platforms
Conducting data research, identifying growth opportunities, and transforming them into practical tools
Promoting a data-driven culture and expanding access to useful analytics for marketing
Working end-to-end: from problem definition and research to implementation and impact on business metrics
Collaborating with marketing, UA leads, analysts, and engineers, aligning solutions across teams
Quickly diving into problem contexts and taking ownership of results


We hope that you have


Expert-level SQL and experience working with large-scale data (ClickHouse / BigQuery / Redshift / PostgreSQL)
Strong Python skills (pandas, numpy, scipy, statsmodels, sklearn, etc.)
Deep understanding of marketing metrics and unit economics (LTV, CAC, ROI, retention, payback)
Hands-on experience with advertising platforms (Meta, Google Ads, Axon, etc.) and MMPs (AppsFlyer, Adjust)
Experience in taking analytical solutions through to implementation and measurable business impact
Ability to work in ambiguity: independently define approaches and drive tasks to completion
Ability to rapidly prototype solutions and validate hypotheses
Proactivity: willingness to suggest improvements in processes, tools, and approaches
Strong communication skills: ability to discuss solutions, align with stakeholders, and collaborate across teams


Nice to have


Experience building production-ready solutions
Experience with PySpark / Databricks
Experience designing ETL pipelines (Airflow, Luigi, or similar)
Experience automating processes (including with AI)
Experience building tools for marketing or UA teams
Experience in mobile products or the gaming industry
Strong systems thinking: ability to structure problems, define logic, and design processes


What We Offer


Healthcare Medical care program (including dental service) or medical care compensation are available for you wherever you wish to work. The basic medical care program is also available for your family members if you choose to work from our official locations – Cyprus, Armenia, and Kazakhstan.
Work, life, and balance We offer a fair amount of paid vacation days and sick leave. You can work at one of our comfortable offices in official locations or from home.
Sports You can choose various sports activities like yoga, football, and volleyball in our official locations.
Relocation support A relocation package is available for you if you decide to join us in one of our official locations.
Skills improvement Let's learn languages: you can visit online English language courses. Greek and Armenian language lessons are available for everyone who works in Cyprus or Armenia. Our company encourages people of Nexters to take part in game jams, hackathons, and relevant conferences and meetups as expert speakers.
Community & ESG We are proud of our local communities and support employees’ social initiatives. Dream big, act big: Nexters contribute to gaming’s positive impact on the world, hold a sustainability strategy, and take part in charity projects all over the world.


Work format

In one of the company's offices in Armenia, Cyprus, Kazakhstan or remotely.

Title: BI & Data Developer
Description:
BI & Data Developer

📍 Malta | Hybrid Working Available




A well-established financial services organisation is looking to hire a Business Intelligence / Data Developer to join its growing Data & Information Technology team.




This is an exciting opportunity for a data professional who enjoys working with modern Microsoft data technologies, building scalable ETL solutions, developing reporting platforms, and supporting business intelligence initiatives that drive strategic decision-making.




You will play a key role in designing, developing, and maintaining data solutions that support analytics, reporting, and operational efficiency across the organisation.




The Opportunity

As a Business Intelligence / Data Developer, you will work closely with business stakeholders and technical teams to build reliable data pipelines, reporting solutions, and data warehouse components that enable data-driven decision making.

This role offers exposure to a variety of projects across data integration, reporting, business intelligence, and data management within a highly regulated and technology-driven environment.







What You'll Be Doing

Design, develop, and maintain ETL processes using Microsoft SSIS, SQL Server, and Python
Build and maintain database stored procedures and data integration solutions
Generate and manage XML and CSV exports for internal and external systems
Develop and support business reports using SSRS and Power BI
Ensure data quality, integrity, consistency, and security across data platforms
Design and enhance data warehouse solutions to support reporting and analytics requirements
Document business processes, data flows, and technical solutions
Collaborate with business users to understand reporting and data requirements
Support testing, deployment, and ongoing maintenance of data solutions
Contribute to continuous improvement initiatives within the data and BI environment







What We're Looking For

Bachelor's Degree in Information Technology, Computer Science, or a related field
3–5 years of experience in data development using Microsoft technologies
Previous experience within the financial services sector is considered an advantage
Strong experience with SQL Server Data Tools (SSDT)
Solid understanding of SQL development and data modelling concepts
Hands-on experience developing and maintaining SSIS ETL solutions
Experience creating and maintaining SSRS reports
Strong Python development experience
Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills
Strong attention to detail and commitment to data quality
Good written and verbal communication skills




Nice to Have

Experience with DBT
Experience building Power BI dashboards and reports
Experience developing .NET applications
Experience with web application development
Exposure to data warehousing and business intelligence best practices




What's On Offer

Hybrid and flexible working arrangements
Ongoing professional development and career growth opportunities
Fully subsidised study loans and support for further education
Private health insurance, with the option to extend coverage to dependants
Life insurance and personal accident cover
Annual wellness allowance (€400)
Additional birthday leave
Annual salary reviews and performance appraisals
Employee wellbeing support, including professional counselling services
Preferential employee financial products and benefits
Corporate discounts and additional employee perks
Stable and supportive working environment within a respected financial institution




This is an excellent opportunity for a BI and Data professional looking to work on meaningful projects, develop their technical expertise, and contribute to the evolution of a modern data environment.




For more information, contact Kristoffer Parkhagen confidentially at +356 9999 8469 or send your CV to kristoffer.parkhagen@boston-link.com

Title: Data Acquisition Specialist
Description:
Our Mission:

At Sunrise Robotics, we are dedicated to augmenting humanity through intelligent robotics. Our mission is to elevate the world of manufacturing by introducing intelligent, flexible robots that enhance human capabilities and existing machinery, ushering in the next era of production at higher quality, with less waste, and lower cost.

Our Vision:

We see a future where every element of manufacturing, from design to assembly, is optimised with intelligent automation. Our vision is to integrate flexible robotic solutions, based on generic hardware and advanced software/AI capabilities, into manufacturing, particularly in small and medium-sized enterprises, to make automation economically viable and accessible, for all sizes of manufacturers. We are not just building robots; we are creating the strategically crucial components for autonomous, intelligent agents of the future.

The Role

As our Data Acquisition Specialist, you sit at the point where our customers’ factories meet our product roadmap. You go deep into electronics manufacturing processes, capture how they really work, and turn that complex reality into structured data, analytics, and reporting that the product team uses to decide which robotics capabilities to build next.

You are the technical face of Sunrise in these conversations: trusted by customer engineers to understand their process, and trusted internally to translate it into evidence the product team can act on. This is a balanced, hands-on role, part time in the field with customers, part time building the data foundation.

What You’ll Do

Capture and translate customer processes:


Work directly with customer engineering and operations teams to observe, document, and map their manufacturing processes (for example PCBA, assembly, inspection, test, and material handling).
Elicit and write up process requirements, constraints, cycle times, tolerances, and failure modes in a clear, structured form.
Translate domain language in both directions between customers and our product and engineering teams.


Structure data and build the analytics foundation:


Define what data we should capture across customer engagements, and design simple, durable structures to hold it.
Build and maintain the datasets, queries, dashboards, and reports the product team relies on, using strong SQL and Python and a BI tooling.
Keep the data clean, consistent, and trustworthy across many processes and customers. You own this lightweight infrastructure end to end.


Turn data into product insight:


Analyse process and capability data to surface patterns, reuse opportunities, and gaps that should shape the roadmap.
Produce the recurring reporting and one off analyses the product team use to prioritise.
Recommend where robotics capabilities will unlock the most value, backed by evidence.


Be a technical face of the company externally:


Represent Sunrise credibly with customer technical stakeholders, both on-site and remote.
Build trust by genuinely understanding both the manufacturing process and what modern robotics software and machine learning can and cannot do for it.
Feed customer reality back into product decisions, and clear technical narratives back out to customers.


What You’ll Bring


Hands-on analytical and data skills. SQL and Python, comfort structuring messy data into usable datasets, and building dashboards and reports in a BI tool. You don’t need to be a data engineer, but you should be able to stand up lightweight data infrastructure and own it.
Deep familiarity with manufacturing processes, from either a process or industrial engineering background in any manufacturing environment, or from deploying robotics and automation into manufacturing. You can walk a factory floor and understand what you’re seeing.
Requirements capture. The ability to elicit, structure, and clearly document complex technical requirements.
Customer-facing presence. Credible, trusted, and clear with technical stakeholders; comfortable being the external technical representative of the company.
Working understanding of modern robotics and machine learning. Enough to reason about feasibility and value. You don’t need to be a practitioner, but you need to understand what it takes.
Excellent written communication. Able to turn observation and data into crisp, decision-ready narratives.


Nice to have


Direct experience in electronics manufacturing (PCBA, box build, test, or EMS / contract manufacturing environments).
Experience working alongside or within a product team.
Familiarity with data pipeline or warehouse tooling, even if it isn’t your primary muscle.
Exposure to robotics, computer vision, or machine-learning-driven automation projects.


Location and travel


Remote-first, CET +/- 3Hrs
You’ll travel to customer manufacturing sites as projects require (minimum 50% travel), with the flexibility to be on the floor when it matters most.

Title: Data Analyst II
Description:
About Us

GoCardless is a global bank payment company. Over 100,000 businesses, from start-ups to household names, use GoCardless to collect and send payments through direct debit, real-time payments and open banking.

GoCardless processes US$130bn+ of payments annually, across 30+ countries; helping customers collect and send both recurring and one-off payments, without the chasing, stress or expensive fees. We use AI-powered solutions to improve payment success and reduce fraud. And, with open banking connectivity to over 2,500 banks, we help our customers make faster, more informed decisions.

We are headquartered in the UK with offices in London and Leeds, and additional locations in Australia, France, Ireland, Latvia, Portugal and the United States.

At GoCardless, we're all about supporting you! We’re committed to making our hiring process inclusive and accessible. If you need extra support or adjustments, reach out to your Talent Partner — we’re here to help!

And remember: we don’t expect you to meet every single requirement. If you’re excited by this role, we encourage you to apply!

Payment Intelligence

Our mission is to make GoCardless the best way to pay and be paid. Our ambition is to dramatically improve payment performance and safeguard our customers from bad outcomes like payment fraud and failures. You will help find data opportunities and areas to improve our payment intelligence products. You should be comfortable with large data sets, customer data, and machine learning principles. You’ll work alongside Product Managers, Engineers and Data Scientists in our two core Payment Intelligence teams to provide critical insights and help drive product direction.

Our existing Payment Intelligence Products are already one of the key reasons why customers choose GoCardless. These include Protect+, which defends customers from fraud, and Success+, which helps customers intelligently recover failed payments. You will need to own our understanding of these products and identify insights and opportunities to help us develop new intelligent features.

What You'll Do

At GoCardless analytics is at the core of everything we do and drives business performance through data insight. You will play a critical role partnering closely with stakeholders across the business (including product, operations, marketing, and commercial teams) in order to deliver actionable insights to influence decisions at every level of the organisation. The role is focussed on identifying value and driving change throughout the business derived from insights. Your insights will be turned into actions that will help drive the future of GoCardless. You will act as the bridge between the data, technical and business stakeholders informing strategic plans and will own and evolve a suite of reports designed to give a wide range of stakeholders access insights.


Own and iterate on experiments to generate user insight and drive business metrics, allowing the team to quickly take action on business opportunities.
Define how we quantitatively evaluate success, setting KPIs, designing tracking to measure what really matters, delving into the leading indicators of user behaviour
Influence the organisation to increase Product Data coverage and implement user tracking best practices - acting as a mentor to guide other members of the organisation to become data driven
Build models of user-segmentation, customer lifetime value etc in order to inform strategic product & commercial decisions
Continuously innovate on the practices and the tools we rely on to deliver the right insights at the right decision points (A/B Testing, Analysis (SQL), Visualisation (Looker), Tracking (Amplitude), Automation (Python), AI (Gemini)


What Excites You


Become a strategic partner and advisor for the business: you like to proactively seek out gaps in our product capabilities, suggest areas of improvement based on tangible data, as well as owning processes to run and improve testing from the tracking point of view when developing new features.
Act as a catalyst for change across the organization, driving the evolution of our data analytics standards, internal processes, and overall product data strategy to ensure scalability and operational excellence.
Translate complex technical findings into compelling, high-impact narratives. You will bridge the gap between technical data and commercial strategy, influencing product roadmaps and executive decision-making across the entire organization.
Stay current with AI and data trends to solve real-world problems. You like to leverage these technologies to enhance the payment experience, as well as to optimize data analysis, and deliver high-impact insights and strategic recommendations across the business.
Unlocking new layers of payment performance, from uncovering new fraud vectors to maximizing success rates
Serve as a cross-functional mentor, championing a data-driven mindset and elevating the analytical capabilities of team members throughout the company.


What Excites Us


You have a background working in similar fast paced product-driven teams and are great at communicating insights to a range of audiences, including senior stakeholders, to drive data-informed product decisions
You are a self-starter, focused on results, with a demonstrated background in using analytics to drive the understanding, growth, and success of a product
You have extensive experience with data visualisation tools mentioned above and develop compelling visualisations which influence decision making and business understanding
You have advanced SQL skills and intermediate Python skills & have experience leveraging AI capabilities to enhance data analysis, automate tasks, and improve decision-making processes
You have a background working in similar fast paced teams and are great at communicating insights to a range of audiences, including senior stakeholders, to drive data-informed decisions.
You are solution-oriented and able to own projects from start to finish. You are comfortable motivating stakeholders across the business and negotiating timelines to unblock yourself and make things happen!


Salary range: the expected base salary range for this position is between €32,000 - €48,000.

Base salary ranges are based on role, job level, location, and market data. Please note that whilst we strive to offer competitive compensation, our approach is to pay between the minimum and the mid-point of the pay range until performance can be assessed in role. Offers will take into account level of experience, interview assessment, budgets and parity between you and fellow employees at GoCardless doing similar work.

(some of) The good stuff


Wellbeing - stay healthy with dedicated support and medical cover
Work away scheme - gives you the option to work away from your country of residence for up to 90 days in any 12 month period
Adaptive Working - allows you to work flexibly, around your lifestyle
Equity - all permanently employed GCs get equity to help you make a valuable contribution
Parental leave - to suit everyone embarking on life's great adventure
Learning Budget - lead your own development with an annual learning budget
Time off - generous holiday allowance, + 3 annual volunteer days, + 4 annual business-wide wellness days (‘GC Fridays’)


Life at GoCardless

We're an organisation defined by our values; We start with why before we begin any project, to ensure it’s aligned with our mission. We act with integrity, always. We care deeply about what we do and we know it's essential that we be humble whilst we do it. Working this way creates the GC magic- the reason we all love showing up to work.

Diversity & Inclusion

As of April 2025, we had 806 employees (GeeCees) globally, with 524 based in the UK, 163 based in Latvia and 119 across our other offices.

To Ensure That We're Representative Of The World Around Us - And To Be Able To Review Relevant Benchmarks - We Ask GeeCees To Voluntarily Disclose Diversity Data. This Year, The Proportion Of GeeCees Providing Data Increased To 88% (up From 79% In 2024). With Regards To Diversity Within GoCardless, We Can See GeeCees Identifying As

Asian, Black, Mixed or Other — 25%

Neurodiverse — 9%

LGBTQIA+ — 9%

Disabled — 1%

Average age — 33

Female — 45%

Male — 55%

We’re rooting for you during your application and GoCardless aims to provide reasonable adjustments to make our recruitment process as remarkable and accessible as we can. Please speak to your Talent Partner if you need extra support.

If you want to learn more, you can read about our Employee Resource Groups and objectives here

Sustainability

We’re committed to reducing our impact on the environment, leaving a more sustainable world for future generations. Check out our sustainability action plan here.

Find out more about Life at GoCardless via Twitter, Instagram and LinkedIn.

Title: Data Lead (Growth & Analytics)
Description:
MeetMinded is a fast growing startup based in Slovenia, that help turn strangers into friends. Thousands of people attend our local meetups every week - in major cities across the US, UK, Canada and beyond.




We’re looking for someone who’s not just looking for a job, but wants to be part of a story that pushes what’s possible.




If you’re the kind of person who doesn’t just get things done, but does them better than most — keep reading.




As our Data Lead (Growth & Analytics) you'll own everything data: event tracking, pipelines, the dashboards that drive our decisions. If a metric matters to the business, you built the system that surfaces it.




We're an AI-first team - that includes BI. We're building AI-powered dashboards and we want someone who finds that exciting and has done this in the past.




We're headquartered in Ljubljana city centre and the team works on-site. Our team is international and English is our first office language.




We trust good instincts and use data to pressure-test our thinking, not replace it. We hold ourselves to a high bar because we genuinely believe we're building something that can be the best in its space.




And we're just getting started!




WHAT YOU'LL BE DOING




Data infrastructure

– Own data architecture decisions and tooling selection across marketing, revenue, finance, product, and operations

– Set up and maintain a data warehouse

– Set up experimentation tooling

– Ensure data accuracy, freshness, and consistency

– Implement data validation, anomaly detection, and monitoring




Tracking

– Build attribution tracking for Meta Ads, Google Ads, email and other channels

– Measure reliable data at every step of the acquisition funnel

– Diagnose and resolve tracking discrepancies across platforms

– Work with other teams to implement and QA tracking changes




Data analysis

– Translate data into clear, actionable recommendations for leadership

– Build forecasting models for revenue, churn, and growth scenarios

– Identify leading indicators and growth constraints

– Support strategic decisions around pricing, roadmap, and scale strategy

– Implement cohort dashboards, churn prediction tools, and LTV/CAC reporting

– Segmentation by geographies, niches, channels, etc

– User feedback analysis

– Operational efficiency analysis




Experimentation

– Set success metrics and guardrails for experiments

– Run A/B tests on landing pages, onboarding funnels, pricing pages, upsells, etc

– Evaluating A/B tests and suggesting new ones




Product & business insights

– Analyze user behavior across onboarding, activation, engagement, and retention

– Identify friction points and drop-offs in core user journeys

– Partner with product, marketing, and ops to drive improvements




WHAT WE EXPECT




Essential:

– At least 3 years of relevant experience

– Experience working with a subscription/SaaS business (familiar with metrics such as MRR, churn, cash conversion cycle,...)

– Comfortable working with SQL, data modeling, BI, retention/LTV analysis, attribution, and forecasting.

– AI-first mindset - ability to heavily rely on AI tools and build in such a way that we can keep leveraging AI in the future

– Strong revenue strategy mindset: understands monetisation, pricing, payback logic, and CAC/LTV trade-offs.

– Comfortable working in ambiguity, adapting to changes and wearing multiple hats

– Ability to identify ideal software and integrate it into the company’s workflow

– Attention to detail and the ability to work independently without constant oversight

– A lot of drive, energy, and time.

– Ability to work from our office in Ljubljana city center

– Fluent in English (written and spoken)




Bonus points:

– Previous startup experience (especially early-stage)

– Experience building teams from scratch

– You are based close to Ljubljana (or willing to move)




WHAT WE OFFER

– a chance to be part of a fast-growing story and shape products used across the world

– real ownership

– opportunity to grow fast into a higher role

– a small, sharp team that moves fast and sweats the details

– direct impact: your work shows up in real numbers and real users

– room to experiment, test new ideas, and push what’s possible




⬇️ Fill out the form and join us ⬇️

Title: Analyst (Research and Analysis)
Description:
About The Department

The Financial Stability function is responsible for identifying and assessing risks that may threaten the stability of the financial system and the broader economy. This includes analysing systemic risks and evaluating how developments in the domestic economy, the financial services sector, and the external environment may influence the resilience of the financial system. The function is composed of two complementary teams. The Financial Stability Team focuses on the assessment and monitoring of systemic and sector-specific risks through the use of sensitivity analysis, stress testing and forward-looking risk assessments. This work is complemented by macroprudential research across the banking, insurance, and securities sectors, as well as broader areas such as cyber, climate, geopolitical and concentration risks. It also contributes to the design and calibration of macroprudential policies, assessing their impact and effectiveness. The Intelligence and Research Team is responsible for the function’s statistical capabilities, to carry out quantitative and qualitative research and analysis to assess the national and global economic environment and financial markets, structural shifts as well as market practices that may influence the evolution of the financial services sector. The team also evaluates the impact of these developments, including any measures and initiatives proposed to address them.

About The Role

As an Analyst (Research and Analysis) within the Intelligence and Research team you will be assigned to carry out work which will include the following duties:


Conducting research and assisting in the development of data models to support the MFSA strategic priority areas with a focus on new business models and innovation, particularly in areas such as sustainable finance, digital transformation and AI;
Carrying out qualitative and quantitative market-wide research;
Contributing to impact assessments and cost benefit analysis to evaluate the implications of regulatory changes;
Contributing to the collection of the statistical capabilities of the function;
Drafting analytical reports and technical papers, both for internal and publication purposes;
Attending and contributing to technical meetings within the MFSA as well as at national and international level;
Carrying out any other responsibilities delegated by the line manager or other members of senior leadership in line with the function’s remit and/or the Authority`s exigencies.
Undertake any other duties as assigned by the Head and/or as may be required by the MFSA from time to time based on the exigencies of the Authority.


About You

The selected candidate is expected to be a confident individual who feels at ease working in a multidisciplinary environment, is flexible, proactive, dynamic, has the ability to prioritise tasks, has a and a even when under time pressure. The individual is also required to demonstrate good research and have excellent oral, written and presentation skills.

You will hold qualification at MQF Level 6 or higher in statistics, mathematics, banking and finance, economics or in any other relevant field.

A minimum of three (3) years prior experience is required, preferably within the financial services sector or related fields.

If you do not have the necessary academic qualifications but have at least seven (7) years of relevant experience, we would still be interested in speaking with you.

You Will Also Have The Below Skillset


Experience of working with business intelligence tools, such as MS PowerBI
Knowledge of programming languages and data analysis packages, such as R, Python or SQL
Critical and analytical mind-set
High attention to detail
High level of integrity


Other Important Information

The MFSA is an Equal Opportunities Employer as certified by the NCPE (National Commission for the Protection of Equality) and is committed to a policy of equal opportunity in all aspects of employment and will take care to avoid any form of discrimination in its recruitment procedures. The MFSA reserves the right to withdraw this call at any time and not to select any of the Candidates.

It is the responsibility of applicants in possession of qualifications awarded by Universities and other similar institutions outside Malta to produce a recognition statement on comparability of qualifications issued by the Malta Qualifications Recognition Information Centre (MQRIC). Applicants should do so preferably at application stage or otherwise at the preliminary interview should an applicant be selected for such interview. Details can be obtained by accessing the National Commission for Further and Higher Education website on www.ncfhe.org.mt under MQRIC heading.

The MFSA shall ensure that any processing of personal data is in accordance with Regulation (EU) 2016/679 (General Data Protection Regulation), the Data Protection Act (Chapter 586 of the Laws of Malta) and any other relevant European Union and national law. For further details, you may refer to the Data Privacy Notice on the MFSA webpage - https://www.mfsa.mt/privacy-notice/.

Candidates are to note that the submission of any false statement/s or omission, even if unintended, may lead to the cancellation of their application and may render the candidate’s appointment liable to termination.

Furthermore, please note that candidates may be asked to submit any documentation in support of the information provided, including but not limited to, proof of qualifications and Police Conduct Certificates.

Title: Finance Data Analyst
Description:
About Bolt

Bolt is shaping a future where cities are built for people, not cars. What started as an ambitious project of a 19-year-old in 2013 has grown into a global mobility platform used by more than 200 million customers and 4.5 million driver and courier partners across 50 countries.

From ride-hailing and food delivery to scooters, e-bikes, and car-sharing, Bolt helps people move through cities every day. Small, autonomous teams drive this work, combining the speed and ownership of a startup with the scale of a global technology company backed by more than €1bn in funding.

And we're just getting started.

Why join Bolt?


Build the future of mobility and help transform how people live and move in cities.
Tackle challenges at scale that span technology, operations, regulation, and growth.
Take real ownership early. Small, lean teams mean your decisions matter from day one, and you'll learn fast alongside some of the best people in the industry.
Join one of Europe's leading technology companies, backed by world-class investors including Sequoia.


About The Role

As a Finance Data Analyst, you’ll transform financial data into insights that power smarter decisions and real-world impact. This role is all about turning numbers into action - automating data flows, uncovering trends, and helping our teams make data-driven decisions faster and better. You’ll collaborate across departments to refine and improve financial processes, build insightful dashboards, and ensure everything runs seamlessly behind the scenes. In this fast-paced environment, you’ll be the go-to expert for clean, accurate data and clear, actionable insights -driving change and making a difference.

Main Tasks And Responsibilities


Extract, compile, and validate financial data from internal databases, ERP systems and external sources, keeping core processes, such as monthly VAT reporting, accurate and on time.
Consolidate and analyse financial data to fuel monthly reporting, ad-hoc deep-dives, and strategic decisions across the business.
Automate data processes and reporting workflows, cutting manual work and levelling up data quality wherever you can.
Build dashboards and visualisations that make financial insights instantly clear and put faster decisions within everyone's reach.
Collaborate closely with stakeholders and functional teams, understanding their data needs and helping them leverage analytics to enhance their work.
Promote a data-driven culture and continuous improvement, proactively identifying and leading process enhancements while documenting best practices and data management standards.


About You


You come from finance, fintech or a related field, and financial data and processes feel like home turf.
You work daily with SQL and Excel; bonus points if you've also touched Python, or data modelling.
You're genuinely curious about data, with the analytical sharpness and structured mindset to turn questions into answers.
You’ve worked with VAT or tax reporting before, and you're comfortable turning that data into dashboards and reports stakeholders actually use.
You move fast solo and just as well in a team, owning your work from kickoff to finish line.
You communicate information in a clear and structured way.


Why you’ll love it here


Accelerate your professional growth with unique career opportunities.
Enjoy a rewarding salary and stock options, knowing that as Bolt succeeds, so do you.
Take care of your physical and mental health with our wellness perks.
Celebrate 5 years at Bolt with a 1-month paid sabbatical to recharge.
Connect with colleagues at annual company events and smaller team gatherings.
Balance flexibility and in-person collaboration with our hybrid model, including at least 12 monthly in-office days.


Some perks may differ depending on your location and role.

At Bolt, using AI daily is a baseline expectation for everyone. If there’s a faster, smarter way to do your work, we expect you to find it. You’re still responsible for the output, so the standard stays high when using AI tools.

Experience is great, but what we really look for is drive, intelligence, and integrity. So even if you don’t tick every box, please consider applying!

Everyone is welcome

Bolt is an equal opportunity employer. We are committed to building a diverse and inclusive workplace where all qualified applicants are considered for employment without regard to race, colour, nationality, religion, gender or gender identity, sexual orientation, age, or disability.

We welcome applications from all backgrounds and are happy to make reasonable accommodations throughout our hiring process. If you require any adjustments, please let us know.

Title: Data Analyst (Dealing / Trading Analytics)
Description:
We are a leading trading platform that is ambitiously expanding to the four corners of the globe. Our top-rated products have won prestigious industry awards for their cutting-edge technology and seamless client experience. We deliver only the best, so we are always in search of the best people to join our ever-growing talented team.

We are hiring a Data Analyst to partner closely with our Dealing team to improve trading and operational decision-making through data. This person will build and maintain analytics and reporting capabilities, translate business needs into well-scoped data solutions, and deliver actionable insights to support product evaluation and day-to-day operations. Strong SQL and Python skills are required, along with proven experience in trading/fintech/financial markets.

Responsibilities:


Partner day-to-day with the Dealing team to support trading and operational workflows with data and insights
Build and enhance analytics and reporting capabilities to support product evaluation, trading performance, and operational monitoring
Gather business requirements and apply market/trading context to scope data requests, define metrics, and recommend practical solutions
Analyze large, disconnected datasets to synthesize insights and highlight risks/opportunities
Collaborate with business leaders and cross-functional stakeholders to enable market-facing solutions powered by data
Communicate findings clearly (written and verbal) and deliver solutions reliably and on time
Ensure data quality by validating inputs, monitoring key metrics, and improving definitions/documentation where needed



Requirements:


Strong SQL and database experience (querying, joining, modeling/structuring data for analysis)
Advanced working knowledge and experience with Python
Basic statistical knowledge and ability to apply it appropriately in analysis and reporting
Demonstrated experience manipulating, processing, and extracting value from large, messy, and/or disconnected datasets
Strong understanding of trading/dealing concepts and market microstructure (execution, pricing, liquidity, slippage, exposure, hedging basics)
Familiarity with anti-fraud / trading abuse patterns and investigation workflows
Experience working with API connections (REST/WebSocket)
Experience with machine learning / deep learning / data science methods (nice to have)



What you will get in return:


Competitive Salary: We believe great work deserves great pay! Your skills and talents will be rewarded with a salary that makes you feel valued and motivated
Work-Life Harmony: Join a company that genuinely cares about you - because your life outside of work matters just as much as your time on the clock
Generous Time Off: Need a breather? Our annual leave policy lets you recharge and enjoy life outside of work without a worry
Employee Referral Program: Love working here? Share the love! Bring your talented friends on board and get rewarded for growing our awesome team
Comprehensive Health & Pension Benefits: From medical insurance to pension plans, we’ve got your back. Plus, location-specific benefits and perks!
Workation Wonderland: Live your digital nomad dreams with 30 extra days to work remotely from anywhere in the world (some restrictions apply). Adventure awaits!
Volunteer Days: Make a difference! Take two additional paid days each year to support causes you care about and give back to the community



Be a key player at the forefront of the digital assets movement, propelling your career to new heights! Join a dynamic and rapidly expanding company that values and rewards talent, initiative, and creativity. Work alongside one of the most brilliant teams in the industry.

Title: Technical Data Analyst - Data Warehouse Services
Description:
Established in 2004, we are a tech pioneer offering world-class adult entertainment and games on some of the internet’s safest and most popular platforms. With the support of an international team of dynamic and collaborative innovators, we are on a mission to enable safe user experiences and empower our communities by celebrating diversity, inclusion, and expression — all while maintaining robust trust-and-safety protocols.

We embrace the best of both worlds! Local talent can thrive in our collaborative office space with the flexibility of a hybrid work environment, while remote team members play an integral role in shaping our dynamic culture from afar. We have offices in Montreal (Quebec), Austin (Texas) and Nicosia (Cyprus).


A select number of positions require full-time in office attendance*


The Data Warehouse Services team plays a vital role in our organization. They are responsible for storing vast quantities of data from many sources and responsible for disseminating data across the organization to various different groups. They are one of the backbones of data at our organization and they are seeking a Senior Data Analyst to join their team.

What you'll be doing:


Collaborate with internal stakeholders including; Directors, Analysts, and Developers to understand business objectives and requirements, and help define the strategy, content, and features for our reporting environments.
Engage with business partners at all levels to discuss and clarify data analysis, needs and requirements.
Analyze data structures and content from data marts to support business intelligence initiatives.
Thrive in a fast-paced environment, where multitasking and adaptability are essential.
Participate in brainstorming sessions to define functional requirements, organize content, map process flows, and specify report features.
Develop and optimize queries to investigate data issues or new reporting structures for various business units.
Work closely with Database Administrators and Developers to estimate time and effort for report projects and refining these estimates throughout the project.
Collaborate with Project Managers to track project status, identify potential risks,and resolve issues as they arise.
Become a subject matter expert in relevant business domains and reporting tools.
Assist in coordinating and maintaining development roadmaps to ensure alignment with business goals.


What you'll need to be successful:

Must Haves:


5+ years of experience in a similar role.
Knowledge of tools to create, extract, and analyze from SQL Server / Excel / PowerBI / Fabric / Cubes.
Strong customer support experience.
Understanding Data flow Concepts.
Understanding Business Model Concepts.


As an equal opportunity employer, we celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees

In this role you may be exposed to adult content

Title: Junior Data Analyst
Description:
Cencora, previously known as AmerisourceBergen, is a leading global pharmaceutical solutions organization centered on improving lives. Ranked #21 on the Global Fortune 500, our team members are united in our responsibility to create healthier futures.

Our Shared Service Center in Lithuania is experiencing rapid growth and we have many diverse and exciting roles in Customer Service, Operations, IT, Finance and HR. Join us and make a positive impact on human and animal health.

Job Details

We are seeking a motivated Analytic Support Junior Analyst to join our Analytic Support team under the guidance of the Analytic Support Manager. This role offers the opportunity to lead impactful projects, work across departments to deliver analytics solutions, and support data-driven decision-making across the organization.

This is a dynamic role with opportunities for professional growth, including advanced analytics, project management, and leadership development.

Responsibilities


Operate as the first point of contact for World Courier employees by keeping track of reporting incidents.
Ensure the resolution of basic incidents, and/or escalate to senior team members based on incident complexity.
Support business requests related to reporting system access and reporting system usage.
Create, update and debug Alteryx workflows.
Perform initial assessment of SQL stored procedures to identify potential data errors.
Assist in administration of Alteryx Server and Alteryx Gallery environments.
Actively review queues and monitor tool usage.
Run periodic reports in Excel. Ensure timely report delivery to stakeholders.
Support data extraction from SQL database based on requests from different business segments.
Serve as a resource for interpreting data and information.
Create and/or update documentation, self-service guides reporting tools.
Work closely with all other teams in Analytics to coordinate cross-functional activities and ensure deliverables are met.
Support senior team members on enhancements to existing tools and processes.


Education


Bachelor’s degree in a relevant discipline (e.g., Mathematics, Computer Science, Engineering, Economics).


Work Experience


Minimum 1+ years of experience in analytics and reporting, ideally within logistics or a related field.


Skills And Knowledge


Planning, analytical, and conceptual skills to evaluate business problems to identify and apply appropriate solutions.
Knowledge of Microsoft Word, PowerPoint, Outlook, and Excel (essential).
Ability to write and understand Structured Query Language (SQL).
Experience with relational databases (e.g. Microsoft SQL) and data management (desirable).
Ability to create and/or understand one of the following - Alteryx Designer, Power BI (desirable).
Experience with ServiceNow or any other ticket management system (desirable).
Ability to effectively participate in cross-functional global teams.
Intellectual curiosity and a self-starter mindset.
Strong organizational skills with attention to detail.
Ability to build strong relationships and collaborate across teams.


Salary: 1780 – 2543 eur gross/month

What Cencora offers

We offer a competitive annual bonus, life insurance from Day 1, a best-in-class health insurance package, and up to 6 fully paid benefit days a year. As a Cencora employee, you have the benefit of our referral bonus scheme, our boundless learning opportunities and our global Employee Assistance Program. We have a wonderful office location in Quadrum, equipped with everything you need for a small break at work and fresh snacks at all times. Become part of our purpose-driven, multicultural team now and help us create healthier futures

  • Operate as the first point of contact for World Courier employees by keeping track of reporting incidents.
  • Ensure the resolution of basic incidents, and/or escalate to senior team members based on incident complexity.
  • Support business requests related to reporting system access and reporting system usage.
  • Create, update and debug Alteryx workflows.
  • Perform initial assessment of SQL stored procedures to identify potential data errors.
  • Assist in administration of Alteryx Server and Alteryx Gallery environments.
  • Actively review queues and monitor tool usage.
  • Run periodic reports in Excel. Ensure timely report delivery to stakeholders.
  • Support data extraction from SQL database based on requests from different business segments.
  • Serve as a resource for interpreting data and information.
  • Create and/or update documentation, self-service guides reporting tools.
  • Work closely with all other teams in Analytics to coordinate cross-functional activities and ensure deliverables are met.
  • Support senior team members on enhancements to existing tools and processes.

Education

  • Bachelor’s degree in a relevant discipline (e.g., Mathematics, Computer Science, Engineering, Economics).

Work Experience

  • Minimum 1+ years of experience in analytics and reporting, ideally within logistics or a related field.

Skills And Knowledge

  • Planning, analytical, and conceptual skills to evaluate business problems to identify and apply appropriate solutions.
  • Knowledge of Microsoft Word, PowerPoint, Outlook, and Excel (essential).
  • Ability to write and understand Structured Query Language (SQL).
  • Experience with relational databases (e.g. Microsoft SQL) and data management (desirable).
  • Ability to create and/or understand one of the following - Alteryx Designer, Power BI (desirable).
  • Experience with ServiceNow or any other ticket management system (desirable).
  • Ability to effectively participate in cross-functional global teams.
  • Intellectual curiosity and a self-starter mindset.
  • Strong organizational skills with attention to detail.
  • Ability to build strong relationships and collaborate across teams.

Salary: 1780 – 2543 eur gross/month

What Cencora offers

We offer a competitive annual bonus, life insurance from Day 1, a best-in-class health insurance package, and up to 6 fully paid benefit days a year. As a Cencora employee, you have the benefit of our referral bonus scheme, our boundless learning opportunities and our global Employee Assistance Program. We have a wonderful office location in Quadrum, equipped with everything you need for a small break at work and fresh snacks at all times. Become part of our purpose-driven, multicultural team now and help us create healthier futures

Title: Product Analyst
Description:
Working at Infobip means being part of something truly global. With 75+ offices across six continents, we’re not just building technology — we’re shaping how more than 80% of the world connects and communicates.

As employees, we take pride in contributing to the world’s largest and only full-stack cloud communication platform. But it’s not just what we do, it’s how we do it: with curiosity, passion, and a whole lot of collaboration.

We operate with an AI-first mindset, embedding intelligent tools into our daily workflows to work smarter and more efficiently. Every role here benefits from and contributes to this approach.

If you're looking for meaningful work and challenges that grow you in a culture where people show up with purpose, this is your opportunity.

Let’s build what’s next, together.

What this role is all about?

We are seeking a Product Analyst to own analytics end-to-end for an Infobip self-service area - from defining what to measure, through instrumentation and analysis, to delivering actionable recommendations that inform product decisions.

You'll work embedded in a cross-functional team alongside product managers, designers, engineers, and researchers. You won't just report on what happened - you'll help the team figure out what to do next.

What You'll Do


Own product analytics for your area: define metrics, identify gaps in measurement, and ensure data quality
Design, plan, and analyze A/B tests - including determining sample size, statistical significance, and when to call results
Conduct funnel, cohort, and segmentation analysis to identify bottlenecks and opportunities
Build interactive dashboards tailored to stakeholder decision-making needs, not just reporting
Co-define behavioral metrics with product and engineering and ensure instrumentation captures what matters
Translate findings into product implications and clear recommendations - not just charts
Monitor user behavior from acquisition through activation and retention
Challenge assumptions with data - push back when the numbers don't support the hypothesis
Use AI-assisted workflows for data exploration, anomaly detection, and pattern recognition


What makes you a strong fit?


Experience with event tracking implementation and instrumentation design
Experience in product analytics or data analysis within cross-functional product teams
Strong knowledge of statistics theory and experience in applying statistical concepts in practice
Proficiency with product or web analytics tools (e.g., Amplitude, Mixpanel, Google Analytics) and BI tools (e.g., Power BI, QlikSense, Looker)
Experience working with and extracting/manipulating large datasets
Experience designing and interpreting A/B tests (not just monitoring someone else's)
Ability to connect analysis to product decisions - portfolio of work that influenced what got built, changed, or killed
Clear communication of findings to non-technical stakeholders


Nice To Have Qualifications


Knowledge of growth marketing concepts and revenue metrics
Python or R for statistical analysis
Experience with competitive/industry analytics


Why you'll love it here


Financial rewards & recognition - A fair compensation aligned with your experience, industry, and market standards, performance-driven bonuses, regular reviews to support your growth and recognize your contributions, and a culture that values your impact.
Flexible work arrangements - We combine in-person collaboration with remote work and flexible working hours, because great ideas happen everywhere - and not always between 9 and 5.
ESOP (Employee Stock Ownership Plan) - As an Infobip employee, you’ll have the opportunity to share in our company’s success through stock options.
Work-life balance and Well-being  - We offer time off when you need it, special leave days for life’s big moments, and a flexible hybrid work model tailored to local regulations.
Career mobility - Your career is a journey. With internal mobility, upskilling, and mentorship, we help you shape your path. 
Professional development - Learning never stops. Onboarding, mentorship, and training programs help you grow - no matter where you start.
International mobility - Ready to take your career global? Explore short and long-term opportunities in our Hubs worldwide. 


While some benefits may vary by location, our goal remains the same: to support your growth, well-being, and success - wherever you are. 

Diversity drives connection

Infobip is built on diverse backgrounds, perspectives, and talents. We’re proud to be an equal-opportunity employer and are committed to fostering an inclusive workplace.

No matter your race, gender, age, background, or identity — if you have the passion and skills to thrive, there’s a place for you here.

All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, ancestry, religion, age, sex, sexual orientation, gender, gender identity, national origin, citizenship, disability, veteran status or any other part of one's identity.

Read more about our hiring process.

Title: Marketing Data Analyst
Description:
Why Sintra?

50,000+ small business owners use Sintra every day. Not because it's another SaaS tool — but because our AI helpers actually feel like teammates. They reply to customers, post on social media, analyze data, and much more. For someone running a business alone, this is the first time they've had help that actually helps.

We've built something people care about. Now we need to make it great.

About Sintra As a Company


Small team. We believe in hiring less but hiring better.
Not a 9–5. Building a startup is hard. Building a great startup is even harder.
Close environment. You'll work closely with founders and key decision makers.
Freedom. No micromanagement. Lots of trust and ownership.
Care. We stay close to our customers and build products that solve real problems.


About The Role

We're looking for a Marketing Data Analyst who will own marketing analytics across the company and help our Growth and Marketing teams make smarter, data-driven decisions.

You will work across multiple acquisition channels, stakeholders and initiatives, ensuring marketing data is reliable, accessible and actionable.

We're looking for someone who doesn't stop at reporting what happened but can explain why it happened and what we should do next.

Responsibilities


Own marketing data and analytics across the organization.
Ensure data quality, consistency and accuracy.
Build and maintain dashboards and reporting frameworks.
Analyze marketing channel performance and campaign effectiveness.
Work across multiple data sources and systems.
Support attribution and marketing measurement initiatives.
Generate actionable insights and recommendations for business stakeholders.
Help Marketing and Growth teams make data-driven decisions.
Create a scalable framework for measuring marketing performance.
Partner closely with Marketing leadership and channel owners.


Requirements:


4+ years of experience as a Marketing Data Analyst or similar role.
Experience working with digital marketing data.
Experience in a SaaS business environment.
Strong SQL skills.
Experience working with DBT.
Strong business mindset and stakeholder management skills.
Ability to communicate insights and recommendations clearly.
Comfortable navigating ambiguity and shifting priorities.
Strong ownership mentality and proactive approach.


Tech Stack: Google Cloud Platform (BigQuery), DBT, Lightdash, Mixpanel, Growthbook.

Company offers:


Hybrid work model.
Daily team lunch prepared by our in-house chefs.
Stock options.
MacBook and everything you need to do your best work.


If you're excited about marketing analytics, growth and helping scale one of Europe's.

Monthly Salary:

Salary: €4,000–€6,000 gross/month depending on experience.

Benefits


Equity package: share in the company’s long-term success through an equity plan that rewards your impact and commitment.
Flexible working model: enjoy a flexible approach to work that supports a healthy balance between your professional and personal life.
Private health insurance: comprehensive private health coverage to support your well-being.
Workout sessions with a personal trainer: access guided workout sessions with a personal trainer to help you stay active, healthy, and motivated.
Daily office-provided lunch: fresh, complimentary lunch provided in the office every workday to keep you energized and focused.


By submitting your application you confirm that you have read and understood our Privacy Notice for Job Candidates: https://bit.ly/4b9hhlE

Title: Analytics Engineer, Marketplace Strategy & Planning
Description:
Brief Info About Vinted

Our mission is to make second-hand the first choice, and we're looking for people who want to help us get there. Every day, we work together to help our members buy and sell pre-loved clothing and lifestyle items, giving each piece a second life – or even a third.

The Vinted Group is made up of three business units that support this mission:

Vinted Marketplace is Europe’s leading platform for second-hand fashion and a go-to destination for all kinds of pre-loved items, with a growing range of categories. Our platform connects millions of members across 20+ markets, helping great items find a new life.

Vinted Go enhances the shipping experience with a vast network of over 500,000 pick-up and drop-off points, partnering with more than 60 carriers across Europe, with added services like item verification for peace of mind on high-value pieces.

Vinted Pay is the newest part of the Vinted Group, dedicated to bringing secure, reliable payments to buyers and sellers across Europe. Seamlessly integrated into the Vinted app, it helps keep every transaction safe, efficient, and easy for our members.

Founded in 2008 in Lithuania, Vinted began as a way for friends to find new homes for clothes they no longer needed. In 2019, we became Lithuania's first unicorn! Today, our headquarters remain in Vilnius, and we've grown with offices across Europe, supported by a team of over 2,000 people.

Information about the position

As an Analytics Engineer in the Marketplace Strategy & Planning team, you will build analytical data models, dashboards, and other data products that provide insight into the performance of Vinted’s Marketplace and help shape our strategy.


About the team: Marketplace Strategy & Planning domain identifies growth opportunities for Vinted’s Marketplace in different markets, categories and other business opportunities. As the data team inside Strategy & Planning we quantify and measure our Marketplace strategy, including setting OKRs, defining metrics, tracking markets and competition, and forecasting of our performance. We work on highly visible strategic projects, with a wide range of stakeholders and data sources, and deliver the data products to support strategic decision making.
About the role: This is an end-to-end analytical role, where you will architect, build and maintain complete data products. You will work closely with two other Analytics Engineers in the Marketplace Strategy & Planning domain and be part of Vinted’s broader 40+ person Analytics Engineering Guild, benefiting from shared best practices, tooling, and regular knowledge sharing sessions.


In this position, you’ll


Architect and build scalable and reliable data models and pipelines in dbt, BigQuery, and Looker that turn internal and external source data into datasets and dashboards used for reporting, forecasting, and analyses.
Implement data quality checks, tests, and controls to improve the reliability and trustworthiness of our data.
Translate stakeholder needs into technical delivery by gathering requirements, aligning metric definitions, and turning them into well-documented models and metrics.
Create and enable data products that help stakeholders understand marketplace and competitor performance and make informed decisions.


About You


2+ years of working experience (or alternative activities resulting in a similar level of proficiency) as an Analytics Engineer or a closely related role (e.g. BI Engineer, Data Engineer or Data Analyst) with ownership of production pipelines
Strong technical skills, comfortable in our analytics engineering tech stack, which includes SQL/Google BigQuery, dbt, Python, Looker/LookML, Git.
Experience with data modeling and interested in developing and maintaining data products at scale, while balancing scalability, efficiency and reliability.
Excellent attention to detail. You care about data quality, edge cases, and consistency across pipelines, metrics and dashboards. You value documentation and clean, tidy code
Proactive and independent: you look for ways to iterate on and improve the data products you and your team own, and improve stakeholder experience.
You communicate clearly and proactively, and can be a reliable partner for business and technical stakeholders both within the domain and the broader organisation. You are comfortable in ambiguous and complex situations.


Studies show that women and members of underrepresented communities often apply for jobs only if they meet 100% of the qualifications. If you don’t recognise yourself in all of the above, you might still be an excellent candidate for this position. If this role excites you, we encourage you to apply.

Work perks


The opportunity to benefit from our share options programme
25 working days of holiday
Access to all the tools & tech needed for work
Home office support: we provide IT workstation equipment and a personal budget of up to €540 for home workplace furniture
Private health insurance
Digital mental and emotional health support and Employee Assistant Program (EAP)
Frequent team-building events
A personal monthly budget for shopping on Vinted
The opportunity to spend up to 90 days per year - 21 of which can be spent working outside of the EU - on workation
A dog-friendly office
In Vilnius office: Gym & in-house meals at friendly prices
In Kaunas office: a monthly lunch allowance, and a once-a-week provided in-house lunch and breakfast


Working at Vinted

Individual Learning Budget

We invest in your professional growth! As part of our commitment to continuous learning, we offer an annual learning budget to support your personal and career development through courses, certifications, workshops and more.

Hybrid Work

We’ve adopted a hybrid workplace model where 2 days in the office are recommended but not enforced. It’s up to you and your team to decide on the exact days you’ll spend working together in person.

Equal Opportunity

The Vinted Group is committed to building an inclusive workplace where people from all walks of life feel a sense of belonging. We welcome applications from people of all backgrounds, identities and life experiences. At Vinted, all applicants are treated fairly without regard to their race, age, religion or belief, sex, national origin, citizenship, gender identity, sexual orientation, disability, or any other protected characteristic.

The gross monthly salary range for this position is:

€3.433—€4.650 EUR

Title: Junior Data Analyst
Description:
Description

We are seeking a motivated and detail-oriented Junior Data Analyst to join our dynamic team. This entry-level position offers an excellent opportunity for individuals who are passionate about data and eager to develop their analytical skills in a professional environment. As a Junior Data Analyst, you will collaborate with various departments to collect, process, and analyze data to support business decision-making. You will work under the guidance of senior analysts and managers to transform raw data into meaningful insights that drive strategic initiatives. The ideal candidate should have a strong foundation in data analysis techniques, be proficient with data tools, and possess a keen eye for accuracy and problem-solving. In this role, you will gain hands-on experience with data visualization, reporting, and database management, contributing directly to the company’s growth and operational efficiency. This position is perfect for someone looking to kick-start their career in data analytics and grow their expertise in a supportive and innovative workplace. Join us and be part of a team that values curiosity, collaboration, and continuous learning, all while making a tangible impact through data-driven insights.

Responsibilities


Collect, clean, and validate data from various sources to ensure accuracy and completeness.
Assist in the development and maintenance of dashboards and reports for internal stakeholders.
Conduct basic statistical analysis to identify trends, patterns, and insights.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to understand data requirements and provide analytical support.
Prepare visualizations and presentations to communicate findings effectively to non-technical audiences.
Support senior analysts in complex data projects and ad hoc reporting tasks.
Monitor data quality and identify areas for process improvement or data enhancement.


Requirements


Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, Economics, or a related field.
Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and experience with data visualization tools such as Tableau or Power BI.
Basic knowledge of SQL for querying databases.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with attention to detail.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
Ability to work collaboratively in a team-oriented environment.
Eagerness to learn new technologies and data analysis methodologies.

This position is perfect for someone looking to kick-start their career in data analytics and grow their expertise in a supportive and innovative workplace. Join us and be part of a team that values curiosity, collaboration, and continuous learning, all while making a tangible impact through data-driven insights.

Responsibilities


Collect, clean, and validate data from various sources to ensure accuracy and completeness.

Prepare visualizations and presentations to communicate findings effectively to non-technical audiences.

Job Type: Contract

Location: Remote

Experience working in fast-paced, data-driven organizations.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
We are looking to hire a data analyst to join our data team. You will take responsibility for managing our master data set, developing reports, and troubleshooting data issues. To do well in this role you need a very fine eye for detail, experience as a data analyst, and a deep understanding of the popular data analysis tools and databases. Data Analyst Responsibilities: Managing master data, including creation, updates, and deletion. Managing users and user roles. Provide quality assurance of imported data, working with quality assurance analysts if necessary. Commissioning and decommissioning of data sets. Processing confidential data and information according to guidelines. Helping develop reports and analyses. Managing and designing the reporting environment, including data sources, security, and metadata. Supporting the data warehouse in identifying and revising reporting requirements. Supporting initiatives for data integrity and normalization. Assessing tests and implementing new or upgraded software and assisting with strategic decisions on new systems. Generating reports from single or multiple systems. Troubleshooting the reporting database environment and reports. Evaluating changes and updates to source production systems. Training end-users on new reports and dashboards. Providing technical expertise in data storage structures, data mining, and data cleansing. Data Analyst Requirements: Bachelor s degree from an accredited university or college in computer science. Work experience as a data analyst or in a related field. Ability to work with stakeholders to assess potential risks. Ability to analyze existing tools and databases and provide software solution recommendations. Ability to translate business requirements into nontechnical, lay terms. High-level experience in methodologies and processes for managing large-scale databases. Demonstrated experience in handling large data sets and relational databases. Understanding of addressing and metadata standards. High-level written and verbal communication skills.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Job Title: Data Analyst

About Busek

Busek Co. Inc. is a pioneer in advanced space propulsion and technology. Located in Natick, MA, we develop innovative solutions that enable the future of space exploration. Our products and technologies include electric propulsion systems, chemical propulsion, and advanced material development. Join our team to work on cutting-edge projects that shape the space industry.

Position Overview

Busek is seeking a Data Analyst to support the analysis of test and production data for advanced space propulsion systems. This role will work closely with Engineering, Test Operations, and Manufacturing to transform large volumes of technical data into meaningful insights that improve product performance, manufacturing processes, and production readiness.

The ideal candidate is analytical, curious, and self-motivated, with the ability to work independently and quickly learn new systems and technologies. While experience with electric propulsion is beneficial, candidates with strong data analysis skills and experience supporting engineering or manufacturing organizations are encouraged to apply.

Key Responsibilities


Analyze data generated from engineering testing, production, and manufacturing activities to identify trends, anomalies, and opportunities for improvement
Develop reports, dashboards, and visualizations that communicate technical findings to engineering and leadership teams
Partner with Engineering, Manufacturing, and Test Operations to support product development and production initiatives
Validate data quality and ensure the integrity of engineering and manufacturing datasets
Support root cause investigations through data analysis and statistical evaluation
Assist in developing automated reporting and data analysis tools to improve efficiency
Document analysis methods, assumptions, and results to support engineering decisions
Present findings and recommendations clearly to technical and non-technical audiences
Support continuous improvement initiatives by identifying opportunities to improve data collection, reporting, and decision-making


Qualifications


Bachelor's degree in Engineering, Data Analytics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Physics, Statistics, or a related technical field
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to interpret complex technical data
Experience analyzing large datasets using Excel and other data analysis tools
Ability to work independently, manage multiple priorities, and learn new technologies with minimal supervision
Strong written and verbal communication skills
Attention to detail and a commitment to producing accurate, actionable analysis


Preferred Skills


Experience using Python, MATLAB, SQL, Power BI, Tableau, or similar analytical tools
Experience supporting engineering, manufacturing, aerospace, or other technical organizations
Familiarity with statistical analysis and data visualization techniques
Experience working with production, manufacturing, or test data
Knowledge of electric propulsion, aerospace systems, or other advanced engineering technologies is a plus but not required


Benefits


Health, dental, and vision insurance
Paid time off and holidays
Retirement savings plan (401k)
Professional development opportunities
Collaborative and innovative work environment


What We Offer


Opportunity to work on cutting-edge space propulsion technology
Exposure to advanced engineering, testing, and manufacturing operations
Opportunity to influence product development and production decisions through meaningful data analysis
Collaborative environment working alongside engineers developing next-generation space systems


U.S. Citizenship Requirement

Due to the nature of work performed under U.S. government contracts and applicable security and contractual requirements, this position is limited to U.S. citizens.

Title: Data Analyst Internship
Description:
About ConnectPrep: Our goal is to redefine "tutoring" - to use data, insight, and our extensive teaching experience find solutions for our students. ConnectPrep is about connecting - connecting students, parents, educators, and the community. We have bold ideas and an eye towards the future.

The Opportunity: We are offering a Data Analyst Internship for qualified candidates. This is a cross-disciplinary role, both instructional and technical. You'll engage with our team of educators to validate and iterate the models that drive our analytics. Our Data Analysts develop an understanding of our algorithms, the technologies and platforms that support our products, and our proprietary educational content. This knowledge will then be synthesized into actionable tests to build modules that will assist in identifying the academic needs of each individual student. You will engage with team members that think outside of the box, with the goal of improving education through the creation of a data infrastructure and insightful analytics.

Requirements

What we are looking for:


Core skills
You enjoy creative problem solving and working with messy, real-world data.
You have strong math/quantitative skills and excellent attention to detail.
You are curious about how data can improve learning and student outcomes.
Academic & technical background
You are studying (or have studied) Applied Mathematics, Statistics, Computer Science, Data Science, or a related field.
You have some coding experience and a strong interest in technology and AI.
You have experience with at least one of the following:
SQL, Python data tools (Pandas, NumPy, or similar)
Building simple data pipelines or analysis scripts in Python
Excel & analysis skills
You are comfortable doing serious work in Excel, including several of the following:
Advanced formulas and functions
Tables and formatting
Conditional formatting
Advanced charting and visualizations
Pivot tables and pivot reporting
Using Excel productively for analysis, not just basic spreadsheets
Data tables, simulations, and Solver
(Bonus) VBA and macros
AI, data, and analytics work you will support
Work with AI tools and workflows to support data-driven decision-making
Perform data mining and cleaning from multiple sources (web, internal trackers, marketing platforms)
Support projects involving AI and chatbot development for ConnectPrep's programs and website
Conduct SEO and keyword research to identify growth opportunities and track performance
Analyze website analytics (traffic, engagement, conversions) and prepare simple dashboards or summary reports
Assist with data analysis for marketing campaigns, content performance, and business operations
Help test, document, and iterate on new data and AI workflows as they are developed
Nice to have
Prior internship or project experience in data analysis, business intelligence, or marketing analytics
Familiarity with web analytics and marketing tools (for example, Google Analytics, GA4, Search Console, Looker Studio, or similar platforms)
Experience visualizing data in tools such as Tableau, Power BI, or Data Studio
Experience working with SEO tools or keyword platforms
Experience collaborating in remote or hybrid teams (Slack, Zoom, project management boards)
Benefits

Why join ConnectPrep?


Get in on the ground floor of an exciting new company, with the potential to help shape our direction
Be involved with a team that has bold ideas, with the goal of reinventing education across the globe
Gain valuable experience, meet technology innovators, and learn about the business side of our company


Sound Interesting?

Please submit your resume and letter of interest at careers@connectprep.com.

All applicants must be a U.S. Citizen or National of the U.S., an alien lawfully admitted for permanent residence, or an alien authorized to work in the U.S. for this employer. ConnectPrep is an equal opportunity employer EEO/M/F/D/V.
