Title: Jr People Data Analyst
Description:
Anduril Industries is a defense technology company with a mission to transform U.S. and allied military capabilities with advanced technology. By bringing the expertise, technology, and business model of the 21st century’s most innovative companies to the defense industry, Anduril is changing how military systems are designed, built and sold. Anduril’s family of systems is powered by Lattice OS, an AI-powered operating system that turns thousands of data streams into a realtime, 3D command and control center. As the world enters an era of strategic competition, Anduril is committed to bringing cutting-edge autonomy, AI, computer vision, sensor fusion, and networking technology to the military in months, not years.

About The Team

The People Analytics team is at the forefront of transforming how we understand and optimize our workforce. We are a dynamic group of analytical thinkers and storytellers who leverage advanced analytics and robust methodologies to create strategic insights from complex HR data. Collaborating closely with HR Business Partners, Program Management Teams, Business Operations, Talent Acquisition and various cross functional stakeholders we provide actionable insights that drives informed decisions across the entire employee lifecycle; from optimizing talent acquisition and development to enhancing engagement and retention. Our work directly shapes a thriving employee experience, fuels organizational growth, and ensures our people strategy is truly data-driven.

About The Job

The People Data Analyst owns the reporting and data quality foundation that enables our People Analytics team to deliver insights across Talent Acquisition and People Operations. You'll write the SQL, maintain the dashboards, run audits, and fulfill the ad hoc requests that keep our stakeholders informed and our data trustworthy.

This role is ideal for an early-career analyst who thrives on precision, wants deep exposure to HR data systems end-to-end, and is looking to build the technical muscle that underpins a career in people analytics. You'll work closely with our Senior Analysts — learning how upstream data work translates into downstream strategy while owning your own deliverables from day one.

What You'll Do


Provide critical support to the Senior People Data Analysts by executing standard data runs, owning reporting requests, and maintaining operational continuity across both TA and People Ops domains.
Write and optimize SQL queries against our data warehouse to extract, clean, and prepare datasets for analysis.
Maintain, troubleshoot, and perform minor enhancements on existing dashboards to ensure they remain accurate, functional, and up to date.
Own the execution of regular data quality audits across core systems (e.g., HRIS, ATS, LMS), identifying discrepancies and coordinating with internal systems admin teams to correct input errors.
Manage the intake and fulfillment of standard, internal ad-hoc data requests from various cross functional teams ensuring timely and accurate delivery.


Required Qualifications


1–3 years of professional experience in a data analyst, business intelligence, or HR operations role.
Solid proficiency in SQL for data extraction, joining tables, and data manipulation.
Intermediate to advanced Excel skills (e.g., pivot tables, index/match, formulas, data cleaning).
Hands-on experience navigating and utilizing business intelligence tools (e.g., Tableau, Power BI, Looker) for data visualization and report generation.
Exceptional attention to detail with a proven track record of identifying data discrepancies and running quality assurance checks on large datasets.
Bachelor’s degree in a quantitative or technical field (e.g., Information Systems, Business Analytics, Computer Science, Statistics, Economics) or equivalent practical experience.


Preferred Qualifications


Familiarity with HR systems and data structures (e.g., Greenhouse ATS, Gem CRM, Rippling, Workday).
Basic understanding of core HR and recruiting metrics (e.g., headcount, turnover, time-to-hire, pipeline conversion, retention).
Familiarity with data warehouse concepts or version control (e.g., Git).
Familiarity with Python or R for basic data manipulation tasks.
Familiarity with Palantir Foundry


US Salary Range

$112,000—$149,000 USD

Benefits

The salary range for this role is an estimate based on a wide range of compensation factors, inclusive of base salary only. Actual salary offer may vary based on (but not limited to) work experience, education and/or training, critical skills, and/or business considerations. Highly competitive equity grants are included in the majority of full time offers; and are considered part of Anduril's total compensation package. Additionally, Anduril offers top-tier benefits for full-time employees, including:

At Anduril, we invest in our people. Our comprehensive, competitive benefits package (available at little to no cost to employees) ensures you’re supported in health, recovery, and whatever comes next. For more information, Explore Our Benefits.

Protecting Yourself from Recruitment Scams

Anduril is committed to maintaining the integrity of our Talent acquisition process and the security of our candidates. We've observed a rise in sophisticated phishing and fraudulent schemes where individuals impersonate Anduril representatives, luring job seekers with false interviews or job offers. These scammers often attempt to extract payment or sensitive personal information.

To Ensure Your Safety And Help You Navigate Your Job Search With Confidence, Please Keep The Following Critical Points In Mind


No Financial Requests: Anduril will never solicit payment or demand personal financial details (such as banking information, credit card numbers, or social security numbers) at any stage of our hiring process. Our legitimate recruitment is entirely free for candidates.
Please always verify communications:
Direct from Anduril: If you receive an email from one of our recruiters, it will only come from an @anduril.com address.
Via Agency Partner: If contacted by a recruiting agency for an Anduril role, their email will clearly identify their agency. If you suspect any suspicious activity, please verify the agency's authenticity by reaching out to contact@anduril.com.
Exercise Caution with Unsolicited Outreach: If you receive any communication that appears suspicious, contains grammatical errors, or makes unusual requests, do not engage. Always confirm the sender's email domain is @anduril.com before providing any personal information or clicking on links.
What to Do If You Suspect Fraud: Should you encounter any questionable or fraudulent outreach claiming to be from Anduril, please report it immediately to contact@anduril.com. Your proactive caution is invaluable in protecting your personal information and upholding the security and trustworthiness of our recruitment efforts.

Data Privacy

To view Anduril's candidate data privacy policy, please visit https://anduril.com/applicant-privacy-notice/.

By submitting your application, you consent to Anduril Industries using a third-party service provider to conduct pre-employment risk, integrity, and due diligence screening and assessing potential risks as part of your application process. This third-party service provider provides risk-intelligence services that may include analysis of sanctions and watchlists, adverse media, public-record information, and other lawful open-source or commercial data sources. This third-party service provider does not act as a consumer reporting agency. Use of this provider helps to ensure compliance with applicable laws and protect technology, intellectual property, and organizational security.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Job Title: Data Analyst

About Busek

Busek Co. Inc. is a pioneer in advanced space propulsion and technology. Located in Natick, MA, we develop innovative solutions that enable the future of space exploration. Our products and technologies include electric propulsion systems, chemical propulsion, and advanced material development. Join our team to work on cutting-edge projects that shape the space industry.

Position Overview

Busek is seeking a Data Analyst to support the analysis of test and production data for advanced space propulsion systems. This role will work closely with Engineering, Test Operations, and Manufacturing to transform large volumes of technical data into meaningful insights that improve product performance, manufacturing processes, and production readiness.

The ideal candidate is analytical, curious, and self-motivated, with the ability to work independently and quickly learn new systems and technologies. While experience with electric propulsion is beneficial, candidates with strong data analysis skills and experience supporting engineering or manufacturing organizations are encouraged to apply.

Key Responsibilities


Analyze data generated from engineering testing, production, and manufacturing activities to identify trends, anomalies, and opportunities for improvement
Develop reports, dashboards, and visualizations that communicate technical findings to engineering and leadership teams
Partner with Engineering, Manufacturing, and Test Operations to support product development and production initiatives
Validate data quality and ensure the integrity of engineering and manufacturing datasets
Support root cause investigations through data analysis and statistical evaluation
Assist in developing automated reporting and data analysis tools to improve efficiency
Document analysis methods, assumptions, and results to support engineering decisions
Present findings and recommendations clearly to technical and non-technical audiences
Support continuous improvement initiatives by identifying opportunities to improve data collection, reporting, and decision-making


Qualifications


Bachelor's degree in Engineering, Data Analytics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Physics, Statistics, or a related technical field
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to interpret complex technical data
Experience analyzing large datasets using Excel and other data analysis tools
Ability to work independently, manage multiple priorities, and learn new technologies with minimal supervision
Strong written and verbal communication skills
Attention to detail and a commitment to producing accurate, actionable analysis


Preferred Skills


Experience using Python, MATLAB, SQL, Power BI, Tableau, or similar analytical tools
Experience supporting engineering, manufacturing, aerospace, or other technical organizations
Familiarity with statistical analysis and data visualization techniques
Experience working with production, manufacturing, or test data
Knowledge of electric propulsion, aerospace systems, or other advanced engineering technologies is a plus but not required


Benefits


Health, dental, and vision insurance
Paid time off and holidays
Retirement savings plan (401k)
Professional development opportunities
Collaborative and innovative work environment


What We Offer


Opportunity to work on cutting-edge space propulsion technology
Exposure to advanced engineering, testing, and manufacturing operations
Opportunity to influence product development and production decisions through meaningful data analysis
Collaborative environment working alongside engineers developing next-generation space systems


U.S. Citizenship Requirement

Due to the nature of work performed under U.S. government contracts and applicable security and contractual requirements, this position is limited to U.S. citizens.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Werfen is a growing, family-owned, innovative company founded in 1966 in Barcelona, Spain. We are a worldwide leader in specialized diagnostics in the areas of Hemostasis, Acute Care Diagnostics, Transfusion, Autoimmunity, and Transplant. Through our Original Equipment Manufacturing (OEM) business line, we research, develop, and manufacture customized assays and biomaterials. We operate directly in 30 countries, and in more than 100 territories through distributors. Our Headquarters and Technology Centers are located in the US and Europe, and our workforce is more than 7,000 strong.

Our success comes from a specific focus in these rapidly evolving diagnostic areas, our commitment to customers, and our dedication to innovation and quality. We’re passionate about providing healthcare professionals the most valuable and complete solutions to improve hospital efficiency and enhance patient care.

Overview

Position Summary:

The Data Analyst role will directly support the Hemostasis Applications team by:


Managing reagent sequesters for Integrated Health Networks, including establishing systems for monitoring lot status, lot expiration, usage trends, and other related metrics
Establishing good documentation practices for performance verification studies and annual lot rollover analysis
Serving as a liaison with the Commercial Operations team to resolve data integrity issues related to tools and reports needed to effectively run the applications business
Define and execute a system for managing and communicating forecasts for installs and go-lives
Act as a SME for current and future platforms used by the Applications team – Wrike, Apps Tracker, Fiori, and Salesforce.com


The Data Analyst will routinely partner with Applications, Sales, Marketing, and Commercial Operations.

Responsibilities

Key Accountabilities


Migrate validated and enriched data that can be trusted for downstream analytics.
Work with highly refined and aggregated data to power analytics and production automation capabilities.
Collaborate with the team and business stakeholders to identify requirements, define models, and initiate new analytics initiatives.
Maintain deep knowledge of Werfen Business and all datasets within the data lake to provide guidance to stakeholders.
Apply appropriate data models to address specific business problems.
With stakeholders’ needs in mind, leverage creative solutions, analytics, and visualization/reporting techniques to enhance knowledge and drive actionable insights that deliver ROI.
Identify trends, risks, and opportunities for the business that could impact objectives, strategy, and tactics. Present insights to functional management to further understanding of operating results through timely and accurate analyses.
Take responsibility for continuous improvement related to the quality and reliability of data and responsiveness to business requests.
Strong analytical skills – data analysis, data interpretation, and translating data into clear and simple actions
Effective oral and written communication skills
Prior experience in defining and leading newly defined sustainable processes
Ability to work in a fast-paced setting
Organization skills
Ability to collaborate with diverse stakeholders
A continuous improvement mindset
Ability to work independently with a high degree of autonomy
Collaborate regularly with various internal teams, including Applications, Sales, Marketing, and Commercial Operations.
Perform other duties as assigned.


Preferred Attributes


Minimum 3 years of experience in clinical laboratory, medical device, pharmaceutical, hospital, or related technical environments.
Previous experience as a Medical Technologist (ASCP) preferred and/or experience in Hemostasis
Preferred experience in field applications, field engineering, technical support, or sales support environments
Familiarity with SAP, PowerBI, MS Office Suite.


Qualifications

Minimum Knowledge & Experience for the position:


Bachelor’s degree in Business Administration, Finance, Business Analytics, or a related field required. Master’s degree preferred.
1–3 years of work experience in Quality, Operations, Finance, or Business Analysis function required.
Experience in the Medical Device/IVD industry preferred.
Advanced skills with SQL, MS Excel, and Python required.
Strong computer skills, including Microsoft Office, databases, and web-based applications.
Experience with data visualization tools such as MS Power BI or Tableau required.
Experience with CRM and ERP systems required.
Microsoft Azure, Agile, GIT software versioning, and SAP experience preferred.


Management has the discretion of substituting relevant work experience for a degree and/or making exceptions to the years of experience requirement.

Skills & Capabilities


Customer-first mindset.
Excellent aptitude for working with large amounts of data and an eye for detail.
Highly focused, accurate, and thorough in work.
Ability to present data to end users in a digestible manner.
Proven experience as a collaborative team player.
Critical-thinking and problem-solving skills.
Ability to decipher and interpret complex business challenges.
Excellent verbal and written communication skills.
Ability to work independently to organize and prioritize tasks.
Able to thrive in a fast-paced, deadline-driven environment.


Travel Requirements

Less than 5 percent

The annual base salary range for this role is currently $65,000.00 to $85,000.00. Individual employee compensation will ultimately depend on factors including education, relevant experience, skillset, knowledge, and particular business needs.

This role is eligible for medical, dental, and vision insurance, 401k plan retirement benefits with an employer match, as well as paid vacation and sick leave. Our sales roles are eligible for participation in a commission plan and our management, and select professional roles, are eligible for a performance-based bonus.

Closing

If you are interested in constantly learning and being challenged on a daily basis we encourage you to submit your resume or CV.

Werfen is an Equal Opportunity employer and is committed to a diverse workplace. Werfen strictly prohibits unlawful discrimination, harassment or retaliation based upon an individual’s race, color, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity/expression, national origin/ancestry, age, mental/physical disability, medical condition, marital status, veteran status, or any other protected characteristic as defined by applicable state or federal law. If you have a disability and need an accommodation in relation to the online application process, please contact NAtalentacquisition@werfen.com for assistance.

We operate directly in over 30 countries, and in more than 100 territories through distributors. Annual revenue is approximately $2 billion and more than 7,000 employees around the world comprise our Werfen team.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Crown Linen Service, Inc is looking to add an ON-SITE Data Analyst to the team in Brockton, MA!

Join Crown Linen Service Inc as a Full-Time Data Analyst and immerse yourself in an environment where innovation meets excellence. Located in Brockton, MA, this onsite role offers the opportunity to tackle real-world problems and drive strategic initiatives. With a competitive pay range of $80,000 - $90,000, you will be rewarded for your analytical skills and contributions to our forward-thinking team. Experience the thrill of collaborating with passionate professionals dedicated to high performance and integrity in everything they do.

THIS IS NOT A REMOTE POSITION AND REQUIRES DAILY ON-SITE REPORTING TO OUR BROCKTON, MA LOCATION.

You will be provided with great benefits such as Medical, Dental, Vision, 401(k), Life Insurance, Health Savings Account, Flexible Spending Account, Competitive Salary, and Paid Time Off. Don’t miss the chance to shape the future of our services while advancing your career in a supportive and dynamic workplace.

Make a difference as a Data Analyst

We are seeking a Data Analyst to join our data team. We are a small team at a well-established company, giving us the opportunity to make a real and immediate impact. This role focuses on developing reports and automated dashboards to support multiple departments.

What We Offer


A nimble work environment that provides the freedom to explore our data landscape and discover new strategies
An executive team that values the insights that data can provide
A direct impact on the business via reporting, as well as data-driven strategies
A company culture that supports its workforce and is focused on operating an ethical and sustainable business.


What You Bring


Three to five (3-5) years of working experience with progressively more responsibility and advanced work product.
A curious mind - you will often be tasked with vague targets, and it is up to you how to get there.
A collaborative spirit - we touch every aspect of the business and need to be able to work seamlessly across all business functions.
A heightened business acumen - we are not just data folks. We regularly interact with all levels of the organization and must be able to relate our findings to executable business strategies.
A technically savvy approach.
Be an Excel wizard - much like an instrument, you should be able to play Excel like a fiddle
A comfortable working knowledge of how to query data sets across multiple sources with significant exposure to at least one SQL language, including Joins, CTEs, Aggregations and/or Windows Functions (our bread and butter)
The ability to transform vague business requests into concrete data driven solutions
Work experience with at least one BI tool such as PowerBI, Tableau, etc.
The ability to design, implement and maintain an ever-changing ETL landscape

As a new Data Analyst at Crown Linen Service Inc, you can expect a dynamic work environment where each day brings new challenges. Your schedule will be Monday to Friday, with standard business hours dedicated to analyzing data, identifying trends, and providing actionable insights to enhance our services. You will collaborate closely with various departments, facilitating cross-functional meetings to ensure alignment on projects and initiatives. Expect to create and maintain detailed reports, supporting strategic decision-making processes. Your role will involve problem-solving and innovation, requiring you to think critically and propose solutions that drive efficiency.

Additionally, you will participate in ongoing training and development opportunities, allowing you to continuously enhance your skills and knowledge in a high-performance culture.

Make your move

If you're ready to take your Data analyst skills to the next level and join a team that values hard work and your business analyst expertise, apply today!

It is unlawful in Massachusetts to require or administer a lie detector test as a condition of employment or continued employment.

Salary: $80000.00 - $90000.00 per year

Title: SIOP Data Analyst - Direct Hire
Description:
Senior Data Analyst

Department: Office of the COO

Reports To: SIOP Manager

Location: Full‑time Onsite — MetroWest Boston Area

Schedule: 5 days onsite (future potential for 4 onsite / 1 remote)

Target Salary Range: $80,000 – $120,000




About the Company (Disguised)

Our client is a leading provider of advanced manufacturing and automation solutions, operating multiple technology centers across North America. The organization specializes in custom machining systems, automation integration, and turnkey production solutions, supporting a wide range of industrial and high‑precision manufacturing customers.

Position Summary

The Senior Data Analyst is a key analytical partner within the Office of the COO, responsible for transforming complex operational, sales, and service data into strategic insights that drive business performance. This role leads the development of enterprise dashboards, reporting frameworks, and data models that support SIOP, sales operations, and aftermarket service functions.

The analyst collaborates closely with cross‑functional leaders to deliver data‑driven recommendations that enhance revenue growth, operational efficiency, and continuous improvement initiatives.

Key Responsibilities

Lead the collection, integration, and analysis of data from CRM, ERP, and enterprise systems to support sales performance, aftermarket services, and SIOP planning cycles.
Design and maintain advanced dashboards and KPI reports across equipment sales, service revenue, parts consumption, and forecasting accuracy.
Serve as the primary analytics partner for SIOP, providing demand/supply analysis, forecast variance reporting, and scenario modeling for executive decision‑making.
Develop standardized aftermarket analytics, including service contract utilization, customer lifecycle metrics, and parts‑usage trends.
Perform complex statistical analyses to identify trends, patterns, and opportunities for operational and revenue optimization.
Ensure data integrity and governance through rigorous validation, documentation, and quality‑assurance practices.
Collaborate with Sales, Operations, Finance, and Aftermarket teams to translate business needs into scalable, automated reporting solutions.
Lead ad‑hoc analytical projects and present insights and recommendations to senior leadership.

Required Qualifications

Education: Bachelor’s degree in Data Analytics, Statistics, Computer Science, Business Administration, or related field (or equivalent experience).
Experience: 2–7 years in data analysis, business intelligence, or analytics—preferably in manufacturing, distribution, or industrial sales environments.

Technical Skills

Advanced proficiency with Excel, Power BI, Tableau, or similar visualization tools.
Strong SQL skills for complex data extraction and transformation.
Experience with Python, R, or similar analytical programming languages.
Familiarity with ERP/CRM platforms such as Infor CSI, Salesforce, ServiceNow, SAP, or similar systems.
Understanding of SIOP/S&OP planning tools and processes.

Soft Skills

Ability to synthesize complex data into clear, actionable insights for technical and non‑technical audiences.
Strong critical‑thinking and problem‑solving abilities.
Excellent organizational skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast‑paced environment.
Proven cross‑functional collaboration and stakeholder‑management capabilities.

Supervisory Responsibilities

No direct reports; mentorship of junior analysts may be expected.

Travel Requirements

Minimal; occasional travel to regional facilities.

Physical Requirements

Extended periods of computer‑based work.
Ability to lift up to 20 lbs as needed.

Title: Senior Data Analyst, Marketing Analytics
Description:
RSAC is the premier series of global events and year‑round learning for the cybersecurity community. RSAC is where the security industry converges to discuss current and future concerns and gain access to experts, unbiased content, and ideas that help individuals and companies advance their cybersecurity posture and build stronger, smarter teams. 

Both in‑person and online, RSAC brings the cybersecurity industry together and empowers the collective “we” to stand against cyberthreats around the world. RSAC is the ultimate marketplace for the latest technologies and hands‑on educational opportunities, helping industry professionals discover how to make their organisations more secure while showcasing the most enterprising, influential, and thought‑provoking thinkers and leaders in cybersecurity today. 

RSAC is seeking a highly analytical, business-minded Senior Data Analyst, Marketing Analytics to serve as the dedicated analytics partner supporting both Conference and Membership Marketing initiatives. 

This role will be responsible for transforming marketing data into actionable insights that drive acquisition, engagement, retention, and revenue growth. The ideal candidate combines strong technical expertise with exceptional business acumen and stakeholder management skills. They are comfortable moving between strategic discussions with marketing leadership and hands-on analytical execution. 

The Senior Data Analyst will partner closely with Marketing, Conference, Membership, and Data & Analytics teams to build reporting frameworks, measure campaign effectiveness, develop attribution models, identify growth opportunities, and provide executive-level insights that influence business decisions. 

This role reports into the Data & Analytics organization while serving as the primary analytical partner to Marketing. 

What You’ll Do 

Marketing Performance & Campaign Analytics 

Serve as the primary analytics partner for Marketing, supporting both Conference and Membership business lines. 

Develop and maintain reporting frameworks for marketing campaign performance across channels. 

Analyze marketing effectiveness and provide actionable recommendations to improve acquisition, engagement, and retention outcomes. 

Measure campaign ROI and support data-driven budget allocation decisions. 

Establish standardized marketing KPI definitions and reporting methodologies. 

Attribution & Funnel Analytics 

Develop and maintain marketing attribution frameworks across Conference and Membership initiatives. 

Analyze acquisition channels, conversion funnels, and customer journeys to identify opportunities for optimization. 

Partner with Marketing stakeholders to improve lead generation, conversion, and audience growth strategies. 

Evaluate performance across paid, owned, and earned marketing channels. 

Conference Marketing Analytics 

Support conference registration reporting and performance measurement. 

Analyze registration funnel performance and identify conversion opportunities. 

Measure effectiveness of conference marketing campaigns and promotional activities. 

Support sponsorship analytics and attendee engagement reporting. 

Provide insights that help drive conference growth and improve attendee experience. 

Membership Marketing Analytics 

Analyze subscriber acquisition, activation, retention, and lifetime value metrics. 

Measure performance of email campaigns, content marketing initiatives, and member engagement efforts. 

Identify audience behaviors, engagement patterns, and opportunities to improve member retention and growth. 

Partner with Marketing to develop actionable audience segmentation strategies. 

Executive Reporting & Insights 

Design and maintain executive dashboards that provide visibility into marketing performance and business outcomes. 

Translate complex analytical findings into clear recommendations for senior leadership. 

Deliver recurring performance reviews and strategic insights to Marketing leadership and executive stakeholders. 

Serve as a trusted advisor and strategic thought partner to Marketing leadership. 

Data Analysis & Visualization 

Write complex SQL queries to analyze large datasets and answer business questions. 

Develop and maintain dashboards and self-service reporting solutions using Tableau. 

Partner with Data Engineering and Analytics Engineering teams to improve data quality, accessibility, and reporting capabilities. 

Support development of scalable reporting and measurement frameworks. 

Experimentation & Strategic Analytics 

Support marketing testing and experimentation initiatives through measurement design and analysis. 

Develop audience segmentation methodologies to improve targeting and personalization. 

Conduct ad hoc analyses to support strategic business decisions. 

Identify trends, opportunities, and risks through proactive analysis and stakeholder engagement. 

Application Requirements 

Core Requirements 

Bachelor's degree in Data Analytics, Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Information Systems, Marketing Analytics, Business Analytics, or a related quantitative field. 

5–7 years of experience in data analytics, business intelligence, marketing analytics, or related analytical roles. 

Advanced SQL skills with the ability to independently analyze large and complex datasets. 

Experience building dashboards and visualizations using Tableau or similar BI tools. 

Experience working with cloud data platforms such as Snowflake. 

Strong understanding of marketing analytics, campaign measurement, attribution, and performance reporting. 

Experience with audience segmentation, customer lifecycle analytics, retention analysis, cohort analysis and lifetime value modeling. 

Experience communicating analytical findings to business stakeholders and senior leaders. 

Strong problem-solving skills with the ability to translate business questions into analytical solutions. 

Excellent written and verbal communication skills. 

Ability to manage multiple priorities and stakeholders in a fast-paced environment. 

Bonus Points  

Experience supporting marketing, growth, membership, subscription, media, SaaS, or event-based businesses. 

Familiarity with Google Analytics (GA4) and digital marketing measurement concepts. 

Familiarity with dbt and modern data stack technologies. 

Exposure to Python or other analytical programming languages. 

Experience supporting conference, event, or membership-based organizations. 

Experience designing and measuring A/B tests and marketing experiments. 

Success in This Role 

Within the first year, a successful candidate will: 

Establish themselves as the trusted analytics partner for Conference and Membership Marketing teams. 

Independently own marketing analytics initiatives with minimal supervision. 

Deliver executive-ready reporting and dashboards that drive business decisions. 

Implement scalable marketing performance and attribution reporting frameworks. 

Improve reporting speed, consistency, and analytical rigor across marketing functions. 

Provide strategic insights that influence acquisition, engagement, retention, and revenue growth initiatives. 

Earn the confidence of stakeholders as a trusted advisor and thought partner. 

Location & Eligibility 

Please be aware that although this is a remote position, to be considered for the vacancy you must have residency in one of the following states: 

California, Colorado, Florida, Kansas, Massachusetts, New Hampshire, New Jersey, New York, Pennsylvania, Texas, Utah, Virginia, Washington 

Benefits 

RSAC believes in investing in our people. We offer: 

Salary range: $110 – $125 

Employer‑subsidised medical, dental, and vision insurance 

401K retirement employer match 

Home office equipment stipend and monthly technology stipend 

Thirteen paid holidays per calendar year 

Flexible personal time off 

Annual employee bonus dependent on company and personal performance 

Annual company‑wide offsite 

Our Culture 

We believe that our differences make us stronger, and we are committed to fostering a culture of respect, empathy, and understanding. 

We are a fully remote team operating across the United States, giving our employees the flexibility to work from wherever they choose. 

Our team is passionate and results‑oriented, striving to achieve excellence in everything we do. 

We strongly believe in creating an inclusive environment that values diversity and encourages our team members to share their unique perspectives. 

We recognise that by collaborating and working together, we can achieve our goals faster and more effectively. 

Why RSAC? 

The RSAC team takes great pride in helping shape the future of cybersecurity and being part of an expansive global community. We’re always looking for imaginative and visionary individuals who share our passion for providing cutting‑edge programs that equip cybersecurity practitioners worldwide with the intel and knowledge they need to thrive and safeguard organisations against cyberthreats. 

Our Values 

Adaptability: In our ever‑changing world, we innovate through determination, creativity, and resourcefulness. 

Community: We bring people together and build trust by embracing the unique thoughts and perspectives of others with kindness and respect. 

Excellence: Because we are where the world talks security, we have the highest expectations of ourselves and our partners. 

Equal Opportunity Statement 

RSAC is an equal opportunity employer committed to inclusion and diversity. We take affirmative action to ensure all qualified applicants receive consideration for employment without regard to race, colour, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, veteran status, or other legally protected characteristics.

Title: Data & Product Analyst (Benchmarking), CoStar Debt Solutions
Description:
Job Description

Data & Product Analyst (Benchmarking)

Location: Boston, in-office

About The Role

CoStar Debt Solutions is building the next generation of CRE analytics software — a platform where data, benchmarking intelligence, and client workflows come together in a single experience to support how lenders originate, evaluate, and manage risk.

A central part of this vision is our Benchmarking product, which enables lenders to compare loan performance, pricing, and risk characteristics against a trusted market dataset. This product is rapidly evolving, with significant investment in expanding coverage, improving methodologies, and delivering more actionable insights directly into client workflows.

We’re hiring a Data & Product Analyst to help drive the continued growth of this Benchmarking product — both as a data asset and as a highly client-facing solution.

This role combines data analysis, product development, and direct client engagement, with a strong emphasis on supporting client adoption and enabling the sales process. You will work closely with clients and internal sales teams to demonstrate product value, answer analytical questions, and ensure clients can effectively leverage benchmarking insights in their workflows.

In year one, this role will focus on analyzing benchmark data, supporting product enhancements, and actively participating in client meetings, product demos, and sales support activities. Over time, the role expands into shaping benchmark methodologies, influencing product strategy, and helping scale how benchmarking insights are delivered across the platform.

This is a hybrid role at the intersection of data analysis, product development, and client/sales engagement — ideal for someone who can translate complex datasets into clear insights and communicate them effectively to both internal and external audiences.

What You’ll Own


The analysis of complex datasets that power the Benchmarking product — identifying trends, inconsistencies, and opportunities to improve data coverage and quality
The evolution of benchmark methodologies, outputs, and usability in partnership with Product Managers and cross-functional teams
Direct engagement with clients, including leading and supporting client conversations to understand benchmarking use cases, answer analytical questions, and gather actionable feedback
Active support of sales and go-to-market efforts, including participating in product demonstrations, responding to client inquiries, and helping articulate the value of benchmarking insights
Ongoing client support and enablement, ensuring users can interpret and apply benchmark data effectively in their workflows
Collaboration with engineering teams to ensure accurate delivery of benchmark data, calculations, and features
Monitoring product usage and client feedback to identify areas for improvement and drive continuous enhancements
Data validation, quality checks, and troubleshooting to maintain the reliability and integrity of benchmark datasets
Staying current on industry trends and competitive benchmarking solutions to inform product direction


What We Expect You To Bring


Bachelor’s degree from an accredited, not-for-profit, in person, university or college. Ideally in Data Analytics, Data Science, Business Analytics, Finance, Economics, Computer Science, Information Systems, or a related field
2+ years of experience working with data analytics, financial data, or data-driven products
Strong hands-on experience working with large datasets and performing detailed analysis
Strong communication skills, with the ability to engage effectively with both technical stakeholders and clients
Proven ability to support client-facing interactions, including presenting, explaining analytical concepts, and responding to client questions in real time
Experience with analytics or benchmarking products, or similar data-driven solutions
Strong analytical thinking, attention to detail, and problem-solving skills
A track record of commitment to prior employers


Nice To Have


Exposure to SQL, Python, or similar languages
Experience with benchmarking, peer comparison, or performance analytics solutions
Experience supporting client-facing products or working in a customer-oriented environment
Familiarity with financial services, lending, or real estate data
Experience with data visualization tools
Understanding of APIs and data integration concepts


The Growth Path

The first year in this role is focused on deeply understanding the benchmark dataset, client needs, and current product capabilities — contributing directly to data analysis, active client engagement, sales support, and incremental product improvements. You will play a key role in client conversations, product demonstrations, and helping drive adoption of benchmarking insights in real-world workflows.

Beyond that, the role expands into a more strategic position:


Shaping how benchmark methodologies evolve as the dataset scales
Driving enhancements to how insights are surfaced within client workflows
Partnering with sales and client teams to improve how benchmarking is positioned, adopted, and delivered
Influencing product direction based on client usage patterns, feedback, and market trends


This role is a strong path into broader product ownership, client-facing product strategy, or advanced analytics leadership, particularly for someone interested in building and scaling data-driven products that directly support client outcomes and business growth.

What’s Unique About This Role

Most data analyst roles focus on generating insights. This role goes further — connecting those insights directly to product decisions and client outcomes.

You’ll Not Only Analyze Data, But Also


Work directly with clients and sales teams to translate insights into adoption and business value
Play a key role in client-facing discussions that shape both product direction and commercial success
Help bridge the gap between data, product, and revenue-generating activities


What’s in it for you?

When you join CoStar Group, you’ll experience a collaborative and innovative culture working alongside the best and brightest to empower our people and customers to succeed. We offer you generous compensation and performance-based incentives. CoStar Group also invests in your professional and academic growth with internal training, and tuition reimbursement.

Our Benefits Package Includes (but Is Not Limited To)


Comprehensive healthcare coverage: Medical / Vision / Dental / Prescription Drug
Life, legal, and supplementary insurance
Virtual and in person mental health counseling services for individuals and family
Commuter and parking benefits
401(K) retirement plan with matching contributions
Employee stock purchase plan
Paid time off
Tuition reimbursement
On-site fitness center and/or reimbursed fitness center membership costs (location dependent)
Access to CoStar Group’s Employee Resource Groups
Complimentary gourmet coffee, tea, hot chocolate, fresh fruit, and other healthy snacks


This position offers an annual base salary range of $99,000 - $133,000, based on relevant skills and experience and includes a generous benefits plan

We welcome all qualified candidates who are currently eligible to work full-time in the United States to apply. However, please note that CoStar Group is not able to provide visa sponsorship for this position.

CoStar Group is an Equal Employment Opportunity Employer; we maintain a drug-free workplace and perform pre-employment substance abuse testing

#CoStar

Title: Digital and Social Analytics Associate
Description:
Summary

The Digital & Social Analytics Associate will be responsible for supporting the tagging, tracking, reporting and optimizing of content created for Patriots.com, Patriots Mobile App, Patriots social media, GilletteStadium.com, Gillette Stadium social media, and other digital channels across Kraft Sports + Entertainment. The position will also assist with the management of digital and social inventory to help optimize value for external partners and in-house brands.

Duties And Responsibilities


Create regular reports to in-house stakeholders and external partners, leveraging data from Adobe Omniture, Google Analytics, Emplifi, Google Ads, Chartable, iTunes, and other analytics platforms
Tag social posts daily to contribute to analytics reports
Support Digital Marketing team in creation and tracking of campaigns for sponsors and in-house brands
Support fulfillment of sponsor commitments and internal campaign needs across digital and social platforms
Evaluate KPIs in search of meaningful insights to help improve future platform and campaign performance
Provide project management support for organizational marketing initiatives
Consume sports and marketing content across other teams, leagues and brands
Special assignments and projects as business dictates


Supervisory Responsibilities


This position has no supervisory responsibilities


Skills And Qualifications


Bachelor’s degree in Business Analytics or related (or in pursuance of)
0-2 years of professional experience required.
Strong skills in Excel, with proficiency in PowerPoint and Word
Knowledge of digital technologies, software and applications
Quantitative interests (background in Analytics, Accounting, Marketing or similar)
Experience with Facebook, Twitter, Instagram, Snapchat, YouTube and their analytics
Strong communication and interpersonal skills
Highly motivated self-starter with a sense of ownership, willingness to learn, high degree of curiosity
Flexibility in working quickly and efficiently under pressure to handle shifting priorities
Detail and results oriented, with strong organizational and project management skills


PHYSICAL DEMANDS


Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions


WORK ENVIRONMENT


Normal office environment


CERTIFICATES, LICENSES, REGISTRATIONS


None Required


Other Duties

Please note this job description is not designed to cover or contain a comprehensive listing of activities, duties or responsibilities that are required of the employee for this job. Duties, responsibilities and activities may change at any time with or without notice.

This company is an equal opportunity employer. We evaluate qualified applicants without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, veteran status, and other legally protected characteristics.

Title: Modeling Analyst II
Description:
Join the Market Leader in Electric Power Data and Analytics Solutions

The electrical grid is the largest and most complicated machine ever built. Yes Energy’s industry-leading electric power trading analytics software provides real-time visibility into the massive amount of data generated by the North American electrical grid daily. Our unique and innovative view of the data informs real-time trading decisions and mid-to-long-term investment decisions that keep utility prices low, support the energy transition, and keep the grid running. It’s both challenging work and work with a purpose.

Be a part of our successful, growing business during international transformation.

Position Summary

As a Modeling Analyst II, you will be the subject matter expert on fundamental market price forecasting within our EnCompass product line. This role plays a critical part in ensuring that EnCompass meets the needs of our customers by providing advanced customer support to current and future customers, supporting product development and documentation, and testing and updating market databases for North America. The ideal candidate will have deep expertise in power system modeling including production costing, market price formation, and economic transmission analysis.

This position requires close collaboration with Product Management, Engineering, and Sales to ensure the success of our customers, while also providing customer-facing expertise including day to day customer support, onboarding and training.

Position Details


Salary range: $110,000 - $140,000 plus bonus
Full-time
Hybrid or Remote
Reporting to: Senior Director, Customer Success
Travel requirement: Up to 30%


Primary Responsibilities


Ensure customers know how to use our products to their fullest potential by providing exceptional customer service, answering questions, troubleshooting issues, and providing technical support.
Engage existing and new customers in online and in-person training and onboarding - utilizing presentation skills, industry knowledge, and Yes Energy’s product expertise to maximize our customers’ product use experience.
Update and create product documentation, tutorials, and related materials.
Work closely with Product Management and Engineering to facilitate communication about product issues, user experience and to implement and test new EnCompass features.
Assist Power Infrastructure and partner companies in providing robust EnCompass databases for the North American power markets.
Provide subject matter expertise to Sales and Marketing to develop campaigns and participate in industry events.


Minimum Qualifications


Minimum of three years of experience in power system modeling, including market price forecasting, production costing, economic transmission analysis, and/or resource planning.
Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Mathematics, Economics, or a related analytical field.
Experience using Excel or BI tools to analyze data.
Excellent communication skills.
Ability to troubleshoot complex modeling errors and guide customers through resolution.
Ability to travel to customer sites to provide in person training, up to 2 times per month.


Preferred Qualifications


Direct experience with EnCompass, PLEXOS, Aurora, or PROMOD models.
Familiarity with FERC regulations, ISO/RTO operations, and resource adequacy constructs.
Prior experience in a client-facing or consultative role, particularly within the energy sector.


At Yes Energy, we value connecting directly with candidates. We kindly ask that third-party recruiters and agencies not submit resumes, as we are not open to external recruiting partnerships.

Overview

ABOUT YES ENERGY

Yes Energy delivers real-time market data and electric power trading decision solutions. Over 1,000 market participants use Yes Energy solutions daily. The business is a leader in all aspects of information content collection and management, developing and delivering data and market analytics solutions. Since its inception in 2008, Yes Energy has become a trusted and respected supplier of innovative and reliable solutions focused on the needs of power market analysts, traders, and trade managers. Yes Energy has a team of over 350 amazing professionals in Boulder, CO (HQ); Boston, MA; Chicago, IL; Glendora, CA; Richmond, VA; London, United Kingdom; Auckland, New Zealand, Tokyo, Japan; and Bucharest, Romania.

Culture

Yes Energy has been named one of the Best Places to Work in Colorado, and we have the culture to prove it. At Yes Energy, we care about saying “Yes” to customers. We like to listen, learn, and develop our solutions in line with their needs. We think about customers as business partners, and when we help them be more successful … we are more successful, too.

Around the office, our culture is driven by some pretty fundamental values that we’re proud of:


We love innovation and solving tough challenges;
We are “high standards people” who combine passion and pride with hard work and rewards of all kinds-- in an ethic that is consistent across the company;
We’re team-focused with a flat hierarchy-- we work in small teams on well-defined projects that directly impact the success of the business;
We play to the strengths and experience of each person while each of us also works along a continuum of roles adjacent to our focus area. This presents the challenge of maintaining a broad set of skills as well as an opportunity to learn and contribute in many ways;
We are constantly growing. Professional development happens every day and every year.


Compensation And Benefits

We offer highly competitive salaries and real bonuses that are achievable and that you can impact. Our benefits package is also very competitive, including medical insurance, a 401 (k) Plan with matching, flexible vacation, and flexible work schedules. Yes Energy encourages and funds investment in both formal and informal professional development.

At Yes Energy, we are dedicated to building a diverse, inclusive, and authentic workplace. If you’re excited about this role but your experience doesn’t perfectly align with every qualification in the job description, we encourage you to apply anyway. You may be just the right candidate for this or other roles.

In accordance with Colorado law, the range provided is Yes Energy’s reasonable estimate of the base compensation for this role. The actual amount may be higher or lower based on non-discriminatory factors such as location, experience, knowledge, skills, and abilities.

Yes Energy provides equal employment opportunities (EEO) to all employees and applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, or genetics. In addition to federal law requirements, Yes Energy complies with applicable state and local laws governing nondiscrimination in employment in every location where the company has facilities. This policy applies to all terms and conditions of employment, including recruiting, hiring, placement, promotion, termination, layoff, recall, transfer, leaves of absence, compensation, and training.

Title: Competitive Intelligence Research
Description:
As a Senior Analyst in Meta’s Competitive Intelligence organization, you will operate at the intersection of advanced analytics, data science, and market strategy. You will lead major projects and product areas—often in environments of significant ambiguity or technical complexity—driving both technical and business outcomes. This is a hands-on, high-impact role for builders who thrive on solving real problems. This role demands a unique blend of analytical and statistical expertise, strategic thinking , and the ability to translate complex insights into impactful product and business decisions. You will be recognized as a thought partner by cross-functional leads and will help shape the analytical foundations that inform how we build and grow our products.

Competitive Intelligence Research Responsibilities:


Market Strategy: Influence organization-level product direction through data-driven narratives and an in depth understanding of the market landscape. Demonstrated experience operating at scale, and across ambiguous, environments with working knowledge of econometrics. As a quantitative market-strategist, you will blend practical and applied understanding with technical expertise, including pressure-testing data for quality, reliability, understanding data-biases and being solution driven
Analytics Leadership: Conduct advanced analyses with 3P datasets, develop statistical models and forecasts, and deliver actionable insights that informs market and business strategy. These include, but are not limited to:
Data onboarding: Identify, onboard, and rigorously evaluate 3P datasets to determine their signal-to-noise ratio and predictive power
Data triangulation: Triangulate data from many sources of imperfect information. Synthesize multiple, low-fidelity 3rd-party signals into a single high-fidelity trend report using Bayesian aggregation or other methods
Data transformation: Apply quasi-experimental designs (e.g., synthetic control, diff-in-diff) to isolate the impact of exogenous market shocks and competitor actions on internal performance metrics, using 3rd-party behavioral and economic datasets
Insight and implications: Apply guidance from such analyses to increase the accuracy of forecasts and better understand market trends
Technical & Methodological Expertise: Act as a recognized professional in a technical or methodological area (e.g., causal inference, bayesian aggregation), driving the adoption of advanced methods and organization-wide best practices that raise the bar for the entire team
Data Governance & Quality: Ensure data privacy, security, and compliance with organizational standards. Champion data quality frameworks and documentation practices that enable credible reproducible analyses
Resourceful, adaptable professional with a bias for action


Minimum Qualifications:


Bachelors degree and a minimum of 6 years of work experience (minimum of 4 years with a Ph.D.) in business intelligence, product analytics, or economic or strategy consulting in a technology environment with increasing scope and impact
Demonstrated skill to ethically source, validate, and synthesize high-signal insights from people (e.g., stakeholder interviews, skilled conversations, field research, and relationship-based information gathering) while maintaining high standards for privacy, consent, and integrity
Proficiency in AI-powered tools: Demonstrate working knowledge of Generative AI technologies (e.g., LLM and AI agents) and experience designing, prompting, and orchestrating AI systems (e.g., prompt engineering) to automate data analyses, synthesize insights, and execute multi-step analytical tasks (e.g., prompting agent to clean datasets, build visualizations)
Practical working understanding of data-analytics tools, and direct experience managing, analyzing, manipulating and interpreting 1P and external 3P datasets
Experience with data querying languages (e.g., SQL), scripting languages (e.g., Python), and/or statistical/mathematical software (e.g., R)
Proven experience with statistical analysis including causal inference (e.g., randomized control trials, quasi-experimentation such as synthetic control, diff-in-diff, meta-analyses), and/or bayesian aggregation (e.g., bayesian pooling, hierarchical modeling)
Demonstrated communication skills and experience presenting complex findings to both technical and non-technical stakeholders
Demonstrated experience thriving in ambiguous environments and shape new analytics organizations or products


Preferred Qualifications:


Master's or Ph.D. Degree in a quantitative field such as Quantitative Economics or Political Science, Operations Research, Data Science, Computer Science, Physics, Business, or Mathematics
Demonstrated ability to integrate AI tools to optimize/redesign workflows and drive measurable impact (e.g., efficiency gains, quality improvements)
Experience adhering to and implementing responsible, ethical AI practices (e.g., risk assessment, bias mitigation, quality and accuracy reviews)
Demonstrated ongoing AI skill development (e.g., prompt/context engineering, agent orchestration) and staying current with emerging AI technologies


About Meta:

Meta builds technologies that help people connect, find communities, and grow businesses. When Facebook launched in 2004, it changed the way people connect. Apps like Messenger, Instagram and WhatsApp further empowered billions around the world. Now, Meta is moving beyond 2D screens toward immersive experiences like augmented and virtual reality to help build the next evolution in social technology. People who choose to build their careers by building with us at Meta help shape a future that will take us beyond what digital connection makes possible today—beyond the constraints of screens, the limits of distance, and even the rules of physics.

Meta is proud to be an Equal Employment Opportunity and Affirmative Action employer. We do not discriminate based upon race, religion, color, national origin, sex (including pregnancy, childbirth, or related medical conditions), sexual orientation, gender, gender identity, gender expression, transgender status, sexual stereotypes, age, status as a protected veteran, status as an individual with a disability, or other applicable legally protected characteristics. We also consider qualified applicants with criminal histories, consistent with applicable federal, state and local law. Meta participates in the E-Verify program in certain locations, as required by law. Please note that Meta may leverage artificial intelligence and machine learning technologies in connection with applications for employment.

Meta is committed to providing reasonable accommodations for candidates with disabilities in our recruiting process. If you need any assistance or accommodations due to a disability, please let us know at accommodations-ext@meta.com.

$177,000/year to $247,000/year + bonus + equity + benefits

Individual compensation is determined by skills, qualifications, experience, and location. Compensation details listed in this posting reflect the base hourly rate, monthly rate, or annual salary only, and do not include bonus, equity or sales incentives, if applicable. In addition to base compensation, Meta offers benefits. Learn more about benefits at Meta.

Title: Lead Data Analyst
Description:
Do you want to be a part of the clean energy movement? Are you passionate about improving our environment for this generation and those to follow? Are you ready to take on new challenges and collaborate with a future-focused team leading the way into new markets? Join Nexamp! 

This is where you can learn from industry leaders and become one yourself. It's fast-paced, mission-based work that challenges the status quo. Be on the team that's changing the world. 

What we’re looking for: 

We're looking for a Lead Data Analyst to craft insightful and compelling data stories, empower business owners across Nexamp to make more effective data-driven decisions, and help build our self-service data platform. As one of the nation’s largest and longest tenured solar and storage owner operators – and one of the few that is fully vertically integrated – our data is a differentiator in the market and a catalyst for future growth. You’ll be solving uniquely challenging data problems, alongside a strong team, and for a company in which the business model and mission are inseparably intertwined: our solar farms save customers money, help the planet, and are profitable for Nexamp and its investors. 

You will be hybrid out of our Boston office. You will report to the Director, Product Management - Data.

What you’ll do:  

Independently gather requirements for, prioritize, deliver, maintain, and continuously improve reusable data models, dashboards, analyses, A/B tests, and other data assets. Act as the product manager and implementer for your domain’s data offerings. Implement work using advanced SQL and BI tool skills.
Become the subject matter expert for data in your business areas. Identify opportunities for impact, proactively surface insights, support decision-making, and contribute to and influence business strategy and initiatives. Serve as your business stakeholders’ right hand. Support other business areas opportunistically.
Create and maintain comprehensive data documentation and communicate updates to your stakeholders. Write user stories or tickets for data engineering, validate data mapping, conduct QA, and implement data quality monitoring.
Monitor the usage of data assets, increase data literacy, and promote self-service through BI tools among stakeholders. Provide training, guidance, and support as needed.
Work within a centralized data team using agile methodologies. Collaborate with peer analysts through overlapping work, priority shifting, and code reviews to broaden your Nexamp knowledge.
Partner with the data architect, engineers, scientists, and analysts to shape the vision and roadmap for Nexamp’s data platform. Define and track annual OKRs to measure your impact. 

What you’ll bring:

7+ years of experience as a data analyst serving business owners and collaborating with data engineers (experience in the energy/construction industry is a plus, but not required)
Bachelor's degree in a quantitative field would be preferred; advanced degree is a plus.
Advanced in SQL and BI tool administration and usage (experience with Databricks and Sigma are a plus, but not required)
Strong track record of independently solving problems.
Commitment to Nexamp's mission and have a passion for solving tomorrow's climate crisis today.
Demonstrated experience in effectively communicating information, ideas, and perspectives with people inside and beyond your organization.   
Experience in showcasing initiative to make improvements to current work, processes, products, and services across the organization. We value accountability and an ownership mentality. 
Ability to ask appropriate questions, analyze data, identify the root causes of problems, and present creative solutions. 
Expertise in building strong internal and external relationships with customers and stakeholders, instilling trust and loyalty across the industry. 
Eagerness to develop a fundamental understanding of how Nexamp operates and then apply that knowledge effectively to inform business decisions.

If you don’t meet 100% of the above qualifications, but see yourself contributing, please apply.

At Nexamp, our mission is to build the future of energy so it is clean, simple, and accessible for all. We are committed to providing a work environment free from discrimination. We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate against applicants on the basis of race, ethnicity, religion, sex, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, disability status, veteran status, or any other basis protected by law. By encouraging a culture where ideas and decisions come from all people, we believe it will help us grow, innovate, and be a part of environmental and social change.

You’ll love working here because:  

Not only will you get to take part in meaningful work and have the chance to change the world alongside innovative, dedicated, and motivated peers, but you will also have access to all the benefits that Nexamp offers! This includes our competitive compensation package; a 401(k) employer-match; health, dental, and vision insurance starting day one; flexible paid time off and holiday PTO; commuter benefits, and cell phone reimbursement. We have headquarters in Boston, MA and Chicago, IL, in addition to growing offices nationwide. We provide healthy snacks, coffee, service days and other volunteer opportunities, company outings, and more!  

Compensation

The reasonably estimated salary for this role at Nexamp ranges from $110,000 - $130,000. In addition to base salary, the competitive compensation package may include, depending on the role, participation in an incentive program linked to performance (for example, annual bonus programs based on individual and company performance, non-annual sales incentive plans, or other non-annual incentive plans). Additionally, you may be eligible to participate in the Company’s stock option plan. Actual base salary may vary based upon, but is not limited to, skills and qualifications, internal equity, performance, and geographic location. 

Nexamp's People team manages all aspects of recruitment and hiring within our organization. We want to inform third-party recruiters, staffing firms, and related agencies that Nexamp does not accept unsolicited resumes. Resumes will only be considered from these entities if a signed agreement is in place and the People team explicitly authorizes external recruiting assistance for a specific position. Any unsolicited resumes received will be deemed the property of Nexamp. We want to emphasize that Nexamp is not liable for any fees associated with unsolicited resumes.

  • 7+ years of experience as a data analyst serving business owners and collaborating with data engineers (experience in the energy/construction industry is a plus, but not required)
  • Bachelor's degree in a quantitative field would be preferred; advanced degree is a plus.
  • Advanced in SQL and BI tool administration and usage (experience with Databricks and Sigma are a plus, but not required)
  • Strong track record of independently solving problems.
  • Commitment to Nexamp's mission and have a passion for solving tomorrow's climate crisis today.
  • Demonstrated experience in effectively communicating information, ideas, and perspectives with people inside and beyond your organization.   
  • Experience in showcasing initiative to make improvements to current work, processes, products, and services across the organization. We value accountability and an ownership mentality. 
  • Ability to ask appropriate questions, analyze data, identify the root causes of problems, and present creative solutions. 
  • Expertise in building strong internal and external relationships with customers and stakeholders, instilling trust and loyalty across the industry. 
  • Eagerness to develop a fundamental understanding of how Nexamp operates and then apply that knowledge effectively to inform business decisions.

If you don’t meet 100% of the above qualifications, but see yourself contributing, please apply.

At Nexamp, our mission is to build the future of energy so it is clean, simple, and accessible for all. We are committed to providing a work environment free from discrimination. We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate against applicants on the basis of race, ethnicity, religion, sex, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, disability status, veteran status, or any other basis protected by law. By encouraging a culture where ideas and decisions come from all people, we believe it will help us grow, innovate, and be a part of environmental and social change.

You’ll love working here because:  

Not only will you get to take part in meaningful work and have the chance to change the world alongside innovative, dedicated, and motivated peers, but you will also have access to all the benefits that Nexamp offers! This includes our competitive compensation package; a 401(k) employer-match; health, dental, and vision insurance starting day one; flexible paid time off and holiday PTO; commuter benefits, and cell phone reimbursement. We have headquarters in Boston, MA and Chicago, IL, in addition to growing offices nationwide. We provide healthy snacks, coffee, service days and other volunteer opportunities, company outings, and more!  

Compensation

The reasonably estimated salary for this role at Nexamp ranges from $110,000 - $130,000. In addition to base salary, the competitive compensation package may include, depending on the role, participation in an incentive program linked to performance (for example, annual bonus programs based on individual and company performance, non-annual sales incentive plans, or other non-annual incentive plans). Additionally, you may be eligible to participate in the Company’s stock option plan. Actual base salary may vary based upon, but is not limited to, skills and qualifications, internal equity, performance, and geographic location. 

Nexamp's People team manages all aspects of recruitment and hiring within our organization. We want to inform third-party recruiters, staffing firms, and related agencies that Nexamp does not accept unsolicited resumes. Resumes will only be considered from these entities if a signed agreement is in place and the People team explicitly authorizes external recruiting assistance for a specific position. Any unsolicited resumes received will be deemed the property of Nexamp. We want to emphasize that Nexamp is not liable for any fees associated with unsolicited resumes.

Title: Senior Associate - Data Analyst
Description:
Job Description

We are currently seeking a senior associate data analyst to help us collect, transform and extract meaning from data for the Global Technology Services (GTS) Control Tower team at State Street. The position performs a wide range of activities with experts from GTS, business intelligence, business analysis, data engineering, process engineering, governance, and data analysts to collaboratively solve a wide array of complex technical problems.

About The GTS Control Tower Team

The GTS Control Tower team is an exciting and growing area of State Street with unique insight into the whole spectrum of our data, metrics, and dashboards at State Street. This name change reflects the work we are actively doing across GTS: integrating dependency management, business analysis, process design, and governance to transform data into action and keep remediation efforts flying smoothly.

 

The GTS Control Tower Mission

Our core focus is on transforming data into actionable insights. Our goal is to enable GTS Leadership to accelerate progress against key strategic initiatives across GTS and to drive results.  

Core Responsibilities


Report & Dashboard Development: Design, develop, and maintain high‑quality, accessible interactive Power BI dashboards and reports that clearly communicate key performance indicators (KPIs) and business insights.
Data Modeling & Transformation: Build and optimize semantic data models, including relationships, calculated columns, and measures using DAX. Perform data extraction, transformation, and loading (ETL) using Python and Power Query (M).
Data Source Integration: Connect, consolidate, and manage data from multiple sources such as SQL databases, Azure services, Excel, and other enterprise systems into reliable, well‑structured datasets.
Performance Optimization: Tune data models and DAX calculations to ensure optimal performance, scalability, and user experience.
Security & Platform Administration: Implement and maintain row‑level security (RLS), manage Power BI workspaces and apps, and support capacity and governance best practices within the Power BI Service.
Stakeholder Collaboration: Partner with business stakeholders to gather requirements, translate business needs into analytical solutions, and deliver actionable, decision‑ready insights.
Documentation & Standards: Create and maintain clear documentation for data models, transformation logic, report definitions, and operational procedures to support transparency and long‑term maintainability.



What We Value


Willingness to learn and develop new skills, embracing challenges and continuous improvement
Strong work ethic, focus on achieving goals, and delivering measurable results
Desire for creative thinking, problem-solving, and proposing new ideas to improve processes or products
People who thrive in a collaborative team environment



Requirements


An online portfolio with examples with nonconfidential data that demonstrates your analysis skills
Some experience with Microsoft Power BI, DAX, Power Query, M, ETL, Python, PySpark, SQL
A Bachelor's degree in a data analytics, data science, computer science or equivalent experience



Salary Range

$52,000 - $87,880 Annual

The range quoted above applies to the role in the primary location specified. If the candidate would ultimately work outside of the primary location above, the applicable range could differ.

Employees are eligible to participate in State Street’s comprehensive benefits program, which includes: our retirement savings plan (401K) with company match; insurance coverage including basic life, medical, dental, vision, long-term disability, and other optional additional coverages; paid-time off including vacation, sick leave, short term disability, and family care responsibilities; access to our Employee Assistance Program; incentive compensation including eligibility for annual performance-based awards (excluding certain sales roles subject to sales incentive plans); and, eligibility for certain tax advantaged savings plans.

For a full overview, visit https://hrportal.ehr.com/statestreet/Home.

About State Street

Across the globe, institutional investors rely on us to help them manage risk, respond to challenges, and drive performance and profitability. We keep our clients at the heart of everything we do, and smart, engaged employees are essential to our continued success.

We are committed to fostering an environment where every employee feels valued and empowered to reach their full potential. As an essential partner in our shared success, you’ll benefit from inclusive development opportunities, flexible work-life support, paid volunteer days, and vibrant employee networks that keep you connected to what matters most. Join us in shaping the future.

As an Equal Opportunity Employer, we consider all qualified applicants for all positions without regard to race, creed, color, religion, national origin, ancestry, ethnicity, age, disability, genetic information, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, citizenship, marital status, domestic partnership or civil union status, familial status, military and veteran status, and other characteristics protected by applicable law.

Read our CEO Statement

It is unlawful in Massachusetts to require or administer a lie detector test as a condition of employment or continued employment. An employer who violates this law shall be subject to criminal penalties and civil liability.
