Title: HDS Senior Data Analyst
Description:
Site: Mass General Brigham Incorporated

Mass General Brigham relies on a wide range of professionals, including doctors, nurses, business people, tech experts, researchers, and systems analysts to advance our mission. As a not-for-profit, we support patient care, research, teaching, and community service, striving to provide exceptional care. We believe that high-performing teams drive groundbreaking medical discoveries and invite all applicants to join us and experience what it means to be part of Mass General Brigham.

Job Summary

The Senior Data Analyst, within the Mass General Brigham Enterprise Analytics (EA) Department, providing dedicated analytical support to Health Data Solutions (HDS), is responsible for identifying, querying, analyzing, curating and visualizing clinical data to support initiatives throughout Mass General Brigham. The Senior Data Analyst will be responsible for using MGB data assets to build curated datasets according to complex requirements. The position requires close collaboration and dialogue with external collaborators, clinical personnel, various analytic groups, and hospital and system leadership.

The Senior Data Analyst partners closely with clinicians, researchers, and analytics and digital colleagues to translate complex questions into rigorous analytical approaches. The role requires strong expertise in Snowflake SQL, Python, and R, with an emphasis on reproducible, well-documented analytical workflows.

In addition, the Senior Data Analyst will serve as a resource and role model for other data analysts, sharing best practice methodologies, training other analysts and end-users, and growing the team’s wealth of knowledge and documentation. The team primarily leverages Snowflake SQL, Python, R, and the Microsoft Office suite, and is partnering with the MGB Digital data platform teams on a journey to build a new data platform with Microsoft Azure Cloud and Snowflake, and leverages other tools and technologies on an as needed basis.

Principal Duties and Responsibilities


Partners with researchers, clinicians, and other stakeholders to define research-oriented analytic questions and data requirements, translating study objectives and protocols into clear analytical specifications.
Queries and analyzes data from hospital systems such as our electronic medical record system (Epic), enterprise data warehouse (EDW), vendor neutral archive (VNA) and more, to identify and curate cohorts based on dataset specifications and other project requirements. The analyst should be comfortable performing data wrangling across multiple data sources.
Produces and maintains data dictionaries, dataset versioning and release notes.
Presents data and analyses to key stakeholders, including creating materials for and giving presentations as needed.
Uses programmatic tools such as Python or R to support data QA and exploratory analysis. This includes identifying missing data, detecting outliers, and assessing whether anomalies reflect true variation or unreliable data.
Applies data de-identification techniques and tools to protect sensitive healthcare information in accordance with HIPAA and related regulations.
Develops, maintains, and delivers programmatic analytical outputs, including reproducible reports, analytical datasets, figures, and written summaries, to support stakeholder decision-making.
Collaborates with EA, HDS, and MGB Digital partners to continually improve analytical capabilities using SQL, Python, R, and other programmatic analytics tools, including documentation of analytical methods, assumptions, and limitations.
Performs comprehensive quality assurance, ensuring that data and reports meet end user specifications and are accurate and reliable.
Works with new and existing EA analysts to share knowledge in methods of data analysis, summarization, and presentation. Trains other analysts and end-users on key analytic and reporting systems and databases.
Partner with MGB Digital teams to leverage existing tools, methods, and processes in order to deliver timely information to stakeholders across MGB, including Snowflake SQL.
Effectively recognizes when additional help or guidance is needed and seeks it out proactively.
Represents EA on internal and external committees or task forces as needed.
Performs all other related duties as required.


Skills And Abilities


Strong ability to work and communicate effectively with staff at all levels within the organization, including senior hospital management.
Experience with querying, manipulating, cleansing, and statistically analyzing large data sets using tools such as SQL Server Management Studio, Python, and R.
Strong experience presenting analytical findings using programmatic and narrative-driven approaches, including creation of tables, figures, statistical summaries, and written insights appropriate for executive and clinical audiences. With limited input from others, creates data visualizations that are suitable for presentation to senior leaders.
Demonstrated ability to handle protected health information (PHI) under HIPAA, organizational policies and IRB protocols
Familiarity with LLM-based tools to help review unstructured clinical text, extract structured variables, and support pre-labeling workflows.
Ability to clearly communicate complex and sensitive data to senior executives as well as peers and external collaborators.
Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to tailor message to individuals and audiences throughout the institution.
Strong organizational skills, with the ability to focus detailed concentrated effort on multiple projects and re-establish priorities as necessary.
Possesses initiative and ability to work independently while thriving in a setting requiring collaboration and teamwork for maximal efficiency and effectiveness.
Attention to detail that ensures follow through on all initiatives implemented, and all projects undertaken.

Title: Data Reporting Analyst - Remote
Description:
Put your Insurance Experience to work – FROM HOME!




At WAHVE, we value significant insurance experience and want to revolutionize the way people think about phasing into retirement by offering qualified candidates the opportunity to continue their career working from home. As we say – retire from the office but not from work. Our unique platform provides you with real work/life balance and allows you to choose a full or part-time work schedule while continuing to utilize your insurance expertise in a remote, long-term position which includes company benefits!




WHAT YOU’LL LOVE ABOUT WAHVE




We created a welcoming place to work with friendly and professional leadership. We are known for the great care we take with our staff and our clients. We are passionate and determined about delivering the best customer service, preserving insurance industry knowledge, and making a difference by the work that we do.




WHAT WE ARE SEEKING




We have assignments available to help our insurance carrier or MGA clients in Data Reporting Analyst positions for Commercial Lines. Responsibilities may include:




Support the preparation of ISO claims and premium reports through detailed analysis of ISO statistical plans.
Collaborate with IT, TPA Services and other departments to meet ISO reporting requirements.
Maintain clear, thorough documentation of processes for data extraction, formatting, and submission to appropriate bureaus.
Retrieve data from multiple systems, perform necessary Excel manipulation and submit reports to state regulatory agencies.
Analyze business challenges and data issues related to ISO and state reporting and recommend solutions to reduce downstream errors.
Partner with cross-functional teams (IT, Underwriting, etc.) to identify reporting impacts and implement necessary updates.
Ensure issues are properly identified, tracked, reported and resolved promptly.




TO BECOME A WORK-AT-HOME VINTAGE EXPERT, WE REQUIRE




25 years of full-time work experience
10 recent years of Property & Casualty insurance experience




BENEFITS OF BECOMING A WAHVE VINTAGE EXPERT




Health insurance based on eligibility.
401(k) with a 4% match.
Retire from the office but not from work.
Eliminate the office stress and the commute.
Choose the work you would like to do now.
Available in either full or part-time schedules.
Utilize your years of insurance industry knowledge.
Be part of our dynamic yet virtual team environment and connect with other experienced insurance professionals like yourself!




HOW TO GET STARTED




Click APPLY NOW to complete our simple preliminary profile. Be sure to include your preferred contact information as one of our Qualification Specialists will connect with you promptly.




WE LOOK FORWARD TO MEETING YOU!

Title: MDM Analyst
Description:
Job Title: MDM Analyst – Service Integration (SI) & Master Data Management (MDM)

Location: Boston, MA (Hybrid/Onsite as per client requirement)

Duration: 12+ Months




We are seeking an experienced Business Data Analyst (BA) with strong Service Integration (SI) and Master Data Management (MDM) experience to support a new Service Integration initiative for our client, Vertex. The ideal candidate will work closely with business and technical stakeholders to gather, analyze, and document requirements while ensuring seamless integration across services, business processes, and enterprise master data.




Key Responsibilities

Collaborate with business stakeholders to gather and analyze business requirements for Service Integration and MDM initiatives.
Translate business needs into functional requirements, user stories, and process documentation.
Work with cross-functional teams to support Service Integration planning, execution, and MDM implementation.
Analyze and document master data requirements, data governance processes, and data quality standards.
Conduct gap analysis, process mapping, and workflow optimization.
Facilitate workshops, meetings, and requirement review sessions with business and technical stakeholders.
Support testing activities, including UAT planning, execution, and defect tracking.
Ensure project deliverables align with business objectives, integration standards, and MDM best practices.




Required Skills & Experience

Proven experience as a Business Analyst.
Hands-on experience with Service Integration (SI) projects is mandatory.
Hands-on experience with Master Data Management (MDM) initiatives is mandatory.
Strong expertise in requirements gathering, business process analysis, and documentation.
Experience working with enterprise master data domains (e.g., Customer, Product, Vendor, or Supplier data).
Understanding of data governance, data quality, and master data lifecycle management.
Experience working with cross-functional business and technical teams.
Excellent stakeholder management and communication skills.
Familiarity with Agile/Scrum methodologies is preferred.




Preferred Qualifications

Experience working on enterprise integration, digital transformation, or data management initiatives.
Exposure to MDM platforms such as Informatica MDM, Reltio, SAP MDG, IBM InfoSphere MDM, or similar solutions is a plus.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
About Us:

American Technology Consulting (ATC) is a service-first tech company dedicated to helping businesses unlock their full potential through innovative technology solutions. We empower organizations to leverage data for smarter decisions and meaningful impact. At ATC, we foster a culture of growth and invite passionate individuals to join our team, contribute to impactful projects, and excel as data-driven problem solvers. We embrace diversity and welcome candidates from all educational and professional backgrounds, including recent Master’s graduates without prior technical experience.




Job Overview:

As an Entry-Level Data Analyst at ATC, you will be at the forefront of turning raw data into actionable insights. This role offers a fantastic opportunity to work on real-world challenges, collaborate with cross-functional teams, and apply your problem-solving skills in a supportive and innovative environment.




Key Responsibilities:

Collect, clean, and organize data to prepare it for analysis.
Identify trends, patterns, and correlations using statistical and analytical tools.
Develop insightful dashboards, visualizations, and reports using tools like Tableau, Power BI, or Excel.
Assist teams in making data-driven decisions by presenting clear and concise insights.
Collaborate with stakeholders to understand data requirements and deliver solutions.
Ensure data accuracy and consistency through validation and quality checks.
Stay updated on industry trends, best practices, and new data tools or technologies.




Qualifications:

Master’s degree.
Strong analytical and critical thinking skills.
Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and a willingness to learn data visualization tools like Tableau or Power BI.
Excellent communication skills to present findings to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
Attention to detail and ability to manage multiple priorities effectively.
Candidates must have U.S. work authorization.




Preferred Qualifications:

Internship or project experience in data analysis or visualization.
Familiarity with SQL for querying and managing databases.
Knowledge of statistical concepts and tools like Python or R (a plus but not required).




What We Offer:

Career Growth: Opportunity to advance in a dynamic and supportive work environment.
Mentorship: Guidance and training from experienced professionals in the field.
Diversity in Projects: Work across various industries and innovative technologies.
Comprehensive Benefits: Competitive salary, health insurance, and other perks.




Work Authorization Support:

H1B sponsorship or related visa support for eligible candidates.
Applications from individuals on F1/OPT are welcome.




Equal Opportunity Commitment:

ATC is proud to be an equal-opportunity employer, fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace where all employees can thrive.

Title: Logistics Data Analyst
Description:
We are seeking a Logistics Risk Analyst to support logistics operations through data analysis, reporting, and risk monitoring. This role will analyze operational data, identify trends and risks, develop dashboards, and provide actionable insights to improve supply chain performance and operational efficiency.




Key Responsibilities

Analyze logistics and supply chain data to identify operational risks and performance trends.
Develop and maintain dashboards, reports, KPIs, and risk metrics.
Monitor shipment delays, damages, losses, and service performance.
Perform root cause analysis using data and reporting tools.
Prepare regular operational and risk reports for leadership.
Collaborate with IT and operations teams to improve data quality and reporting processes.
Support audits, compliance reviews, and logistics risk assessments.
Recommend process improvements based on analytical findings.




Required Qualifications

Bachelor's degree in Data Analytics, Supply Chain, Engineering, Statistics, Business, or a related field.
3–6 years of experience in logistics analytics, supply chain analytics, operations analytics, or business analytics.
Experience analyzing large datasets and creating dashboards.
Strong analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills.
Ability to work in a fast-paced environment while managing multiple priorities.

Advanced Microsoft Excel (Pivot Tables, VLOOKUP/XLOOKUP, INDEX-MATCH, Formulas, Data Matching & Comparison)

Title: BI Engineer I
Description:
Opportunity

Job Description:

We are seeking an entry level Business Intelligence Engineer to join our dynamic team. As a key member of our Customer Care data analytics department, you will play a pivotal role in supporting our data-driven decision-making. This role is perfect for someone who loves working with SQL, Python, and modern visualization tools, and who wants to grow their skills in streaming data, automation, and cloud-based analytics. You'll work in a fastmoving, data-driven environment where you’ll help build scalable insights solutions, optimize reporting, and contribute to our mission of making data-driven decisions easier and more accessible for everyone.

Responsibilities

Develop and optimize queries to extract and analyze data from large-scale databases.

Work with real-time and batch data processing to support operational reporting and alerting.

Build dynamic dashboards and self-service analytics solutions using modern visualization tools (Tableau, Streamlit, Plotly, etc.).

Automate data collection and reporting workflows, reducing manual effort for the business. Assist in designing data pipelines for customer care analytics, ensuring data is structured for ease of use and scalability. Collaborate with cross-functional teams (engineering, operations, product, and customer experience) to identify and solve business problems using data. Implement data validation, monitoring, and quality checks to ensure clean, reliable data for stakeholders.

Stay up to date on emerging trends in BI, data engineering, and cloud computing to improve our analytics stack.

Interest in empowering teams with self-service dashboards and analytics tools.

Ability to automate manual reporting tasks to drive efficiency. Strong communication and collaboration skills—able to work effectively with business and technical teams.

Support senior analysts in conducting data analysis projects.

Learn and apply data analysis techniques and best practices.

Participate in team meetings and contribute to knowledge sharing.

Qualifications

Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, or a related field.

Understanding of SQL and relational databases.

Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.

Excellent communication and collaboration skills.

Eagerness to learn and develop data analysis skills

Nice To Have

Experience with cloud computing platforms (e.g., AWS, Azure, GCP).

Experience with Python

Familiarity with data visualization tools (e.g., Tableau, Power BI, Streamlit, Plotly).

Knowledge of retail and e-commerce. Experience with A/B testing, statistical analysis, or predictive modeling is a bonus.


The specific salary offered to a candidate may be influenced by a variety of factors including but not limited to the candidate’s relevant experience, education, and work location. In addition, this position is eligible for 401k and a new hire and annual equity grant. C08+ positions may also be eligible for annual bonus.


We offer different types of insurance and benefits, such as medical/Rx, vision, dental, life, disability, hospital indemnity, critical illness, and accident. We offer parental leave, family services benefits, backup dependent care, flexible spending accounts, telemedicine, pet adoption reimbursement, employee assistance program, and many discounts including 10% off pet insurance and 20% off at Chewy.com.

Exempt salary team members have unlimited PTO, subject to manager approval. Team members will receive six paid holidays per year. Team members may be eligible for paid sick and family leave in compliance with applicable state and local regulations.

Chewy is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, ancestry, national origin, gender, citizenship, marital status, religion, age, disability, gender identity, results of genetic testing, veteran status, as well as any other legally-protected characteristic. If you have a disability under the Americans with Disabilities Act or similar law, and you need an accommodation during the application process or to perform these job requirements, or if you need a religious accommodation, please contact CAAR@chewy.com.

To access Chewy's California CPRA Job Applicant Privacy Policy, please click here.

Title: Data Engineer(BI Reporting)
Description:
Job Description (Posting)

Job Title

Data Engineer(BI Reporting)

Job Summary

The Project Manager (SAFe Product Owner/Product Manager) is responsible for overseeing projects from initiation through delivery, ensuring that the projects are completed on time, within scope, and within budget. As a SAFe Product Owner/Product Manager, the individual will be responsible for managing and prioritizing the product backlog, representing the customer, and working closely with the development team to ensure successful product delivery.

Key Responsibilities


Lead and manage cross-functional project teams to deliver high-quality projects within the defined constraints.
Develop and maintain project plans, budget tracking, and resource allocation.
Communicate with stakeholders to ensure alignment on project scope, timelines, and deliverables.
Facilitate team meetings, including sprint planning, reviews, retrospectives, and daily standups.
Collaborate with Product Owners and Scrum Masters to prioritize features and resolve dependencies.
Act as a liaison between the business stakeholders and the development team to ensure clear communication and understanding.
Identify and mitigate project risks, proactively resolving issues to keep projects on track.
Monitor project progress and performance metrics, providing regular updates to leadership.


Skill Requirements


Strong understanding of Agile methodology and the SAFe framework.
Excellent communication and interpersonal skills to effectively collaborate with cross-functional teams and stakeholders.
Ability to prioritize and manage conflicting priorities in a fast-paced environment.
Proficiency in project management tools such as Jira, Microsoft Project, or similar tools.
Knowledge of product management principles and practices.
Strong leadership skills to motivate and guide teams towards successful project outcomes.


Certifications: SAFe Product Owner/Product Manager (POPM) certification is preferred.

Experience

7 12 Years

Skill (Primary)

Scaling Agile (APPS) Product Management SAFe Agile

Other Requirements


3 days onsite mandatory.
Need someone with strong exposure working for service-based organizations, managing projects with onsite and offshore coordination, and extensive client-facing experience.
Excellent communication skills are mandatory.
Strong Capital Markets experience is mandatory.


Job Description

We are seeking an experienced professional with strong expertise in Snowflake, Python, Oracle PL/SQL, and reporting/BI tools to deliver scalable data solutions and lead project execution, with proven delivery experience across Capital Markets corporate functions, securities, and front-to-back-office.

Key Responsibilities


Design, develop, and optimize data solutions using Snowflake, Python, and Oracle PL/SQL.
Develop and maintain reports and dashboards using standard BI/reporting tools to support business decision-making.
Lead end-to-end project deliverables, ensuring quality, timeliness, and alignment with customer requirements.
Drive and manage weekly and monthly governance calls, including status reporting, risk management, and stakeholder communication.
Ensure all project tasks and milestones are completed on time with high quality standards.
Provide technical leadership and mentorship to the team, reviewing designs and code to ensure best practices.
Engage directly with customers to understand business needs, translate them into technical solutions, and manage ongoing interactions.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to ensure smooth delivery and adoption of solutions.
Apply Generative AI concepts and tools where relevant to enhance data engineering, analytics, automation, and productivity use cases.
Continuously identify opportunities for process improvement, automation, and innovation.


Required Skills and Experience

Technical Skills


Snowflake
Oracle PL/SQL
DBT
Python
Any BI reporting tool
Strong hands-on experience with Snowflake, including data modeling, performance optimization, and cost management.
Advanced proficiency in Python for data processing, automation, and integration.
Solid experience in Oracle PL/SQL, including complex queries, procedures, and performance tuning.
Experience with at least one enterprise reporting or BI tool.
Proven leadership skills with experience leading teams and owning project delivery.
Strong customer interaction and stakeholder management skills.
Experience driving governance forums and executive-level status reviews.
Exposure to Generative AI, including use cases, tools, or solution implementation, is highly desirable.


This role requires a strong balance of technical depth, leadership capability, and customer-facing skills to successfully deliver high-impact data and analytics solutions.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Why USAA?

At USAA, our mission is to empower our members to achieve financial security through highly competitive products, exceptional service and trusted advice. We seek to be the #1 choice for the military community and their families.




Embrace a fulfilling career at USAA, where our core values - honesty, integrity, loyalty and service - define how we treat each other and our members. Be part of what truly makes us special and impactful.




We are proud to support active-duty military spouses. USAA roles may offer remote or hybrid flexibility for active-duty military spouses consistent with applicable policy and business needs.




The Opportunity

This role is remote eligible in the continental U.S. with occasional business travel. However, individuals residing within 60 mile radius of a USAA office will be expected to work on-site 4 days per week.




We have multiple exciting opportunities available on our Modeling Factory and Insights & Development teams. As a dedicated Actuarial Analyst I, you will be a near-credentialed analyst responsible for independently applying complex technical and actuarial methodologies as well as requiring expert knowledge in one actuarial practice area (pricing, reserving, modeling, catastrophe risk, claims analytics, product management, etc.) to complete unstructured projects.




What you'll do:

Independently applies complex technical and actuarial methodologies, as well as proficient actuarial knowledge, to complete unstructured projects (e.g., selects and ensures quality of data used to perform trend analysis supports rate level indications; translates business problem into technical requirements; runs catastrophe models and interprets and communicates results to support business activities while executing and enhancing a strong control environment with guidance from an experienced catastrophe modeler.
Utilizes actuarial, mathematical, or statistical techniques to augment actuarial work product.
Creates and delivers training to teammates on mechanics of actuarial tools and processes.
Applies understanding of products supported, stakeholders' needs, and industry trends to solve unstructured business problems.
Identifies compliance gaps and process improvements and recommends develop solutions.
Leads unit initiatives and participates in cross-functional efforts as a technical subject matter expert.
Effectively communicates insights and solutions to all audiences, including executive leadership.
Synthesizes complex and conflicting information into a clear and influential value proposition.
Ensures risks associated with business activities are effectively identified, measured, monitored, and controlled in accordance with risk and compliance policies and procedures.




What you have:

Bachelor's degree; OR 4 years of related Actuarial/business/analytical experience (in addition to the minimum years of experience required) may be substituted in lieu of degree.
4 years of Actuarial or analytical business experience.
No more than 2 Casualty Actuarial Society (CAS) exams away from Associate of the Casualty Actuarial Society (ACAS) Credential and All Validation of Education Experience (VEEs) completed.
Demonstrated competency with relevant actuarial, mathematical, and statistical techniques and approaches used to support fact-based decision-making.
Intermediate knowledge of data analysis tools, data visualization, developing analysis queries and procedures in Python, R, SQL, SAS, BI tools or other analysis software, and relevant industry data & methods and ability to connect external insights to business problems.
Experience communicating complex solutions and analysis to both technical and nontechnical audiences.




What sets you apart:

A passion for creative and innovative work that improves existing processes and creates new, repeatable and automated workflows that enhance team productivity.
Basic understanding of GLM or GBM modeling algorithms.
Intermediate knowledge of Model Risk Management, Model Governance, and Regulatory requirements.
US military experience through military service or a military spouse/domestic partner.




Compensation range: The salary range for this position is: $103,450 - $186,210.

Title: Business Intelligence Data Warehouse Consultant
Description:
Core Responsibilities:

Business Problem Translation & Value Creation

Partner directly with business leaders to understand strategic priorities, operational challenges, and growth opportunities
Translate ambiguous business problems into practical data and analytics solutions
Identify and prioritize high-value use cases that drive measurable business outcomes
Ensure all analytics initiatives are aligned with business objectives and KPIs
Quantify expected and realized business value from analytics investments
Serve as a trusted advisor on how data can improve business performance

Analytics Solution Delivery

Lead the development and delivery of analytics solutions that improve business decision-making
Design and develop dashboards, KPI frameworks, forecasting solutions, analytical models, and decision-support tools
Rapidly deliver MVP solutions to validate business value
Iterate solutions based on stakeholder feedback and evolving business needs
Balance speed, practicality, and business impact over technical perfection

Data-Driven Decision Enablement

Develop actionable insights that drive decisions and business outcomes
Identify trends, risks, opportunities, and performance drivers within enterprise data
Build analytical solutions that support planning, forecasting, operational optimization, and strategic decision-making
Ensure analytical outputs are understandable, actionable, and aligned to business objectives

Adoption, Change Management & Value Realization

Drive adoption of analytics solutions across business functions
Ensure analytics become embedded within business processes and workflows
Develop stakeholder engagement, training, and enablement plans
Monitor utilization, adoption, and business impact of delivered solutions
Identify and remove barriers preventing business adoption
Champion a culture of data-driven decision-making

Data Storytelling & Executive Communication

Translate analytical findings into compelling business narratives
Present insights and recommendations to leadership teams and executive stakeholders
Communicate business impact in language that drives action
Develop executive-ready dashboards, scorecards, and performance reviews

Cross-Functional Leadership

Collaborate closely with:
Business leaders
Data Product Architects
Data Engineers
Analytics teams
IT and enterprise stakeholders
Align analytics initiatives with business priorities
Help establish a culture of accountability, speed, pragmatism, and measurable outcomes
Mentor team members on business-facing analytics and stakeholder engagement

Qualifications:

5–10+ years of experience in analytics, business intelligence, consulting, analytics engineering, or data-driven business roles
Proven experience translating business problems into analytics solutions that delivered measurable value
Strong business acumen and ability to connect analytics to operational and financial outcomes
Advanced SQL skills
Strong Python and/or PySpark experience
Experience with Power BI and data visualization
Experience working with Azure and Databricks analytics environments
Demonstrated experience delivering MVPs and iterative solutions
Strong stakeholder management and change management capabilities
Exceptional communication, facilitation, and presentation skills
Experience in manufacturing, supply chain, ERP, finance, distribution, or operational environments
Experience leveraging Azure Data Factory, Databricks, and Azure Data Lake

Title: Senior Healthcare Data Analyst
Description:
1. About Our Client:

The organization operates within the behavioral health industry, focusing on enhancing the overall well-being of individuals through integrated treatment of mind and body. It serves over 15 million people across the United States, addressing the critical challenge of improving behavioral, physical, and social health through its services.




2. About the Opportunity:

The Senior Healthcare Data Analyst plays a key role in supporting clinical and operational performance by providing data-driven insights. This position is responsible for translating evaluation questions into analytic strategies, generating reports, and conducting statistical analyses that guide decision-making and improve healthcare outcomes.




3. Responsibilities:

• Lead reporting and analytics initiatives to support product innovation and population health strategies.

• Collaborate with clinical and strategy teams to identify target populations and develop performance measures.

• Perform claims and utilization analyses to identify cost drivers and savings opportunities.

• Model cost-saving scenarios and recommend improvements to financial outcomes.

• Evaluate financial impacts of contracts and strategic initiatives.

• Deliver timely analyses to support management and operational goals.




4. Requirements:

• Bachelor’s Degree.

• Minimum of 5 years’ experience in a similar role within healthcare payer or provider settings.

• Knowledge of statistical methods such as regression, propensity matching, and analysis of variance.

• Proficiency in SQL, SAS, Python, R, Tableau, PowerBI, or similar tools for data analysis and visualization.

• Experience with enterprise data warehouses and commercial/Medicare/Medicaid claims data.

• Ability to interpret technical materials and financial data.

• Effective presentation skills for both internal and external stakeholders.

• Strong problem-solving, analytic, and communication skills.

• Ability to work independently and collaboratively.

• Must pass comprehensive background checks and drug screening.




5. Pay Range and Compensation Package:

• Annual compensation between $103,000 and $115,000, plus an annual performance-based, discretionary incentive.

• Compensation depends on factors such as skills, education, certifications, experience, and market data.




6. Benefits & Perks:

• Medical, dental, and vision insurance coverage.

• 401(k) plan with competitive employer matching.

• Company-paid life and disability insurance.

• Paid parental leave and wellbeing incentives.

• Generous paid time off including volunteer time.

• Flexible spending accounts for healthcare and dependent care.

• Professional development and tuition reimbursement.

• Remote work flexibility depending on the role.




Equal Opportunity Statement: Our client is an equal opportunity employer. They celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, or national origin.




Note:

RemoteHunter is not the Employer of Record (EOR) for this role. Our purpose in this opportunity is to connect exceptional candidates with leading employers. We help job seekers worldwide discover roles that match their goals and guide them to complete their full application directly through the hiring company’s career page or ATS.

Title: Analista de Bases de Datos
Description:
Descripción de empleo

Descripción del puesto:

El perfil será parte del equipo encargado de supervisar y gestionar la calidad de los datos para la elaboración de informes y tableros en Power Bi, para la optimización de procesos y recursos, así como la generación de analisis para potenciar la toma de decisiones estrategicas en el Registro Único de Vivienda. Este es un puesto de tiempo completo que se desarrollará de forma presencial en nuestras instalaciones.




Funciones :

Análisis de Datos y Soporte a la Toma de Decisiones.
Optimización de Procesos y Recursos.
Gestión y Supervisión de la Calidad de Datos.
Identificar, analizar y resolver problemas relacionados con la calidad de los datos







Requisitos:

Conocimientos en gobernanza de datos y gestión de calidad de los datos para cumplir con estándares y garantizar la precisión de la información.
Excelente manejo de lenguajes de consulta y gestión de datos en SQL.
Conocimientos en SQL Server, PostgreSQL y MySQL.
Experiencia en gestión de datos maestros y administración eficiente del ciclo de vida de los datos en sistemas de información.
Conocimientos en la creación de tableros, DAX y Power Query en Power Bi.
Conocimiento en Data Quality y procesos ETL/ELT.







Se ofrece:

Sueldo: $29k netos.

Tipo de contratación: Permanente.

Prestaciones de ley, seguro de gastos medicos mayores, vales de despensa, fondo de ahorro, entre otras cosas.




Se Solicita:

1 año de experiencia.
Modalidad: Presencial
Nivel de estudios: Licenciatura/ingeniería Titulado

Title: Data Entry Clerk II
Description:
About The Company

Quest Diagnostics is a leading provider of diagnostic information services, dedicated to improving health and well-being through innovative testing and insights. With a rich history of delivering accurate and timely results, the company serves a diverse client base including healthcare providers, hospitals, and patients across the globe. Committed to advancing healthcare through cutting-edge technology and a focus on customer service excellence, Quest Diagnostics stands as a trusted partner in health management and disease prevention. The organization fosters a collaborative and inclusive work environment, emphasizing professional growth, integrity, and community engagement.

About The Role

The Data Entry Clerk - Billing II role is a vital position within our billing department, responsible for ensuring the accuracy and completeness of billing data related to patient accounts and insurance claims. Working Monday to Friday between 6:30 AM and 3:30 PM Eastern, this full-time position offers an excellent opportunity for individuals with a keen eye for detail and strong customer service skills to contribute to the efficiency of our billing processes. The role involves research, data entry, and communication with internal and external stakeholders to resolve billing discrepancies and facilitate smooth account management. Successful candidates will demonstrate proficiency in billing systems, a solid understanding of insurance payor requirements, and the ability to work independently while collaborating effectively with team members.

Qualifications


High School Diploma, GED, or equivalent work experience
Minimum of 1+ year experience in customer service, phone support, or medical billing office
At least 1+ year of billing knowledge, data entry, and insurance payor familiarity
Proficiency in computer applications, including Windows PC and keyboard navigation
Successful completion of new hire training and demonstrated proficiency
Preferred: Some college education, medical terminology experience, or medical coding certification
Understanding of multiple billing requirements across various payers and states
Experience in a call center environment is a plus


Responsibilities


Research and obtain missing information or demographics to complete accessions accurately
Perform data entry for account accessions ensuring accuracy and completeness
Demonstrate competency in eligibility, billing, receivables systems, and related applications
Educate external customers on billing procedures to prevent future issues
Inform customers of billing problems, issues, and resolutions as appropriate
Make outbound calls to clients, patients, carriers, and internal sources to gather missing or additional information
Answer inbound calls, providing account information and resolving billing inquiries
Respond to account inquiries via written correspondence when necessary
Handle sensitive interactions with clients and patients professionally and confidentially
Maintain accurate documentation of all transactions and communications
Generate and distribute reports to internal teams or external clients as needed
Identify and report trends related to errors, denials, or PHI breaches to management
Work independently and as part of a team to meet performance goals related to accuracy, efficiency, and customer satisfaction
Participate in cross-functional tasks and other duties as assigned


Benefits


Day 1 Medical, dental, vision, and supplemental health coverage for full-time employees working 30+ hours
Well-being programs, including annual health assessments and mental health support
Paid vacation, health/flex time, and holidays, including a "MyDay" off
Financial wellness services such as FinFit coaching and 401(k) with company match up to 5%
Employee stock purchase plan and life/disability insurance options
Flexible Spending Accounts and matching gifts program
Educational assistance through MyQuest for Education
Opportunities for career growth and professional development
And many additional employee-centric benefits designed to promote a healthy work-life balance


Equal Opportunity

Quest Diagnostics is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. We do not discriminate based on race, color, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, religion, national origin, disability, veteran status, or any other legally protected characteristic. We encourage qualified individuals from all backgrounds to apply and join our team.

Title: Data Center Technician
Description:
About the Role




Sycomp’s Professional Services Team is seeking a skilled Data Center Technician to support infrastructure deployments, hardware integration, and hands-on technical operations within the New York City area. The ideal candidate brings strong technical aptitude, excellent communication skills, and a customer-centric mindset. This role requires the ability to install, configure, troubleshoot, and maintain enterprise-class systems while collaborating with both internal teams and external customers.




About Sycomp




Sycomp is a global IT services and logistics provider with extensive expertise in cloud, data center, endpoint management and security solutions. Sycomp’s diverse team of consultants and engineers deliver on the company’s mission to tackle challenging global IT projects through its state-of-the-art integration and warehouse centers and global technology partnerships. Headquartered in the heart of Silicon Valley, California, Sycomp has successfully shipped, deployed and managed complex IT projects and supporting assets in more than 150 countries helping its Fortune 500 customers and global partners realize a world without boundaries.




Job Duties and Responsibilities:




Install, terminate, and test Cat5e, Cat6, and fiber optic cabling per specifications.
Perform rack and stack servers, switches, and cabling per rack elevation diagrams and port maps.
Install and mount wireless access points (WAPs) and associated cabling.
Perform RJ45 terminations and patch panel installations.
Utilize Fluke or equivalent test equipment to verify cable performance and document results.
Install and label new network devices in existing racks following structured cabling standards.
Troubleshoot and resolve connectivity or hardware issues.
Maintain clean documentation and communicate progress with project leads and clients.
Complete technical certifications as needed.







Experience Requirements




2–5 years of technical experience in IT, data center operations, hardware support, or a related field.
Hands-on experience with servers, switches, and related infrastructure equipment.
Strong troubleshooting and problem-determination skills.




Education & Certifications




University degree in a related field or relevant professional training.
CCNA or CCNP certifications are a plus.




Technology Means the World to Us




Technology connects people, enables business, fosters innovation, and helps organizations operate without boundaries. Every day, Sycomp’s diverse team delivers on the company’s mission to help customers and partners navigate the complexity of global IT—from cloud and data center computing to cyber security, networking, and integration and logistics across more than 150 countries.




If you thrive in a growth-minded and values-driven culture, Sycomp may be your next career adventure. At Sycomp, you will have the opportunity to work in a global setting, collaborate with cross-functional teams, and be part of a dynamic and growing organization.




Sycomp is known for its strong culture and commitment to employee growth. With a 93% company-wide retention rate, we foster an environment where people can make a meaningful impact, develop professionally, and build rewarding careers over the long term.




Learn more about Sycomp and our employee benefits at: https://sycomp.com/careers/




Sycomp is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, religion, color, age, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, gender identity, disability status, veteran status, or any other protected characteristic.




Sycomp will provide reasonable accommodation for qualified individuals with disabilities as needed. If you need assistance or an accommodation in applying, please contact our Human Resources Department at careers@sycomp.com




Sycomp may use automated tools, including artificial intelligence, to support certain aspects of the recruitment process, such as reviewing applications or identifying relevant experience. These tools assist our teams, and all hiring decisions are made following human review.

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
Roles & Responsibilities

Design, develop, and maintain scalable ETL/data integration pipelines using AWS Glue, PySpark, and Python.
Develop and optimize data transformation workflows to process large datasets from multiple source systems.
Utilize Attunity for real-time and batch data replication between on-premises and cloud environments.
Build and manage data lakes and storage solutions using Amazon S3, ensuring data quality, security, and governance.
Implement serverless data processing solutions using AWS Lambda and integrate with other AWS services.
Configure and manage IAM roles, policies, and permissions to ensure secure access to AWS resources.
Automate infrastructure provisioning and deployment using AWS CloudFormation.
Monitor, troubleshoot, and optimize ETL jobs, data pipelines, and cloud resources for performance and reliability.
Collaborate with business, analytics, and application teams to gather requirements and deliver data solutions.
Support CI/CD processes, perform code reviews, and maintain technical documentation following best practices.

Generic Managerial Skills, If any

Planning & Execution – Effectively prioritize tasks, estimate effort, and ensure timely delivery of project milestones while balancing multiple commitments.
Stakeholder Management – Build strong relationships with business teams, clients, and leadership through proactive communication and expectation management.
Communication – Convey technical and non-technical information clearly through meetings, presentations, documentation, and status updates.
Collaboration – Foster teamwork across cross-functional groups, encourage knowledge sharing, and contribute to a positive team environment.
Decision Making – Analyze options, assess risks, and make informed decisions that align with both technical and business objectives.
Leadership & Ownership – Take accountability for deliverables, drive initiatives proactively, and lead by example to achieve team goals.
Risk & Issue Management – Identify potential risks early, address blockers proactively, and implement mitigation strategies to ensure smooth project execution.
Customer & Business Focus – Understand customer needs and business priorities, ensuring technical solutions deliver measurable value.
Mentoring & Coaching – Support team growth through guidance, code reviews, knowledge sharing, and skill development initiatives.
Project & Change Management – Adapt effectively to evolving requirements, manage priorities during change, and ensure successful delivery outcomes.

  • Leadership & Ownership – Take accountability for deliverables, drive initiatives proactively, and lead by example to achieve team goals.
  • Risk & Issue Management – Identify potential risks early, address blockers proactively, and implement mitigation strategies to ensure smooth project execution.
  • Customer & Business Focus – Understand customer needs and business priorities, ensuring technical solutions deliver measurable value.
  • Mentoring & Coaching – Support team growth through guidance, code reviews, knowledge sharing, and skill development initiatives.
  • Project & Change Management – Adapt effectively to evolving requirements, manage priorities during change, and ensure successful delivery outcomes.

Title: Customer Insights Analyst
Description:
CUSTOMER INSIGHTS & ANALYTICS ANALYST




GENERAL PURPOSE OF THE POSITION:

Provie internal and external data to help uncover single and multi-price point white space and assortment opportunities across the Dollar Tree categories to drive incremental traffic and sales. As the organization looks to increase and improve customer relative assortment, it is necessary to deliver support to the merchants to understand performance of current market items and potential incremental opportunities. Dedicated individuals to the mining of this data are imperative to the successful integration of the right new items for our shopper.




ESSENTIAL ACTIVIES & RESPONSIBILITIES:

Support the development and execution of merchandising strategies and decision making by delivering timely, accurate, and actionable insights derived from syndicated data and customer research.
Support strategic initiatives with rigorous analytics, utilizing third party data sources including Nielsen, Numerator, 1010, Circana, and customer research.
Assist in the development and maintenance of daily and weekly standardized reporting.
Organize and evaluate large data sets to assist with the development of “white space” and assortment recommendations to drive traffic and sales.
Assist with senior leader presentations by preparing data driven content.
Assist in customer research initiatives to inform marketing strategies and optimize campaign effectiveness.
Help lead training efforts with merchandising and marketing resources to promote best practices in data interpretation and usage.
Identify, prioritize, and initiate data collection process improvements to improve team efficiency.




EXPERIENCE AND EDUCATION REQUIRED:

Bachelors Degree (BA / BS/ BFA) or equivalent, and 1-2 years experience.
Excellent analytical and problem solving skills. Able to generate and evaluate hypotheses and create narratives for senior leaders.
Detail-oriented. Comfortable working with numerical data and information.
Strong written and verbal communication skills.
Demonstrated initiative, independent thinker and worker.
Team player in a collaborative environment.
Deadline oriented. Ability to anticipate and address conflicts, to skillfully negotiate, and deliver great outcomes.
Advanced skills in Microsoft Office products, especially Excel and PowerPoint.
Must be willing to relocate to Chesapeake, VA




Start Rate = $75,000-$84,500K based on experience plus 10% bonus.

The company offers Health, Dental, & Vision, a flexible spending account, life and disability insurance benefits, a 401k plan, PTO, paid holidays annually, and an employee stock purchase plan.
